  tier varchar [note: 'Hierarchical tier classification of the segment indicating strategic priority and resource allocation level. Tier 1 represents highest-value segments with dedicated partner attention; Tier 4 represents standard service segments.']

  Note: 'Reference master defining the firms client segmentation taxonomy used for relationship management, pricing strategy, rate card selection, and business development targeting. Each segment record captures segment code, segment name (e.g., Global 500, Mid-Market Corporate, Government & Public Sector, High Net Worth Individual, Start-up & Emerging Growth), segment description, applicable practice areas, minimum revenue threshold, preferred AFA types, assigned BD team, segment review cycle, and segment-level discount authority. Segments drive rate card selection in Elite 3E, pipeline segmentation in Microsoft Dynamics 365 CRM, and partner compensation allocation. Maintained by the firms pricing and BD leadership; changes require partnership approval. Typically 8-15 active segments at any time.'
}

Table client.relationship_team {
  relationship_team_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the relationship team assignment record.']
  practice_group_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key to workforce.practice_group.practice_group_id']
  primary_relationship_timekeeper_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the timekeeper (attorney or professional) assigned to this relationship team role.']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the client entity for whom this relationship team is assigned.']
  approval_date date [note: 'The date on which this relationship team assignment was formally approved by the authorizing partner.']
  assignment_end_date date [note: 'The date on which this timekeepers assignment to this relationship team role ended. Null indicates an active assignment.']
  assignment_notes varchar [note: 'Additional free-text notes or comments regarding this relationship team assignment. May include special instructions, client preferences, or coordination requirements.']
  assignment_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text explanation of why this timekeeper was assigned to this relationship team role. May reference practice area expertise, industry knowledge, geographic coverage, client preference, or succession planning.']
  assignment_start_date date [note: 'The date on which this timekeeper was assigned to this relationship team role for the client.']
  assignment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of this relationship team assignment. Active assignments are operational; Inactive assignments are historical; Suspended assignments are temporarily paused; Pending Approval assignments await partner approval.']
  billing_authority_level varchar [note: 'Defines the level of billing authority this timekeeper has for matters related to this client. Full Authority can approve invoices independently; Approval Required must escalate; View Only can review but not approve; No Access cannot view billing information.']
  client_contact_frequency varchar [note: 'The expected frequency of proactive client contact for this timekeeper in this role. Used in relationship management planning and client engagement tracking.']
  client_profile_fk varchar [note: 'FK to client.client_profile.client_profile_id — Relationship teams are assigned TO a client. Without this FK, you cannot determine which partners and BD managers are responsible for which clients — breaking RPE reporting and escalation routing.']
  client_satisfaction_responsibility boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this timekeeper is responsible for monitoring and reporting on client satisfaction metrics for this client. Used in client retention and service quality tracking.']
  conflict_check_authority boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this timekeeper has authority to approve conflict check clearances for new matters involving this client. Typically granted to Client Relationship Partners and Supervising Partners.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this relationship team assignment record was first created in the system.']
  cross_sell_target_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this timekeeper has active cross-sell or upsell targets for this client relationship. Used in Business Development (BD) pipeline management and opportunity tracking in Microsoft Dynamics 365.']
  effective_date date [note: 'The date from which this relationship team assignment becomes operationally effective. May differ from assignment_start_date for planned future assignments.']
  escalation_priority varchar [note: 'Numeric priority order for escalating client issues to this timekeeper. Lower numbers indicate higher priority. Used in automated escalation routing for client service delivery.']
  geographic_coverage_region varchar [note: 'The geographic region or jurisdiction this timekeeper is responsible for covering within the client relationship. Supports multi-jurisdictional client service coordination.']
  is_primary_contact boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this timekeeper is the primary point of contact for the client within their assigned role. Used for escalation routing and client communication prioritization.']
  last_client_contact_date date [note: 'The most recent date this timekeeper had documented contact with the client in this relationship role. Used for relationship health monitoring and engagement gap analysis.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this relationship team assignment record was most recently updated.']
  next_scheduled_contact_date date [note: 'The next planned date for this timekeeper to contact the client in this relationship role. Supports proactive relationship management and follow-up tracking.']
  notification_preference varchar [note: 'Defines the level of client-related notifications this timekeeper wishes to receive for this assignment. Supports communication workflow automation in Microsoft Dynamics 365.']
  origination_credit_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'The percentage of origination credit allocated to this timekeeper for this client relationship. Used in Revenue Per Equity Partner (RPE) and Profit Per Partner (PPP) calculations. Sum across all team members for a client should equal 100%.']
  practice_area_coverage varchar [note: 'The specific practice area or legal specialty this timekeeper is responsible for within the client relationship (e.g., M&A, IP, Litigation, Regulatory Compliance). Supports cross-practice coordination.']
  relationship_role_type varchar [note: 'The specific role this timekeeper plays in the client relationship team. Client Relationship Partner (CRP) is the primary relationship owner; Originating Partner brought the client to the firm; Supervising Partner oversees matter execution; Relationship Manager handles day-to-day coordination; Business Development Manager drives pipeline and cross-sell; Supporting Fee Earner provides specialized expertise.']
  responsibility_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'The percentage of relationship responsibility allocated to this timekeeper for ongoing client management and service delivery. Used in partner performance evaluation and compensation allocation.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'The operational system from which this relationship team assignment record originated. Elite 3E for practice management assignments; Microsoft Dynamics 365 for CRM-driven assignments; Intapp Conflicts for conflict-cleared assignments; Manual Entry for ad-hoc assignments.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'The unique identifier of this relationship team assignment record in the source operational system. Used for data lineage and reconciliation.']
  succession_plan_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this assignment is part of a formal succession plan to transition client relationship ownership. Used in partner retirement planning and relationship continuity management.']
  to_client_profile varchar [note: 'FK to client.client_profile.client_profile_id — Relationship teams are assigned per client — critical for CRP accountability and escalation routing']

  Note: 'Defines the firm-side relationship team assigned to a client, capturing the client relationship partner (CRP), originating partner, supervising partner, relationship manager, business development manager, and supporting fee earners. Stores role type, assignment start date, assignment end date, primary contact flag, and notification preferences. Supports RPE/PPP reporting, client satisfaction tracking, and BD pipeline management in Microsoft Dynamics 365. Enables accountability and escalation routing for client service delivery.'
}

Table client.client_engagement_scope {
  client_engagement_scope_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the client-level engagement scope record. Primary key.']
  attorney_profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the partner (timekeeper) designated as the relationship partner or client relationship manager for this engagement scope. Responsible for overall client relationship and scope governance.']
  engagement_letter_doc_template_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the standard engagement letter template or Letter of Engagement (LOE) template used for matters opened under this scope. Links to the firms document management system.']
  intake_engagement_scope_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key to intake.intake_engagement_scope.intake_engagement_scope_id']
  outside_counsel_guideline_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to client.outside_counsel_guideline. Business justification: Engagement scope references outside counsel guidelines (OCG) that govern service delivery requirements. Currently modeled as outside_counsel_guidelines_reference (STRING), but this should be a proper ']
  policy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.policy. Business justification: Panel appointments and engagement scopes must comply with firm compliance policies (conflicts, AML, data protection). Links engagement terms to governing policies, ensuring client acceptance criteria ']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the client entity for whom this engagement scope is defined. Links to the client master record.']
  afa_framework_type varchar [note: 'Type of Alternative Fee Arrangement (AFA) framework agreed for this engagement scope. Fixed_fee indicates a flat fee per matter; capped_fee indicates a maximum fee limit; contingency indicates success-based fees; blended_rate indicates a single rate for all timekeepers; hourly indicates traditional time-based billing. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: fixed_fee|capped_fee|contingency|blended_rate|success_fee|retainer|hourly — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  aml_risk_rating varchar [note: 'Anti-Money Laundering (AML) risk rating assigned to this engagement scope based on client profile, jurisdictions, and service types. Used for compliance monitoring and enhanced due diligence requirements.']
  authorised_practice_areas varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list or structured text describing the practice areas the firm is authorized to provide services in under this scope (e.g., Corporate M&A, Employment Law, Intellectual Property). Defines the substantive legal domains covered.']
  auto_renewal_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this engagement scope automatically renews upon expiry unless either party provides termination notice. True indicates auto-renewal; false indicates explicit renewal required.']
  billing_frequency varchar [note: 'Agreed frequency for invoicing the client under this engagement scope. Monthly indicates invoices are issued monthly; upon_completion indicates invoicing occurs when matters are closed; milestone_based indicates invoicing tied to project milestones.']
  conflict_check_required boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether a conflict check is required for each new matter opened under this engagement scope. True indicates conflict checks are mandatory; false indicates standing clearance has been granted.']
  created_by_user_code varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who created this engagement scope record. Used for audit trail and accountability.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this engagement scope record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage.']
  data_protection_classification varchar [note: 'Data protection classification level for client data handled under this engagement scope. Special_category indicates GDPR Article 9 special category data (e.g., health, biometric); highly_confidential indicates enhanced security measures required.']
  electronic_billing_required boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the client requires electronic billing submission (e.g., LEDES format) for invoices under this engagement scope. True indicates e-billing is mandatory; false indicates paper or PDF invoices are acceptable.']
  excluded_services varchar [note: 'Free-text description of services or practice areas explicitly excluded from this engagement scope. Clarifies boundaries and limitations of the firms authority (e.g., Litigation services excluded, No criminal defense work).']
  gdpr_processing_basis varchar [note: 'Legal basis under GDPR Article 6 for processing personal data under this engagement scope. Contract indicates processing is necessary for contract performance; legal_obligation indicates processing is required by law; legitimate_interest indicates processing is necessary for legitimate business interests.']
  geographic_jurisdictions_covered varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of geographic jurisdictions (countries, states, regions) where the firm is authorized to provide services under this scope. Uses ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country codes where applicable (e.g., USA, GBR, DEU).']
  kyc_documentation_status varchar [note: 'Status of Know Your Client (KYC) documentation and due diligence for this engagement scope. Complete indicates all required KYC documents are on file and current; expired indicates KYC refresh is needed.']
  ledes_format_version varchar [note: 'Version of the Legal Electronic Data Exchange Standard (LEDES) format required for electronic billing submissions under this engagement scope. LEDES98B is the most common format; LEDES2000 includes additional fields.']
  matter_budget_approval_required boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the client requires formal budget approval before commencing work on individual matters under this engagement scope. True indicates budgets must be submitted and approved; false indicates no formal budget process.']
  modified_by_user_code varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who last modified this engagement scope record. Used for audit trail and change accountability.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this engagement scope record was last modified. Updated whenever any field in the record changes. Used for audit trail and change tracking.']
  originating_office_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the firm office that originated this engagement scope and is responsible for client relationship management. Used for revenue allocation and reporting.']
  panel_appointment_reference varchar [note: 'External reference number or identifier for the panel appointment issued by the client, if this scope is a panel arrangement. Null for non-panel scopes.']
  panel_expiry_date date [note: 'Date on which the panel appointment or engagement scope expires. Null for open-ended arrangements. After this date, the firm must seek renewal or new authority.']
  panel_start_date date [note: 'Date on which the panel appointment or engagement scope becomes effective. Marks the beginning of the firms authority to provide services under this scope.']
  payment_terms_days varchar [note: 'Number of days from invoice date within which payment is due under this engagement scope. Standard terms are typically 30, 60, or 90 days.']
  preferred_counsel_designation boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the firm has been designated as preferred counsel for the client under this scope. True indicates preferred status; false indicates standard panel or non-preferred status.']
  rate_card_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier for the rate card or fee schedule applicable to this engagement scope. Links to the billing rate structure agreed with the client.']
  renewal_notice_days varchar [note: 'Number of days before panel_expiry_date that the firm must provide notice of intent to renew or terminate the engagement scope. Used for proactive relationship management and renewal planning.']
  retention_period_years varchar [note: 'Number of years that records and documents related to matters under this engagement scope must be retained after matter closure, per client requirements and regulatory obligations. Typical values range from 6 to 10 years.']
  scope_approval_date date [note: 'Date on which the engagement scope was formally approved by the client and the firms conflicts and risk committees. Represents the date of mutual agreement.']
  scope_description varchar [note: 'Detailed free-text description of the engagement scope, including any special terms, conditions, or operational notes. Provides context and clarification beyond structured fields.']
  scope_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name or title for this engagement scope, summarizing the nature of the standing authority (e.g., Corporate Legal Panel 2024, Employment Law Retainer).']
  scope_reference_number varchar [note: 'Business-facing unique reference number for this engagement scope, used in communications and documentation. Typically alphanumeric code assigned by the firm.']
  scope_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the engagement scope. Active indicates the firm is currently authorized to provide services under this scope; expired indicates the scope has lapsed.']
  scope_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the engagement scope arrangement. Panel indicates the firm is on a legal panel; retainer indicates ongoing retained services; framework indicates a master services agreement; ad_hoc indicates project-based authority; preferred_counsel indicates designated counsel status.']
  service_level_agreement_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier for the Service Level Agreement (SLA) document or terms applicable to this engagement scope. Defines performance commitments, response times, and quality standards.']
  utbms_task_codes_required boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the client requires Uniform Task-Based Management System (UTBMS) task and activity codes on time entries and invoices under this engagement scope. True indicates UTBMS coding is mandatory.']

  Note: 'Defines the agreed scope of legal services the firm is authorised to provide to a client, as established at the client level (above individual matters). Captures authorised practice areas, geographic jurisdictions covered, excluded services, panel appointment reference, panel start date, panel expiry date, preferred counsel designation, rate card reference, AFA framework type, and scope approval date. Distinct from matter-level engagement letters (owned by the contract domain); this is the client-level standing authority. Sourced from Elite 3E and contract/CLM systems.'
}

Table client.client_status_history {
  client_status_history_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for each client status change record. Primary key for the client status history audit trail.']
  attorney_profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the partner who approved the status change, particularly for transitions to suspended or former status. Required for governance and risk management.']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the client whose status is being tracked. Links this status change event to the client master record.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date when the partner approved the status change. Supports governance audit trail and regulatory compliance documentation.']
  approving_partner_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the approving partner. Denormalized for audit trail clarity and historical record preservation.']
  billing_impact_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this status change has implications for billing, collections, or work-in-progress for the client.']
  change_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code indicating the business or regulatory reason for the status change. Supports root cause analysis and regulatory audit requirements. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: kyc_failure|sanctions_hit|conflict_identified|client_request|engagement_completed|non_payment|regulatory_action — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  change_reason_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative explanation of why the client status was changed. Provides context beyond the standardized reason code for audit and review purposes.']
  changed_by_user_code varchar [note: 'Reference to the system user who initiated or recorded the status change. Supports accountability and audit trail requirements.']
  changed_by_user_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the user who initiated the status change. Denormalized for audit report readability and historical preservation.']
  conflict_check_status varchar [note: 'Result of conflict checking performed at the time of status change. Essential for ethical compliance and professional responsibility.']
  effective_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this status change record was superseded by a subsequent status change. Null for the current active record. Supports temporal querying.']
  effective_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this status change record became the current active record in the history. Supports temporal querying and point-in-time analysis.']
  gdpr_data_retention_impact varchar [note: 'Indicates how this status change affects GDPR data retention and deletion obligations for the client record.']
  is_current_status boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this record represents the current active status for the client. Simplifies queries for current state without temporal logic.']
  kyc_review_outcome varchar [note: 'Result of the KYC review process associated with this status change, if applicable. Critical for AML compliance and regulatory reporting.']
  matter_impact_assessment varchar [note: 'Assessment of how this status change affects active or pending matters for the client. Used for matter management and risk mitigation.']
  new_status varchar [note: 'The status of the client after this transition. Captures the to-state in the status lifecycle.']
  previous_status varchar [note: 'The status of the client immediately before this transition occurred. Captures the from-state in the status lifecycle.']
  reactivation_conditions varchar [note: 'Specific conditions or requirements that must be met before the client can be reactivated, if applicable.']
  reactivation_eligibility varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the client is eligible for reactivation to active status in the future, based on the reason for the current status change.']
  record_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this audit record was first inserted into the data warehouse. Supports data lineage and ETL troubleshooting.']
  record_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this audit record was last modified in the data warehouse. Supports change tracking and data quality monitoring.']
  regulatory_notification_date date [note: 'Date when regulatory authorities were notified of this status change, if notification was required. Supports compliance audit trail.']
  regulatory_notification_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this status change triggers a mandatory notification to regulatory authorities such as SRA or state bar associations.']
  sanctions_screening_result varchar [note: 'Outcome of sanctions list screening performed at the time of status change. Required for AML compliance and risk management.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the operational system where this status change was originally recorded. Supports data lineage and reconciliation.']
  source_system_record_reference varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of this status change record in the source operational system. Enables traceability and reconciliation.']
  status_change_date date [note: 'The business-effective date when the client status transition occurred. Used for regulatory reporting and client lifecycle analytics.']
  status_change_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time when the status change was recorded in the system. Provides audit trail granularity for compliance purposes.']
  status_history_client_profile_fk varchar [note: 'FK to client.client_profile.client_profile_id — Status history records track transitions FOR a specific client profile. Without this FK, audit trail records are orphaned and cannot support regulatory compliance queries.']
  status_history_to_client_profile varchar [note: 'FK to client.client_profile.client_profile_id — Status history tracks transitions of a specific client profile — audit trail requires this FK']
  supporting_notes varchar [note: 'Additional free-text notes providing context, supporting documentation references, or special circumstances related to the status change. Used for internal review and regulatory audit.']
  trust_account_impact_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this status change affects trust account holdings or IOLTA obligations for the client.']

  Note: 'Audit trail of all status transitions for a client profile over its lifecycle, capturing previous status, new status, change reason code, change date, changed by (user), approving partner, and any supporting notes. Tracks transitions such as prospect-to-active, active-to-suspended (e.g., due to KYC failure or sanctions hit), and active-to-former. Supports regulatory audit requirements (SRA, GDPR), conflict-check history, and client lifecycle analytics. Ensures full traceability of client standing changes.'
}

Table client.beneficial_owner {
  beneficial_owner_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the beneficial owner record. Primary key.']
  aml_kyc_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.aml_kyc_program. Business justification: Beneficial ownership identification is a core AML/KYC program requirement under MLR 2017. Each beneficial owner record must reference the program under which verification was conducted for regulatory ']
  kyc_record_id varchar [note: 'FK to client.kyc_record.kyc_record_id — Beneficial ownership verification is part of the KYC dossier — linked for audit trail']
  owned_organisation_id varchar [note: 'FK to client.organisation.organisation_id — Beneficial owners are UBOs OF a corporate entity. This FK is required for AML compliance — you must be able to traverse from organisation to all its declared beneficial owners.']
  primary_beneficial_kyc_record_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the KYC record under which this beneficial owner was identified and verified.']
  primary_beneficial_organisation_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the corporate client entity for which this beneficial ownership is declared. Links to the organisation master data.']
  to_organisation_id varchar [note: 'FK to client.organisation.organisation_id — UBOs are recorded against corporate entities for AML compliance']
  adverse_media_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether adverse media or negative news has been identified for this beneficial owner during screening. True if adverse media is present.']
  adverse_media_summary varchar [note: 'Summary of adverse media findings, including the nature of the negative news and the source. Populated when adverse_media_flag is true.']
  beneficial_owner_status varchar [note: 'The current status of the beneficial owner record: active (current UBO), inactive (no longer a UBO), under review (verification in progress), rejected (failed verification), or superseded (replaced by updated record).']
  control_description varchar [note: 'Free-text description providing additional detail on how the beneficial owner exercises control, especially when nature_of_control is other or when the control structure is complex.']
  country_of_residence varchar [note: 'The country where the beneficial owner primarily resides, represented as a 3-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code.']
  created_by_user_code varchar [note: 'Reference to the user who created this beneficial owner record. Used for audit trail and accountability.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this beneficial owner record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage.']
  date_of_birth date [note: 'The date of birth of the beneficial owner, used for identity verification and PEP screening.']
  disclosure_date date [note: 'The date on which the beneficial owner information was disclosed to the firm by the client organisation. Marks the start of the KYC record for this UBO.']
  full_name varchar [note: 'The complete legal name of the ultimate beneficial owner as it appears on official identification documents.']
  last_modified_by_user_code varchar [note: 'Reference to the user who last modified this beneficial owner record. Used for audit trail and accountability.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this beneficial owner record was last modified. Used for audit trail and change tracking.']
  last_review_date date [note: 'The date on which the beneficial owner record was last reviewed for accuracy and currency. Used to schedule periodic reviews per the firms AML policy.']
  nationality varchar [note: 'The nationality of the beneficial owner, represented as a 3-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code.']
  nature_of_control varchar [note: 'The mechanism by which the beneficial owner exercises control over the client organisation: direct shareholding, indirect shareholding through intermediaries, voting rights, power to appoint board members, significant influence, or other means.']
  next_review_date date [note: 'The scheduled date for the next periodic review of the beneficial owner information. Calculated based on risk rating and firm policy.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for internal notes and observations about the beneficial owner, such as unusual circumstances, additional verification steps taken, or risk mitigation measures.']
  ownership_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'The percentage of ownership or control that the beneficial owner holds in the client organisation. Typically 25% or more triggers UBO disclosure requirements under most AML regimes.']
  pep_role_description varchar [note: 'Description of the political or public role held by the PEP, or the relationship to the PEP if the beneficial owner is a family member or close associate.']
  pep_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the beneficial owner is a Politically Exposed Person (PEP), a family member of a PEP, or a close associate of a PEP. PEP status triggers enhanced due diligence requirements.']
  residential_address varchar [note: 'The full residential address of the beneficial owner, including street, city, postal code, and country. Used for verification and correspondence.']
  risk_assessment_date date [note: 'The date on which the risk rating was last assessed or updated. Used to trigger periodic risk re-assessment.']
  risk_rating varchar [note: 'The AML/KYC risk rating assigned to this beneficial owner based on PEP status, sanctions screening, adverse media, country risk, and other factors. Determines the level of ongoing monitoring required.']
  sanctions_list_match varchar [note: 'Details of any sanctions list match, including the list name, match score, and reason for the match. Populated when sanctions_status is match or potential_match.']
  sanctions_status varchar [note: 'Result of sanctions screening against OFAC, UN, EU, and other sanctions lists. Indicates whether the beneficial owner is sanctioned, potentially sanctioned, or clear.']
  source_of_funds varchar [note: 'Description of the origin of the funds used in the specific transaction or relationship with the firm. Distinct from source of wealth; focuses on the immediate source of capital.']
  source_of_wealth varchar [note: 'Description of the origin of the beneficial owners total wealth, such as inheritance, business ownership, employment, investments, or other sources. Required for enhanced due diligence on high-risk clients.']
  status_change_date date [note: 'The date on which the status of the beneficial owner record was last changed. Used for audit trail and lifecycle tracking.']
  status_change_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text explanation of the reason for the most recent status change, such as ownership transfer, verification failure, or periodic review outcome.']
  tax_identification_number varchar [note: 'The tax identification number of the beneficial owner in their country of residence or nationality. Used for tax reporting and compliance purposes.']
  tax_residence_country varchar [note: 'The country where the beneficial owner is considered a tax resident, represented as a 3-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code. May differ from country of residence.']
  verification_date date [note: 'The date on which the beneficial owner information was verified by the firm. Used to track the currency of KYC data and trigger periodic re-verification.']
  verification_document_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number or identifier of the document used for verification, such as passport number or national ID number. Stored for audit trail purposes.']
  verification_method varchar [note: 'The method or document type used to verify the identity and ownership of the beneficial owner: passport, national ID, drivers license, utility bill, bank statement, corporate registry extract, third-party database, or other. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: passport|national_id|drivers_license|utility_bill|bank_statement|corporate_registry|third_party_database|other — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']

  Note: 'Records the ultimate beneficial owners (UBOs) of corporate client entities as required by AML/KYC regulations (FATF, EU 4th/5th AML Directives, FinCEN). Captures UBO full name, date of birth, nationality, country of residence, ownership percentage, nature of control (direct shareholding, indirect, voting rights, other significant influence), PEP status, sanctions status, verification date, and verification method. Linked to the organisation master and KYC record. Supports SRA and FATF beneficial ownership disclosure obligations.'
}

Table client.outside_counsel_guideline {
  outside_counsel_guideline_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the outside counsel guideline record. Primary key.']
  organisation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to client.organisation. Business justification: Large corporate clients issue Outside Counsel Guidelines at the organisation level that apply enterprise-wide across all subsidiaries and matters. Billing compliance, rate validation, and invoice revi']
  practice_area_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to service.practice_area. Business justification: Outside counsel guidelines often restrict or prefer certain practice areas (e.g., client prohibits patent prosecution, requires diversity reporting for litigation). Guideline compliance checks and ser']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the client entity to which this guideline applies. Links to the client master data.']
  acceptance_date date [note: 'Date on which the firm formally accepted or acknowledged the guideline.']
  acceptance_status varchar [note: 'Firms acceptance status of the client guideline: accepted (firm agrees to comply), rejected (firm declines engagement under these terms), under review, conditionally accepted (with negotiated exceptions), or not applicable.']
  afa_permitted boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the client permits or encourages alternative fee arrangements (fixed fee, capped fee, contingency, blended rate) as opposed to hourly billing.']
  afa_preference varchar [note: 'Description of the clients preferred alternative fee arrangement structures if AFAs are permitted (e.g., fixed fee for transactional work, success fee for litigation).']
  billing_rate_cap_associate decimal(15,2) [note: 'Maximum hourly billing rate permitted for associates under this guideline. Null if no cap specified.']
  billing_rate_cap_paralegal decimal(15,2) [note: 'Maximum hourly billing rate permitted for paralegals under this guideline. Null if no cap specified.']
  billing_rate_cap_partner decimal(15,2) [note: 'Maximum hourly billing rate permitted for equity partners under this guideline. Null if no cap specified.']
  block_billing_prohibited boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the client prohibits block billing (single time entry covering multiple tasks). If true, each task must be separately itemized.']
  budget_approval_threshold decimal(15,2) [note: 'Monetary threshold above which matter budgets or budget overruns require explicit client approval before proceeding.']
  budget_threshold_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the budget approval threshold.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this guideline record was first created in the system. Audit trail field.']
  data_residency_requirement varchar [note: 'Geographic or jurisdictional data residency requirements imposed by the client (e.g., all matter data must remain within EU, UK-only storage). Null if no restriction.']
  diversity_reporting_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the client requires periodic diversity and inclusion reporting on matter staffing (e.g., gender, ethnicity, LGBTQ+ representation).']
  ebilling_platform_mandate varchar [note: 'Name of the e-billing platform the client mandates for invoice submission (e.g., Serengeti Tracker, Legal Tracker, Collaborati, Brightflag). Null if no specific platform required.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date from which this guideline version becomes binding and must be applied to client matters.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date on which this guideline version ceases to be effective. Null for open-ended guidelines.']
  guideline_document_url varchar [note: 'URL or file path to the stored outside counsel guideline document in the firms document management system (e.g., iManage Work, NetDocuments).']
  guideline_name varchar [note: 'Business-friendly name or title of the outside counsel guideline document or preference set.']
  guideline_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the guideline: formal outside counsel guideline issued by client, operational service preference, or hybrid combining both.']
  invoice_delivery_email varchar [note: 'Email address to which invoices should be sent if email delivery method is specified.']
  invoice_delivery_method varchar [note: 'Method by which invoices should be delivered to the client: email, e-billing portal upload, postal mail, or secure file transfer (SFTP).']
  language_preference varchar [note: 'Two-letter ISO 639-1 language code representing the clients preferred language for all communications and documentation (e.g., en for English, fr for French, de for German).']
  matter_update_preference varchar [note: 'Clients preference for how matter updates should be communicated: format, level of detail, and escalation triggers.']
  minimum_time_increment decimal(5,2) [note: 'Minimum time increment for billing entries in hours (e.g., 0.10 for 6-minute increments, 0.25 for 15-minute increments). Null if no specific requirement.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this guideline record was last modified. Audit trail field.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional notes, exceptions, or special instructions related to this guideline that do not fit structured fields.']
  ocg_client_profile_fk varchar [note: 'FK to client.client_profile.client_profile_id — OCGs govern service delivery per client. Required for billing compliance and staffing decisions.']
  ocg_to_client_profile varchar [note: 'FK to client.client_profile.client_profile_id — OCGs are issued by clients and govern service delivery — must link to the client they apply to']
  outside_counsel_evaluator_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the client-side outside counsel evaluator for guideline-related inquiries and compliance reporting.']
  outside_counsel_evaluator_name varchar [note: 'Name of the client-side individual responsible for evaluating and approving outside counsel guideline compliance (typically General Counsel or Legal Operations Manager).']
  outside_counsel_guideline_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the guideline record: active (currently in force), inactive (temporarily suspended), superseded (replaced by newer version), archived (historical record).']
  preferred_billing_format varchar [note: 'Clients preferred invoice format: LEDES electronic standard, PDF narrative, e-billing portal submission, Excel spreadsheet, or custom format.']
  preferred_communication_channel varchar [note: 'Clients preferred primary communication channel for routine matter correspondence and updates.']
  rate_cap_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for billing rate caps (e.g., USD, GBP, EUR).']
  reporting_frequency varchar [note: 'Frequency at which the client expects matter status reports and updates: weekly, biweekly, monthly, quarterly, on-demand, or milestone-based.']
  staffing_restriction varchar [note: 'Free-text description of any staffing restrictions imposed by the client (e.g., maximum number of timekeepers, seniority mix requirements, named attorney requirements).']
  sustainability_reporting_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the client requires sustainability or environmental impact reporting (e.g., carbon footprint of legal services, paperless billing compliance).']
  travel_expense_policy varchar [note: 'Summary of client travel and expense reimbursement policy: permissible travel classes, accommodation standards, meal per diems, and approval requirements.']
  utbms_task_code_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the client requires UTBMS task codes on all time entries and invoices for standardized billing categorization.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the guideline document (e.g., 1.0, 2.3, 2024-Q1). Tracks revisions over time.']

  Note: 'Consolidated record of all client service delivery requirements, encompassing both formal outside counsel guidelines (OCGs) and operational service preferences. For OCGs: captures guideline version, effective date, expiry date, billing rate caps, staffing restrictions, budget approval thresholds, e-billing platform mandate, UTBMS task code requirements, diversity reporting obligations, travel and expense policy, and acceptance status. For operational preferences: captures preferred billing format (LEDES, PDF, e-billing portal), invoice delivery method, reporting frequency, matter update preferences, preferred communication channel, language preference, data residency requirements, and sustainability reporting flags. Represents the complete how to serve this client specification that governs matter staffing, billing, and reporting. Sourced from Elite 3E billing preferences, Microsoft Dynamics 365 CRM, and client-issued OCG documents.'
}

Table client.credit_standing {
  credit_standing_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the client credit standing record. Primary key for this entity.']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the client entity for whom this credit standing record applies. Links to the client master data.']
  timekeeper_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the partner (timekeeper) who approved the current credit rating and limit for this client. Typically the relationship partner or practice group leader.']
  average_days_to_pay varchar [note: 'Rolling average number of days between invoice date and payment receipt for this client over the past 12 months. Key metric for collections prioritization and credit review.']
  bad_debt_write_off_total decimal(18,2) [note: 'Cumulative total of bad debt write-offs for this client over the firms history. Used in credit rating determination and risk assessment.']
  billing_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code in which this client is invoiced. All credit standing amounts are expressed in this currency unless otherwise noted.']
  collections_tier varchar [note: 'Current collections priority tier for this client. Determines frequency and intensity of collections outreach. Escalated and legal_action tiers may involve external counsel or litigation.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this credit standing record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage.']
  credit_hold_date date [note: 'Date when the credit hold was placed on this client. Null if credit_hold_flag is false. Used to track duration of hold and escalation timing.']
  credit_hold_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this client is currently on credit hold. When true, new work intake and billing may be restricted pending collections or credit review.']
  credit_hold_reason varchar [note: 'Reason code explaining why the client is on credit hold. Null if credit_hold_flag is false. Used for collections escalation and partner notification. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: credit_limit_exceeded|payment_default|bankruptcy_filing|dispute_pending|kyc_incomplete|aml_review|manual_hold — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  credit_insurance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the firm carries credit insurance or receivables protection for this client. Used in risk assessment and collections strategy.']
  credit_insurance_policy_number varchar [note: 'Policy number for credit insurance covering this client, if applicable. Null if credit_insurance_flag is false.']
  credit_limit decimal(18,2) [note: 'Maximum outstanding balance (AR + WIP) allowed for this client before credit hold is triggered. Expressed in firm base currency.']
  credit_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes from credit reviewers, collections staff, or partners regarding this clients credit standing. May include payment history context, relationship considerations, or special arrangements.']
  credit_rating varchar [note: 'Internal credit tier assigned to the client for billing risk management. A = highest creditworthiness, D = highest risk. Used to determine payment terms and credit limits.']
  credit_review_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent credit review for this client. Credit reviews are typically conducted annually or triggered by payment issues. Used to schedule next review.']
  credit_standing_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of this credit standing record. Active = normal operations, under_review = credit review in progress, suspended = credit hold or restrictions, closed = client relationship terminated.']
  credit_utilization_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of credit limit currently utilized, calculated as (total_credit_exposure / credit_limit) * 100. Values above 80% typically trigger prebilling alerts or retainer top-up requests.']
  current_wip_exposure decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total unbilled work in progress for this client across all open matters. Represents time and disbursements recorded but not yet invoiced. Sourced from Elite 3E WIP ledger.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date from which this credit standing record is effective. Used for historical tracking and audit trail when credit terms are modified.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date when this credit standing record expires or is superseded by a new record. Null for current active record. Used for historical tracking and audit trail.']
  external_credit_score varchar [note: 'Credit score or rating from external credit bureau or rating agency (e.g., Dun & Bradstreet, Experian). Used to supplement internal credit rating. Format varies by provider.']
  external_credit_score_date date [note: 'Date when the external credit score was obtained or last refreshed. Used to determine if score needs updating during credit review.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this credit standing record was last updated. Used for audit trail and to track frequency of credit standing changes.']
  last_payment_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Amount of the most recent payment received from this client. Provides context for payment behavior analysis.']
  last_payment_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent payment received from this client. Used to calculate payment velocity and aging metrics.']
  last_write_off_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent bad debt write-off for this client. Null if no write-offs have occurred. Used to assess recent payment issues.']
  next_credit_review_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next credit review. Typically set to 12 months after last review or sooner if risk indicators are present.']
  outstanding_ar_balance decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total unpaid invoiced amount for this client. Includes all open invoices minus payments and adjustments. Sourced from Elite 3E AR module.']
  payment_method_preference varchar [note: 'Preferred payment method for this client. Used to streamline collections and payment processing. May be specified in letter of engagement or client setup.']
  payment_terms varchar [note: 'Standard payment terms agreed with the client. Defines the number of days from invoice date by which payment is expected, or alternative arrangement such as retainer-based or AFA milestone billing.']
  retainer_balance decimal(18,2) [note: 'Current balance held in trust or retainer account for this client. Positive balance indicates funds available to offset future invoices. Sourced from Elite 3E trust accounting or Aderant Expert retainer module.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the operational system from which this credit standing record was sourced. Typically Elite 3E or Aderant Expert. Used for data lineage and reconciliation.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for this credit standing record in the source operational system. Used for data lineage, reconciliation, and troubleshooting.']
  total_credit_exposure decimal(18,2) [note: 'Combined total of outstanding AR balance and current WIP exposure. Represents the firms total financial exposure to this client. Compared against credit_limit for risk assessment.']

  Note: 'Tracks the credit and financial standing of a client for billing risk management purposes. Captures credit limit, current WIP exposure, outstanding AR balance, credit rating (internal tier: A, B, C, D), payment terms (net 30, net 60, retainer-based), retainer balance, last payment date, average days to pay, credit hold flag, credit hold reason, credit review date, and credit approving partner. Sourced from Elite 3E AR and billing modules and Aderant Expert collections. Supports prebilling decisions, retainer top-up triggers, and collections escalation.'
}

Table client.service_engagement {
  service_engagement_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for this client-service engagement record. Primary key.']
  legal_service_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the legal service offering record. Identifies which service offering is being engaged.']
  primary_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the client profile record. Identifies which client this service engagement belongs to.']
  responsible_partner_timekeeper_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the partner (timekeeper) responsible for delivering this specific service engagement. May differ from the overall client relationship partner.']
  service_client_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key to client_profile. Part of the many-to-many relationship.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval and lifecycle status of this engagement. Tracks workflow from initial proposal through active delivery to completion. Explicitly identified in detection phase relationship data.']
  billing_arrangement varchar [note: 'Specific billing arrangement negotiated for this engagement, which may differ from the services default pricing model.']
  created_by_user_code varchar [note: 'Reference to the user who created this engagement record.']
  created_date timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this engagement record was created in the system.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date when this service engagement became active and the firm began delivering this service to the client. Explicitly identified in detection phase relationship data.']
  engagement_letter_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier to the formal engagement letter (LOE) or contract document that governs this service engagement.']
  estimated_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Estimated total value of this service engagement in the clients preferred billing currency.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date when this service engagement expires or was terminated. Null indicates ongoing engagement. Explicitly identified in detection phase relationship data.']
  last_modified_by_user_code varchar [note: 'Reference to the user who last modified this engagement record.']
  last_modified_date timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this engagement record was last modified.']
  rate_override decimal(15,2) [note: 'Client-negotiated rate for this specific service engagement, overriding the standard rate defined in legal_service. Null indicates standard rate applies. Explicitly identified in detection phase relationship data.']
  service_scope_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the scope of this specific engagement, tailoring the general service offering to this clients specific needs and context. Explicitly identified in detection phase relationship data.']
  sla_terms varchar [note: 'Client-specific service level agreement terms negotiated for this engagement, which may differ from standard SLA defined in legal_service. Explicitly identified in detection phase relationship data.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the contractual engagement between a client and a specific legal service offering. It captures the terms, pricing, scope, and lifecycle of each service engagement. Each record links one client to one legal service with attributes that exist only in the context of this specific engagement relationship, including negotiated rates, service-level agreements, approval workflows, and engagement-specific scope definitions.. Existence Justification: In legal services, clients engage multiple legal services simultaneously (a corporate client may have active M&A, litigation, IP, and employment law engagements), and each legal service offering is delivered to multiple clients concurrently. The relationship between client and service is not a simple reference but a managed engagement with specific terms, negotiated rates, service-level agreements, scope definitions, and lifecycle tracking from proposal through delivery to completion.'
}

Table client.subscription {
  subscription_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for this client-package subscription record. Primary key.']
  package_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the service package record. Identifies which service package the client has subscribed to.']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the client profile record. Identifies which client holds this subscription.']
  responsible_partner_timekeeper_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the partner (timekeeper) responsible for managing this specific subscription relationship, including renewals, pricing negotiations, and service delivery oversight. May differ from the clients overall relationship partner when specialized practice area partners manage specific package subscriptions.']
  auto_renew_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this subscription automatically renews at the end of the subscription term without requiring explicit client approval. True enables automatic renewal; False requires manual renewal negotiation. Impacts revenue forecasting and client engagement workflows.']
  billing_frequency varchar [note: 'Frequency at which the client is billed for this service package subscription. Determines invoice generation schedule and revenue recognition pattern. May differ from standard package terms based on client-specific agreements.']
  contract_reference_number varchar [note: 'External reference number or identifier for the legal contract or engagement letter governing this service package subscription. Links to physical or electronic contract documents in the document management system for audit and compliance purposes.']
  created_date timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this subscription record was created in the system. Used for audit trail and subscription lifecycle tracking.']
  discount_applied decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage discount applied to the base package price for this client subscription. Captures negotiated discounts, promotional offers, or relationship-based pricing adjustments. Used to calculate package_price from base price and for discount analysis.']
  end_date date [note: 'Date when this clients subscription to the service package expires or was terminated. Null indicates an open-ended or currently active subscription. Used for renewal forecasting and revenue recognition cutoff.']
  last_modified_date timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this subscription record was last updated. Tracks changes to pricing, renewal status, or other subscription terms for audit and change management purposes.']
  package_price decimal(18,2) [note: 'Actual price charged to this specific client for this service package subscription. May differ from the base_price_amount in service_package due to client-specific negotiations, volume discounts, or relationship tier adjustments. Represents the contracted recurring amount.']
  renewal_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the subscription renewal process. Active indicates ongoing subscription; Pending Renewal indicates approaching expiration requiring client decision; Renewed indicates successful renewal executed; Cancelled indicates client-initiated termination; Expired indicates lapsed without renewal; On Hold indicates temporary suspension. Critical for revenue forecasting and client retention workflows.']
  start_date date [note: 'Date when this clients subscription to the service package became active. Marks the beginning of the subscription term and triggers billing cycle initiation.']
  usage_count varchar [note: 'Number of times services within this package subscription have been utilized or invoked by the client. Tracks consumption for usage-based billing models, capacity planning, and value realization analysis. Incremented when matters are opened under this package or when package services are delivered.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the subscription contract between client_profile and service_package. It captures the commercial terms, pricing, and lifecycle status of each clients subscription to a specific service package. Each record links one client to one service package with subscription-specific attributes including pricing overrides, discount terms, renewal status, and usage tracking that exist only in the context of this subscription relationship. This is the SSOT for all active and historical client-package subscription relationships enabling recurring revenue management, renewal forecasting, and cross-sell analytics.. Existence Justification: In legal services, clients routinely subscribe to multiple service packages simultaneously (e.g., a corporate client may have an ongoing compliance retainer, an IP portfolio management package, and a regulatory advisory package). Conversely, each service package is sold to multiple clients with client-specific pricing, discount terms, renewal cycles, and usage patterns. The subscription relationship is actively managed by relationship partners who negotiate pricing, track renewals, monitor usage, and identify cross-sell opportunities.'
}

Table client.document_access {
  document_access_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for the document access grant record']
  client_contact_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the contact who has been granted access to the document']
  document_revoked_by_timekeeper_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the timekeeper who revoked access, if applicable']
  document_shared_by_timekeeper_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the timekeeper (attorney, paralegal) who shared the document with this contact. Identified in detection phase relationship data.']
  legal_document_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the legal document for which access has been granted']
  access_channel varchar [note: 'Channel through which the document was shared with the contact (client portal, email link, direct share, matter workspace)']
  access_count varchar [note: 'Number of times the contact has accessed or viewed this document']
  access_date timestamp [note: 'Date and time when access to the document was granted to the contact. Identified in detection phase relationship data.']
  access_type varchar [note: 'Type of access granted to the contact for this document. Identified in detection phase relationship data.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date on which access to the document expires for this contact. Used for time-limited sharing in client portals. Identified in detection phase relationship data.']
  is_active boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this access grant is currently active or has been revoked']
  last_accessed_date timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the contact last accessed or viewed this document']
  permission_level varchar [note: 'Permission level granted to the contact for this document (view-only, download, comment, full access). Identified in detection phase relationship data.']
  revoked_date timestamp [note: 'Date and time when access was revoked, if applicable']

  Note: 'This association product represents the access grant between contact and legal_document. It captures the sharing, permission, and access control relationship when legal documents are shared with client contacts through client portals or matter management systems. Each record links one contact to one legal_document with attributes that exist only in the context of this access relationship, including who shared it, when access was granted, permission levels, and expiration dates.. Existence Justification: In legal practice, document sharing and access control is a core operational process managed through client portals and document management systems. A single legal document (contract, brief, disclosure) is routinely shared with multiple client contacts (in-house counsel, compliance officers, business stakeholders, signatories) with different permission levels and access windows. Conversely, each contact accesses many documents across multiple matters over time. The firm actively manages these access grants—creating them when documents are shared, tracking who accessed what and when, setting expiration dates for sensitive documents, and revoking access when matters close or contacts leave client organizations.'
}

Table client.kyc_document_verification {
  kyc_document_verification_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for this KYC document verification record. Primary key for the association.']
  kyc_record_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the KYC/AML due diligence dossier that this document verification supports']
  legal_document_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the legal document being verified as part of KYC compliance']
  reviewer_timekeeper_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the compliance officer or timekeeper who verified this specific document for this specific KYC review. May differ from the overall KYC review owner for large dossiers with multiple reviewers.']
  created_date timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this document was linked to this KYC review']
  document_role varchar [note: 'The specific KYC requirement or purpose that this document fulfills within the context of this particular KYC review (e.g., identity verification, address proof, corporate structure evidence)']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date on which this document expires and will no longer satisfy KYC requirements, triggering the need for updated documentation in future reviews.']
  last_updated_date timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to this verification record']
  verification_date date [note: 'Date on which the compliance officer verified this document as satisfactory for this KYC review. Critical for audit trail and regulatory compliance.']
  verification_method varchar [note: 'Method by which this document was verified for this KYC review (original inspection, certified copy, electronic verification, notarization)']
  verification_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes recorded by the compliance officer regarding this specific documents verification within this KYC review context (e.g., concerns, clarifications, follow-up required)']
  verification_status varchar [note: 'Current status of this documents verification within the context of this KYC review. Tracks the compliance officers assessment of this specific document for this specific review.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the verification relationship between KYC reviews and supporting legal documents. It captures which documents satisfy which KYC requirements, the verification status of each document within the context of a specific KYC review, verification dates, expiry tracking, and the reviewer responsible. Each record links one KYC dossier to one supporting document with attributes that exist only in the context of this compliance verification relationship.. Existence Justification: In legal practice, a single KYC review dossier requires multiple supporting documents (passport, utility bill, incorporation certificate, beneficial ownership register, financial statements), and a single document (such as audited financials or a corporate registry extract) can legitimately support multiple KYC reviews across related entities (parent/subsidiary relationships) or periodic renewals for the same client. Compliance teams actively manage which documents satisfy which KYC requirements, track verification status per document-per-review, and monitor expiry dates for periodic re-verification.'
}

Table client.vendor {
  vendor_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for vendor']
  organisation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key to client.organisation.organisation_id']
  parent_vendor_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on vendor (parent_vendor_id)']
  annual_spend_limit decimal(18,2) [note: 'Maximum annual spending threshold approved for this vendor without additional authorization.']
  bank_account_number varchar [note: 'Vendors bank account number for electronic payment processing.']
  bank_name varchar [note: 'Name of the financial institution where the vendor maintains their payment account.']
  bank_routing_number varchar [note: 'Bank routing or transit number for the vendors financial institution.']
  business_address_line1 varchar [note: 'First line of the vendors primary business address including street number and name.']
  business_address_line2 varchar [note: 'Second line of the vendors business address for suite, floor, or building information.']
  business_city varchar [note: 'City where the vendors primary business location is situated.']
  business_country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code for the vendors primary business location.']
  business_postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code for the vendors business address.']
  business_state_province varchar [note: 'State or province of the vendors primary business address.']
  conflict_check_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the vendor must undergo conflict of interest screening before engagement on client matters.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the vendor record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for vendor transactions and payments.']
  diversity_certification varchar [note: 'Type of diversity certification held by the vendor such as minority-owned, women-owned, veteran-owned, or disadvantaged business enterprise status. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: mbe|wbe|vbe|dbe|lgbtbe|sdvosb|none — promote to reference product]']
  insurance_expiration_date date [note: 'Date when the vendors current liability insurance policy expires, requiring renewal verification.']
  insurance_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the vendor is required to maintain liability insurance coverage as a condition of engagement.']
  last_review_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent vendor performance or compliance review conducted by the firm.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the vendor record was last updated or modified.']
  nda_expiration_date date [note: 'Date when the current non-disclosure agreement with the vendor expires.']
  nda_signed_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the vendor has executed a non-disclosure agreement to protect confidential client information.']
  next_review_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next periodic vendor review or recertification.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-form text field for capturing additional information, special instructions, or historical context about the vendor.']
  onboarding_date date [note: 'Date when the vendor was officially onboarded and approved for engagement by the firm.']
  payment_method varchar [note: 'Preferred method of payment for vendor invoices.']
  payment_terms varchar [note: 'Standard payment terms agreed with the vendor specifying the timeframe for invoice payment.']
  preferred_vendor_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the vendor has been designated as a preferred supplier with negotiated rates or priority status.']
  primary_contact_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the primary contact person at the vendor for business correspondence.']
  primary_contact_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the primary contact person at the vendor organization for operational communication.']
  primary_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Primary telephone number for reaching the vendor contact person.']
  risk_rating varchar [note: 'Assessment of the vendors risk level based on financial stability, compliance history, and service criticality.']
  tax_identification_number varchar [note: 'Government-issued tax identification number for the vendor entity used for tax reporting and compliance.']
  termination_date date [note: 'Date when the vendor relationship was formally terminated or the vendor status changed to inactive.']
  termination_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation or business justification for terminating the vendor relationship.']
  vendor_category varchar [note: 'High-level categorization of the vendor for spend analysis and procurement reporting.']
  vendor_code varchar [note: 'The externally-known unique business identifier or code assigned to the vendor for reference in procurement and financial systems.']
  vendor_name varchar [note: 'The full legal name of the vendor organization or individual providing services or products to the firm.']
  vendor_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the vendor relationship indicating whether the vendor is approved for engagement.']
  vendor_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the vendor based on the type of goods or services provided to the legal practice.']
  website_url varchar [note: 'Primary website address for the vendors online presence and service information.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for vendor. Referenced by vendor_id.'
}

Table matter.tribunal {
  tribunal_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the tribunal, court, arbitral body, or ADR (Alternative Dispute Resolution) forum. Primary key for the tribunal master record.']
  presiding_judge_id varchar [note: 'FK to court.judge']
  address_line_1 varchar [note: 'Primary street address of the tribunals courthouse or administrative office (e.g., 500 Pearl Street). Used for physical filing, in-person appearances, and service of process. Organizational contact data classified as confidential.']
  address_line_2 varchar [note: 'Secondary address information such as suite, floor, or building number (e.g., Suite 200, Courtroom 15A). Organizational contact data classified as confidential.']
  adr_program_description varchar [note: 'Textual description of the ADR programs offered by the tribunal, including mediation panels, arbitration services, early neutral evaluation, and settlement conference procedures. Null if ADR services are not available.']
  adr_services_available boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the tribunal offers or requires Alternative Dispute Resolution (ADR) services such as mediation, arbitration, or settlement conferences as part of its case management process. True if ADR programs are available, False otherwise.']
  arbitration_rules_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the arbitration rules governing proceedings before this tribunal if it is an arbitral body (e.g., ICC Arbitration Rules 2021, LCIA Arbitration Rules 2020, UNCITRAL Arbitration Rules, AAA Commercial Arbitration Rules). Null for non-arbitration tribunals.']
  business_hours varchar [note: 'The standard business hours during which the tribunals clerks office is open for filings, inquiries, and in-person visits (e.g., Monday-Friday 9:00 AM - 5:00 PM, 08:30-16:30 GMT). Used to determine filing deadlines and availability for emergency motions.']
  city varchar [note: 'The city or municipality where the tribunals primary courthouse or administrative office is located (e.g., New York, Los Angeles, London, Paris, The Hague). Used for physical appearance scheduling and document delivery.']
  clerk_of_court_name varchar [note: 'The name of the clerk of court or court administrator responsible for managing case filings, dockets, and administrative functions. Primary contact for procedural questions and filing issues.']
  closure_date date [note: 'The date the tribunal was officially closed, dissolved, or merged into another tribunal. Null if the tribunal is still operational.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code representing the primary country where the tribunal is located or operates (e.g., USA, GBR, FRA, CHE for Switzerland-based ICC). For international tribunals, the code of the host country or headquarters.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this tribunal record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  ecf_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the tribunal supports Electronic Case Filing (ECF) for electronic submission of pleadings, motions, and other court documents. True if ECF is available, False if paper filing is required or ECF is not yet implemented.']
  ecf_enrollment_status varchar [note: 'The firms enrollment status with the tribunals ECF system. Enrolled means the firm has active credentials and can file electronically. Pending indicates an application is in progress. Not Enrolled means the firm must enroll before filing. Not Applicable if the tribunal does not offer ECF.']
  ecf_system_name varchar [note: 'The name or identifier of the electronic filing system used by the tribunal (e.g., CM/ECF for federal courts, NYSCEF for New York State courts, File & ServeXpress for California). Null if ECF is not enabled.']
  email_address varchar [note: 'Official email address for the tribunals clerks office or administrative contact. Used for electronic correspondence, filing confirmations, and procedural notifications. Organizational contact data classified as confidential.']
  established_date date [note: 'The date the tribunal was officially established or chartered. For historical courts, this may be the date of enabling legislation. For arbitration panels, the date the institution was founded.']
  fax_number varchar [note: 'Fax number for the tribunals clerks office. Some courts still require or accept fax filings for certain document types or emergency motions. Organizational contact data classified as confidential.']
  filing_deadline_time timestamp [note: 'The cutoff time by which electronic or physical filings must be submitted to be considered filed on that business day (e.g., 11:59 PM local time for ECF, 5:00 PM for paper filings). Critical for calculating compliance with procedural deadlines.']
  filing_fee_schedule_url varchar [note: 'URL to the tribunals published schedule of filing fees for various document types and motions. Used by billing and matter management systems to estimate litigation costs and validate disbursements.']
  jurisdiction_geographic varchar [note: 'The geographic area or territory over which the tribunal has legal authority. For federal courts, this may be a district or circuit (e.g., Southern District of New York, Ninth Circuit). For state courts, the state or county. For international tribunals, the countries or regions covered (e.g., Global, European Union, APAC).']
  jurisdiction_level varchar [note: 'The hierarchical level of legal authority for the tribunal. Federal courts have nationwide jurisdiction, state courts operate within state boundaries, county and municipal courts handle local matters, international tribunals (ICC, LCIA, ICJ) handle cross-border disputes, and tribal courts operate within sovereign tribal lands. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: federal|state|county|municipal|international|regional|tribal — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  language_primary varchar [note: 'The primary language in which proceedings are conducted and documents must be filed (e.g., English, French, Spanish, Mandarin). For international tribunals, this may be the official working language.']
  language_secondary varchar [note: 'A secondary language accepted by the tribunal for filings or proceedings, if applicable. Common in bilingual jurisdictions (e.g., French in Canadian federal courts, Spanish in Puerto Rico). Null if only one language is accepted.']
  local_rules_url varchar [note: 'URL to the tribunals local rules of procedure, which supplement federal or state rules with court-specific requirements for formatting, filing deadlines, motion practice, and courtroom decorum. Essential reference for litigation teams.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional information about the tribunal, including special filing procedures, historical context, relationships with other courts, or firm-specific practice notes (e.g., Judge Smith prefers courtesy copies for motions over 20 pages, This court requires a separate courtesy copy for chambers).']
  operational_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the tribunal. Active indicates the tribunal is fully operational and accepting filings. Inactive means the tribunal is no longer hearing cases. Temporarily Closed indicates a temporary suspension (e.g., due to emergency, renovation). Merged indicates the tribunal has been consolidated with another. Dissolved indicates permanent closure.']
  pacer_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the tribunal provides access to case dockets and documents through PACER (Public Access to Court Electronic Records), the federal judiciarys electronic public access service. True for federal courts with PACER access, False otherwise.']
