  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this document relationship record was last modified. Used for audit trail and change tracking.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments about this document relationship. Provides additional context or explanations for the relationship.']
  privilege_designation varchar [note: 'Privilege status designation for the document relationship. Indicates whether the relationship involves privileged communications or work product.']
  production_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this document relationship is included in eDiscovery production sets. Used for production planning and tracking.']
  relationship_basis varchar [note: 'Textual explanation of the business or legal basis for establishing this document relationship. Provides context for why the relationship exists.']
  relationship_category varchar [note: 'High-level category grouping the relationship type. Used for filtering and reporting across relationship classes.']
  relationship_direction varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the relationship is one-way (unidirectional) or two-way (bidirectional). Determines if the relationship implies a reciprocal link.']
  relationship_hash varchar [note: 'Cryptographic hash of the relationship metadata to ensure integrity and detect tampering. Used for audit trail and chain of custody verification.']
  relationship_source_system varchar [note: 'The system or method that created this document relationship. Indicates whether the relationship was system-generated or manually established.']
  relationship_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the document relationship. Indicates whether the relationship is currently valid and active.']
  relationship_type varchar [note: 'The type of relationship between the source and target documents. Defines the semantic nature of the link (e.g., parent-child for email threads, exhibit for court filings, supersedes for contract versions, family_group for eDiscovery deduplication). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: parent_child|attachment|exhibit|supersedes|amends|related|family_group|thread|bundle|reference — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  sequence_number varchar [note: 'Ordinal position of this relationship within a set of related documents. Used for ordering attachments, exhibits, or version chains.']
  target_legal_document varchar [note: 'FK to document.legal_document.legal_document_id — The target document in a relationship pair. Required for bidirectional relationship traversal — every relationship must have a target document reference.']
  thread_position varchar [note: 'Position of the document within an email thread or conversation chain. Used for reconstructing email correspondence sequences.']
  version_sequence varchar [note: 'Sequential version number in a supersedes or amends chain. Used for tracking contract amendment history and document evolution.']
  workspace_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the DMS workspace or Relativity workspace where this relationship is maintained. Provides system context for the relationship.']

  Note: 'Models typed relationships between legal documents including parent-child (email thread to attachment), document family groupings for eDiscovery, exhibit references for court filings, supersedes/amends chains for contract versions, and related-document links for matter workspaces. Captures relationship type, source document, target document, relationship direction, effective date, and matter context. Essential for eDiscovery family deduplication, exhibit list generation, court bundle assembly, and amendment chain tracing.'
}

Table document.custodian_hold {
  custodian_hold_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for this custodian-hold assignment record. Primary key.']
  esi_custodian_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the ESI custodian whose data is subject to preservation under this hold']
  legal_hold_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the legal hold notice issued for this custodian']
  matter_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key to matter.matter.matter_id']
  acknowledgement_date date [note: 'Date on which this custodian formally acknowledged receipt and understanding of the legal hold notice.']
  acknowledgement_status varchar [note: 'Current status of this custodians acknowledgement of the legal hold notice.']
  collection_completion_date date [note: 'Date on which ESI collection activities were completed for this custodian under this hold.']
  collection_custodian_notes varchar [note: 'Internal notes documenting collection activities, challenges, exceptions, and custodian cooperation for this custodian. [Moved from esi_custodian: These notes document collection activities specific to THIS custodian-hold assignment.]']
  collection_file_count varchar [note: 'Total number of files and documents collected from this custodian for this hold.']
  collection_method varchar [note: 'Method used to collect ESI from this custodians data sources for this hold.']
  collection_notes varchar [note: 'Internal notes documenting collection activities, challenges, exceptions, and custodian-specific considerations for this hold assignment.']
  collection_start_date date [note: 'Date on which ESI collection activities commenced for this custodian under this hold.']
  collection_status varchar [note: 'Current status of ESI collection activities for this custodian under this specific legal hold.']
  collection_volume_gb decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total volume of ESI collected from this custodian for this hold, measured in gigabytes.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when this custodian-hold assignment record was created.']
  custodian_interview_date date [note: 'Date on which the custodian interview was conducted to identify relevant data sources and custodial holdings. [Moved from esi_custodian: The interview is conducted in the context of a SPECIFIC hold to identify relevant data sources for that matter. The same custodian may be interviewed multiple times for different holds.]']
  custodian_interview_notes varchar [note: 'Summary notes from the custodian interview documenting data sources, file locations, communication patterns, and relevant ESI holdings. [Moved from esi_custodian: Interview notes are specific to the data sources and preservation scope relevant to THIS hold. Different holds may require different interview focus areas.]']
  custodian_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of this custodian within this specific legal hold workflow.']
  escalation_count varchar [note: 'Number of times this custodians non-compliance with this hold has been escalated to management or legal counsel.']
  exemption_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation for why this custodian was exempted from preservation or collection requirements under this hold (e.g., no relevant data, left organization before trigger date).']
  interview_date date [note: 'Date on which the custodian interview was conducted to identify relevant data sources and preservation scope for this hold.']
  interview_notes varchar [note: 'Summary notes from the custodian interview documenting data sources, file locations, and preservation scope specific to this hold assignment.']
  issued_date date [note: 'Date on which the legal hold notice was issued to this specific custodian, triggering preservation obligations.']
  last_reminder_date date [note: 'Date on which the most recent reminder notice was sent to this custodian for this hold.']
  legal_hold_acknowledgement_status varchar [note: 'Status of the custodians acknowledgement of the legal hold notice. [Moved from esi_custodian: This status is specific to THIS custodians acknowledgement of THIS hold. The same custodian may have different acknowledgement statuses for different holds.]']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when this custodian-hold assignment record was last modified.']
  release_date date [note: 'Date on which the legal hold was released for this specific custodian, ending their preservation obligations.']
  reminder_count varchar [note: 'Number of reminder notices sent to this custodian for this specific hold.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the assignment of legal hold notices to ESI custodians in litigation and investigation matters. It captures the issuance, acknowledgement, compliance monitoring, and release lifecycle for each custodian-hold pairing. Each record links one custodian to one legal hold with attributes that track the custodians specific preservation obligations, acknowledgement status, interview details, and collection activities under that hold. This is a core eDiscovery workflow entity managed actively throughout the litigation lifecycle.. Existence Justification: In legal eDiscovery practice, legal holds are issued to multiple custodians, and custodians are frequently subject to multiple concurrent legal holds across different matters. Law firms and legal departments actively manage custodian-hold assignments as a distinct workflow: issuing notices, tracking acknowledgements, conducting custodian interviews, monitoring collection progress, and releasing custodians when holds are lifted. This is not a reference relationship—it is an operational business process with its own lifecycle, compliance requirements, and risk management implications governed by FRCP Rule 26 and spoliation case law.'
}

Table document.production_source {
  production_source_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for this production-collection source mapping. Primary key.']
  doc_production_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the eDiscovery production set that includes documents from this collection source.']
  esi_collection_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the ESI collection event that serves as a source for this production.']
  matter_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key to matter.matter.matter_id']
  bates_range_end varchar [note: 'Last Bates number assigned to documents from this collection within this production. Marks the end of the Bates range for this collection subset.']
  bates_range_start varchar [note: 'First Bates number assigned to documents from this collection within this production. Since a production may draw from multiple collections, each collection subset receives its own Bates range within the overall production numbering scheme.']
  collection_subset_filter varchar [note: 'Description of any filters, search terms, or date range restrictions applied to this collection when selecting documents for this production. For example, custodian emails 2019-2021 only or responsive documents tagged hot. This filter is specific to the production-collection pairing.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this production-collection source mapping was created in the eDiscovery platform, typically during production assembly workflow.']
  document_count_from_collection varchar [note: 'Number of documents from this specific ESI collection that were included in the production set. This count is specific to the collection-production pairing and cannot live on either entity alone (a collection contributes different counts to different productions; a production draws different counts from different collections).']
  inclusion_reason varchar [note: 'Business reason or legal justification for including this collection in this production (e.g., responsive to RFP 001-005, supplemental production per court order, custodian added to scope).']
  production_date date [note: 'Date on which documents from this collection were included in the production delivery. In multi-phase productions, different collections may be produced on different dates, making this attribute specific to the collection-production relationship.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User or system account that created this production-collection source mapping, important for audit trail and workflow tracking.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the sourcing relationship between eDiscovery production sets and the ESI collections from which they are derived. In eDiscovery workflows, a single production may draw documents from multiple collection events (e.g., combining custodian collections), and a single collection may contribute documents to multiple productions over time (initial production, supplemental production, different matters). Each record captures the subset of documents from a specific collection included in a specific production, along with the Bates numbering applied to that subset and any collection-specific filters or date ranges used during production assembly.. Existence Justification: In eDiscovery operations, production sets routinely draw documents from multiple ESI collection events (e.g., combining collections from different custodians, time periods, or data sources into a single production), and a single collection event commonly contributes documents to multiple productions over the lifecycle of a matter (initial production, supplemental productions, productions for different matters sharing custodians). Legal teams actively manage this many-to-many relationship in platforms like Relativity during production assembly, applying collection-specific Bates ranges, filters, and document counts that exist only in the context of each production-collection pairing.'
}

Table document.template_clause_usage {
  template_clause_usage_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for this template-clause usage record. Primary key.']
  added_by_timekeeper_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the knowledge management professional or attorney who added this clause to the template. Provides accountability for template design decisions.']
  clause_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the clause library entry being used in the template']
  doc_template_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the document template that incorporates this clause']
  added_date date [note: 'Date when this clause was first incorporated into this template. Tracks the history of template evolution.']
  clause_library_references varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of clause library identifiers for standard clauses incorporated into or available for use with this template. Enables lawyers to access pre-approved alternative language and optional provisions. [Moved from doc_template: This attribute in doc_template is a comma-separated list of clause identifiers, which is a denormalized representation of the many-to-many relationship. The proper normalized model is this association table, making this attribute redundant and a candidate for removal or deprecation.]']
  clause_position_in_template varchar [note: 'The ordinal position or sequence number of this clause within the template document structure. Determines the order in which clauses appear when the template is assembled.']
  clause_variant_selected varchar [note: 'The specific variant of the clause selected for use in this template (e.g., Standard, Aggressive, Conservative, Client-Favorable). Maps to the variant_label in the clause library.']
  customization_notes varchar [note: 'Template-specific notes describing any customizations, modifications, or special instructions for this clause when used in this particular template. Captures context that applies only to this template-clause combination.']
  last_updated_date date [note: 'Date when this template-clause usage record was last modified, including changes to position, variant, customization notes, or mandatory status. Tracks the evolution of template composition.']
  mandatory_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this clause is mandatory (cannot be removed) or optional (can be excluded) when using this template. The same clause may be mandatory in one template and optional in another.']
  section_heading varchar [note: 'The section or heading under which this clause appears in the template (e.g., Representations and Warranties, Indemnification, General Provisions). Provides structural context.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the operational relationship between document templates and clause library entries in the firms knowledge management system. It captures which clauses are incorporated into which templates, including the clauses position within the template, the specific variant selected, customization notes, and whether the clause is mandatory or optional. Each record represents a deliberate design decision by knowledge management professionals about template composition and is actively managed as templates evolve.. Existence Justification: In legal knowledge management systems, document templates are assembled from multiple reusable clause library entries, and each clause is designed to be reused across multiple templates. Knowledge management teams actively manage these template-clause mappings, making deliberate decisions about which clause variant to use, where to position it, whether its mandatory, and any template-specific customizations. This is a core operational process in contract automation and template governance.'
}

Table document.review_set {
  review_set_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for review_set']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the attorney or legal professional responsible for overseeing this review set.']
  legal_hold_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the legal hold order under which this review set was preserved and collected.']
  matter_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the legal matter or case to which this review set belongs.']
  parent_review_set_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on review_set (parent_review_set_id)']
  policy_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the records retention policy governing the lifecycle and disposal of this review set.']
  production_set_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the production set generated from this review set for disclosure to opposing counsel or regulatory authorities.']
  review_created_by_user_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the user who created the review set record.']
  review_modified_by_user_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the user who last modified the review set record.']
  tar_model_id varchar [note: 'Reference identifier for the TAR or predictive coding model applied to this review set, if applicable.']
  assigned_reviewer_count varchar [note: 'Number of reviewers assigned to work on this review set.']
  chain_of_custody_verified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the chain of custody for all documents in the review set has been verified and documented for evidentiary purposes.']
  completed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the review set was marked as completed, indicating all review activities have concluded.']
  confidentiality_designation varchar [note: 'Protective designation assigned to the review set indicating the level of confidentiality and access restrictions.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the review set record was first created in the system.']
  custodian_count varchar [note: 'Number of distinct custodians (document owners or sources) represented in this review set.']
  date_range_end date [note: 'Latest document date included in the review set, defining the temporal scope of the collection.']
  date_range_start date [note: 'Earliest document date included in the review set, defining the temporal scope of the collection.']
  deduplication_applied boolean [note: 'Indicates whether deduplication processing was applied to remove duplicate documents from the review set.']
  document_count varchar [note: 'Total number of documents included in this review set at the time of measurement.']
  email_threading_applied boolean [note: 'Indicates whether email threading analysis was applied to organize email conversations within the review set.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the review set record was last modified.']
  near_duplicate_identification_applied boolean [note: 'Indicates whether near-duplicate detection algorithms were applied to identify substantially similar documents.']
  nlp_processing_applied boolean [note: 'Indicates whether Natural Language Processing techniques were applied for entity extraction, sentiment analysis, or other linguistic analysis.']
  ocr_applied boolean [note: 'Indicates whether Optical Character Recognition processing was applied to extract text from image-based documents.']
  privileged_document_count varchar [note: 'Number of documents designated as privileged (attorney-client privilege, work product, etc.) within this review set.']
  quality_control_accuracy_rate decimal(5,4) [note: 'Measured accuracy rate from quality control sampling, expressed as a decimal proportion.']
  quality_control_sample_size varchar [note: 'Number of documents selected for quality control review to validate coding accuracy and consistency.']
  responsive_document_count varchar [note: 'Number of documents marked as responsive to the legal matter or discovery request within this review set.']
  review_methodology varchar [note: 'The review approach or methodology applied to this set, such as Technology Assisted Review (TAR), Continuous Active Learning (CAL), or linear review.']
  review_platform varchar [note: 'Name of the eDiscovery or document review platform where this review set is hosted and managed.']
  review_set_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique code or identifier for the review set, often used for cross-system reference and reporting.']
  review_set_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the review set purpose, scope, and any special instructions or context.']
  review_set_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name or title assigned to the review set for identification and organizational purposes.']
  review_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the review set indicating its operational state.']
  review_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the review set indicating the purpose or stage of document review (e.g., first pass review, privilege review, quality control).']
  reviewed_document_count varchar [note: 'Number of documents that have been reviewed and coded within this review set.']
  search_terms_applied varchar [note: 'Summary or reference to the keyword search terms, Boolean queries, or filters applied to create this review set.']
  tar_precision_score decimal(5,4) [note: 'Precision metric of the TAR model indicating the proportion of predicted responsive documents that are actually responsive.']
  tar_recall_score decimal(5,4) [note: 'Recall metric of the TAR model indicating the proportion of all responsive documents that were correctly identified.']
  total_page_count varchar [note: 'Aggregate count of all pages across all documents in the review set.']
  total_size_gb decimal(18,4) [note: 'Total data volume of all documents in the review set measured in gigabytes.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for review_set. Referenced by review_set_id.'
}

Table document.review_session {
  review_session_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for review_session']
  attorney_profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the supervising attorney who provides legal oversight and quality assurance for this review session.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the attorney or senior reviewer responsible for overseeing this review session.']
  matter_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the legal matter or case to which this review session belongs.']
  prior_review_session_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on review_session (prior_review_session_id)']
  review_created_by_user_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the user who created this review session record.']
  review_modified_by_user_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the user who last modified this review session record.']
  review_project_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the eDiscovery or document review project under which this session is organized.']
  review_set_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific collection or batch of documents assigned to this review session.']
  workspace_id varchar [note: 'External identifier for the workspace or case environment within the review platform where this session was conducted.']
  actual_cost decimal(15,2) [note: 'Actual cost incurred for this review session, calculated from recorded hours and billing rates.']
  actual_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Actual date and time when the review session was completed or terminated.']
  actual_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Actual date and time when the review session commenced, capturing the real-world event time.']
  average_review_time_seconds decimal(10,2) [note: 'Mean time in seconds spent reviewing each document during this session, used for productivity and cost analysis.']
  coding_layout_name varchar [note: 'Name of the coding layout or form template used by reviewers to code documents during this session.']
  confidentiality_level varchar [note: 'Classification level indicating the sensitivity and access restrictions for documents and data within this review session.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this review session record was first created in the system.']
  custodian_filter varchar [note: 'Custodian or data source filter applied to scope the documents included in this review session.']
  date_range_filter_end date [note: 'End date of the date range filter applied to documents in this review session.']
  date_range_filter_start date [note: 'Start date of the date range filter applied to documents in this review session.']
  documents_pending varchar [note: 'Count of documents remaining to be reviewed in this session.']
  documents_reviewed varchar [note: 'Count of documents that have been reviewed and coded during this session.']
  estimated_cost decimal(15,2) [note: 'Projected cost for completing this review session, based on estimated hours and billing rates.']
  hot_document_count varchar [note: 'Count of documents flagged as hot or key documents requiring special attention or escalation during this session.']
  is_billable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the time and costs associated with this review session are billable to the client.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this review session record was last modified.']
  non_responsive_document_count varchar [note: 'Count of documents coded as non-responsive during this review session.']
  privilege_designation_count varchar [note: 'Count of documents designated as privileged during this review session.']
  quality_control_error_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of documents in the QC sample that were found to have coding errors or inconsistencies.']
  quality_control_sample_size varchar [note: 'Number of documents selected for quality control review to validate coding accuracy and consistency.']
  redaction_required_count varchar [note: 'Count of documents identified as requiring redaction for privileged or confidential information during this session.']
  responsive_document_count varchar [note: 'Count of documents coded as responsive to the matter or discovery request during this session.']
  review_completion_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of assigned documents that have been reviewed, calculated as (documents_reviewed / total_documents_assigned) * 100.']
  review_platform varchar [note: 'The eDiscovery or document review platform used to conduct this review session.']
  review_protocol varchar [note: 'The methodology or protocol applied during this review session, such as Technology Assisted Review (TAR), Continuous Active Learning (CAL), or traditional linear review.']
  review_session_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the review session indicating its operational state.']
  scheduled_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Planned date and time when the review session is scheduled to conclude.']
  scheduled_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Planned date and time when the review session is scheduled to begin.']
  search_terms_applied varchar [note: 'Keyword search terms or queries applied to filter or prioritize documents for this review session.']
  session_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments recorded by the lead reviewer or supervising attorney regarding this review session.']
  session_number varchar [note: 'Business-facing unique identifier for the review session, typically formatted as RS-YYYYMMDD-XXXXXX for external reference and tracking.']
  session_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the review session based on its purpose within the document review workflow.']
  total_documents_assigned varchar [note: 'Total count of documents allocated to this review session for review.']
  total_review_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'Cumulative hours spent by all reviewers during this review session.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for review_session. Referenced by session_id.'
}

Table document.execution_workflow {
  execution_workflow_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for execution_workflow']
  doc_template_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the standard document template used in this execution workflow.']
  doc_type_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the type of legal document this workflow is designed for (e.g., contract, agreement, pleading, motion).']
  execution_created_by_user_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the user who created this workflow definition.']
  execution_modified_by_user_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the user who last modified this workflow definition.']
  owner_user_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the user or attorney who owns and maintains this workflow definition.']
  primary_superseding_execution_workflow_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the newer workflow version that supersedes this one, if applicable.']
  approval_authority_level varchar [note: 'Level of authority required for approval (e.g., Partner, Senior Associate, General Counsel, Board).']
  approval_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether formal approval is required before execution can proceed.']
  audit_trail_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a complete audit trail of all execution activities must be maintained for compliance.']
  business_unit varchar [note: 'Business unit or department that primarily uses this execution workflow.']
  chain_of_custody_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether chain of custody documentation is required for evidentiary purposes.']
  compliance_framework varchar [note: 'Regulatory or compliance framework this workflow must adhere to (e.g., ESIGN Act, UETA, eIDAS, SOX, GDPR).']
  confidentiality_level varchar [note: 'Default confidentiality classification for documents executed through this workflow.']
  cost_center varchar [note: 'Cost center code for financial tracking of execution activities using this workflow.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this workflow record was first created in the system.']
  effective_from_date date [note: 'Date from which this workflow version becomes effective and available for use.']
  effective_until_date date [note: 'Date until which this workflow version remains effective; null for open-ended workflows.']
  escalation_threshold_days varchar [note: 'Number of days after which the workflow should be escalated if not completed.']
  execution_method varchar [note: 'Method by which documents are executed in this workflow (electronic signature, physical wet ink, hybrid, notarized, apostille).']
  is_default_workflow boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this is the default workflow for its document type and jurisdiction.']
  jurisdiction_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code or jurisdiction code where this workflow is applicable (e.g., USA, GBR, CAN).']
  last_used_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this workflow was last used for a document execution.']
  maximum_signatories varchar [note: 'Maximum number of signatories allowed in this execution workflow.']
  minimum_signatories varchar [note: 'Minimum number of signatories required to complete this execution workflow.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this workflow record was last modified.']
  practice_area varchar [note: 'Legal practice area or domain this workflow applies to (e.g., Corporate, Litigation, Intellectual Property, Employment, Regulatory Compliance).']
  privilege_designation varchar [note: 'Legal privilege classification applicable to documents in this workflow (attorney-client, work product, joint defense, none).']
  requires_notarization boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this workflow requires notarization for legal validity.']
  requires_witness boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this workflow requires witness signatures for legal validity.']
  retention_period_years varchar [note: 'Number of years executed documents must be retained per legal and regulatory requirements.']
  sequence_type varchar [note: 'Defines how signatories are sequenced in the workflow (sequential order, parallel/simultaneous, hybrid, conditional routing).']
  signature_platform varchar [note: 'Electronic signature platform or system used for this workflow (e.g., DocuSign, Adobe Sign, HelloSign, internal system).']
  special_instructions varchar [note: 'Additional instructions or notes for users executing documents through this workflow.']
  standard_turnaround_days varchar [note: 'Expected number of business days for completion of this execution workflow under normal circumstances.']
  usage_count varchar [note: 'Total number of times this workflow has been used for document execution.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version number of this workflow definition (e.g., 1.0, 2.1, 3.0.1) for change tracking.']
  workflow_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique business identifier or code for the workflow (e.g., EXEC-STD-001, NDA-SIG-V2).']
  workflow_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the workflow purpose, steps, and business context.']
  workflow_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name or title of the execution workflow (e.g., Contract Execution - Standard, NDA Signature Flow).']
  workflow_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the execution workflow indicating whether it is available for use.']
  workflow_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the execution workflow by its primary purpose (signature, approval, review, attestation, notarization, filing).']

  Note: 'Master reference table for execution_workflow. Referenced by execution_workflow_id.'
}

Table document.workspace {
  workspace_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for workspace']
  matter_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the legal matter associated with this workspace, linking workspace to the underlying legal engagement.']
  originating_attorney_profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the attorney who originated or initiated the workspace, typically the relationship partner or business developer.']
  owner_attorney_profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the attorney who has primary responsibility and oversight for this workspace and its contents.']
  owner_user_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the user who owns or administers this workspace, with full control and delegation rights.']
  parent_workspace_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on workspace (parent_workspace_id)']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the client for whom this workspace is maintained, establishing client ownership and access rights.']
  retention_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the records retention policy governing document lifecycle and destruction schedules for this workspace.']
  workspace_created_by_user_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the user who created this workspace, providing audit trail and accountability.']
  workspace_modified_by_user_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the user who last modified this workspace metadata, supporting audit and compliance requirements.']
  access_control_model varchar [note: 'Security model governing how user permissions and access rights are assigned and enforced for this workspace.']
  archived_date date [note: 'Date when the workspace was moved to archival storage, typically following closure and retention policy requirements.']
  billing_code varchar [note: 'Code used to associate workspace activity and storage costs with client billing and matter accounting.']
  closed_date date [note: 'Business date when the workspace was officially closed and transitioned to inactive or archived status.']
  conflict_check_date date [note: 'Date when the most recent conflict of interest check was performed for this workspace.']
  conflict_check_status varchar [note: 'Current state of ethical conflict of interest review for this workspace and its associated parties.']
  cost_center varchar [note: 'Internal accounting code for allocating workspace infrastructure and operational costs to organizational units.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this workspace was initially created in the system, establishing the start of its lifecycle.']
  destruction_eligible_date date [note: 'Calculated date when workspace contents become eligible for destruction per retention policy, subject to litigation hold overrides.']
  document_management_system varchar [note: 'Source document management system platform where this workspace is hosted or synchronized from.']
  encryption_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicator whether workspace contents are encrypted at rest and in transit, meeting security and compliance requirements.']
  external_collaboration_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicator whether external parties (clients, co-counsel, experts) are permitted to access and collaborate within this workspace.']
  external_system_code varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for this workspace in the source document management system, used for synchronization and integration.']
  jurisdiction varchar [note: 'Primary legal jurisdiction or governing law applicable to this workspace and its contents, influencing compliance requirements.']
  language_code varchar [note: 'ISO 639-1 two-letter code representing the primary language of documents and communications within this workspace.']
  last_accessed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when any user last accessed or viewed content within this workspace, used for activity monitoring.']
  litigation_hold_flag boolean [note: 'Indicator that workspace contents are subject to litigation hold, suspending normal retention and destruction processes.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this workspace metadata was last modified, used for change tracking and synchronization.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-form text field for administrative notes, special instructions, or contextual information about the workspace.']
  opened_date date [note: 'Business date when the workspace was officially opened and made available for use, distinct from system creation timestamp.']
  practice_area varchar [note: 'Primary legal practice area or specialty domain that this workspace supports, used for resource allocation and expertise routing.']
  privilege_designation varchar [note: 'Legal privilege classification applied to the workspace, indicating protection from discovery and disclosure requirements.']
  security_classification varchar [note: 'Data classification level governing access controls, encryption requirements, and handling procedures for workspace contents.']
  storage_location varchar [note: 'Physical or logical storage path or container where workspace documents are persisted, including cloud or on-premises designations.']
  total_document_count varchar [note: 'Current count of documents contained within this workspace, used for capacity planning and billing purposes.']
  total_storage_size_mb decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total storage space consumed by all documents in this workspace, measured in megabytes.']
  version_control_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicator whether document versioning and change tracking are enabled for workspace contents, supporting audit trails.']
  workspace_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the workspace purpose, scope, and key objectives or deliverables.']
  workspace_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name or title of the workspace, typically representing a matter, client, or project.']
  workspace_number varchar [note: 'Business-facing unique identifier or code for the workspace, used for external reference and user navigation.']
  workspace_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the workspace indicating its operational availability and access permissions.']
  workspace_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the workspace by its primary purpose or organizational structure within the legal practice.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for workspace. Referenced by workspace_id.'
}

Table document.production_set {
  production_set_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for production_set']
  matter_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the legal matter or case to which this production set belongs.']
  parent_production_set_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on production_set (parent_production_set_id)']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date on which the production set was approved for delivery by the responsible attorney or case manager.']
  approving_attorney_name varchar [note: 'Name of the attorney who approved the production set for delivery.']
  bates_range_end varchar [note: 'Ending Bates number for the production set.']
  bates_range_start varchar [note: 'Starting Bates number for the production set, used for document identification and tracking.']
  confidentiality_designation varchar [note: 'Protective order designation applied to documents in this production set.']
  created_by_user varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the user who created the production set record.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the production set record was first created in the system.']
  custodian_count varchar [note: 'Number of distinct custodians whose documents are included in this production set.']
  data_source_count varchar [note: 'Number of distinct data sources from which documents in this production set were collected.']
  delivery_location varchar [note: 'Physical or digital location where the production set was delivered (e.g., FTP path, address, cloud link).']
  delivery_method varchar [note: 'Method used to deliver the production set to the recipient.']
  document_count varchar [note: 'Total number of documents included in the production set.']
  hash varchar [note: 'Cryptographic hash value for the entire production set, ensuring integrity and chain of custody.']
  hash_algorithm varchar [note: 'Cryptographic hash algorithm used to generate file integrity checksums for the production.']
  load_file_format varchar [note: 'Format of the load file accompanying the production set.']
  metadata_fields_produced varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of metadata fields included in the production load file.']
  modified_by_user varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the user who last modified the production set record.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the production set record was last modified.']
  native_files_included_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether native file formats are included in the production.']
  page_count varchar [note: 'Total number of pages across all documents in the production set.']
  privilege_log_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a privilege log must accompany this production set for withheld documents.']
  producing_party_name varchar [note: 'Name of the party or organization producing the documents.']
  production_date date [note: 'Date on which the production set was delivered or made available to the recipient.']
  production_deadline_date date [note: 'Court-ordered or agreed-upon deadline for delivering this production set.']
  production_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes regarding the production set, including special instructions, issues, or clarifications.']
  production_protocol_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the production protocol or agreement governing this production set.']
  production_set_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name for the production set, typically reflecting the production phase, recipient, or content scope.']
  production_set_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the production set, typically assigned by the eDiscovery platform or case management system. Externally visible reference number.']
  production_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the production set.']
  production_type varchar [note: 'Type of production format delivered to the requesting party.']
  production_volume_number varchar [note: 'Sequential volume number if the production is split across multiple deliveries.']
  quality_control_completed_date date [note: 'Date on which quality control review was completed.']
  quality_control_reviewer_name varchar [note: 'Name of the individual who performed the quality control review.']
  quality_control_status varchar [note: 'Status of quality control review for the production set.']
  recipient_party_name varchar [note: 'Name of the party or organization receiving the production set.']
  redaction_applied_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether redactions have been applied to documents in this production set.']
  total_file_size_gb decimal(12,3) [note: 'Total file size of the production set in gigabytes.']
  total_production_volumes varchar [note: 'Total number of volumes planned for this production set.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for production_set. Referenced by production_set_id.'
}

Table document.family_group {
  family_group_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for family_group']
  esi_custodian_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the primary custodian or document owner associated with this family group.']
  legal_hold_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the active legal hold order or preservation notice applicable to this family group.']
  master_family_group_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the master family group when this family group has been identified as a duplicate or near-duplicate.']
  matter_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the legal matter or case to which this family group is associated.']
  parent_family_group_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on family_group (parent_family_group_id)']
  parent_legal_document_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the primary or parent document within the family group, typically the root email or master document from which other family members derive.']
  processing_batch_id varchar [note: 'Identifier for the processing batch or ingestion run during which the family group was created or updated, supporting data lineage.']
  production_set_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the production set in which this family group was included for disclosure or production to opposing parties.']
  attachment_count varchar [note: 'Number of attachments within the family group, typically associated with email families.']
  chain_of_custody_verified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the chain of custody for the family group has been verified and documented, ensuring forensic integrity.']
  confidentiality_level varchar [note: 'Classification level indicating the sensitivity and access restrictions for documents within the family group.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the family group record was first created in the system.']
  deduplication_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the family group has been identified as unique, duplicate, or near-duplicate during deduplication processing.']
  discovery_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the family group within the eDiscovery workflow, tracking review and production decisions.']
  disposition_eligible_date date [note: 'The calculated date on which the family group becomes eligible for disposition (archival or destruction) based on retention policy.']
  earliest_document_date date [note: 'The earliest creation or sent date among all documents within the family group, establishing the temporal origin of the family.']
  family_group_code varchar [note: 'Business-assigned unique code for the family group used for external reference and reporting.']
  family_group_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name or label for the family group, used for identification and display purposes.']
  family_group_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the family group record within the document management system.']
  family_group_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the family group indicating the nature of the relationship between grouped documents (e.g., email thread, document family, attachment cluster, version set, related correspondence, discovery family).']
  family_relationship_basis varchar [note: 'The criterion or methodology used to establish the family grouping (e.g., attachment relationship, version lineage, email thread, custodian grouping, matter association, privilege log grouping, near-duplicate analysis, conceptual clustering).']
  hash_algorithm varchar [note: 'The cryptographic algorithm used to generate the hash value for the family group.']
  hash_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Cryptographic hash (MD5, SHA-1, or SHA-256) computed across the family group to ensure integrity and support deduplication.']
  latest_document_date date [note: 'The most recent creation or sent date among all documents within the family group, establishing the temporal endpoint of the family.']
  legal_hold_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the family group is currently subject to a legal hold, preventing disposition pending litigation or investigation.']
  member_count varchar [note: 'Total number of documents included in this family group.']
  modified_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or system process that last modified the family group record.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the family group record was last modified.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for capturing additional context, annotations, or special handling instructions related to the family group.']
  privilege_designation varchar [note: 'Legal privilege status assigned to the family group indicating whether documents are protected by attorney-client privilege, work product doctrine, or other legal protections.']
  redaction_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether documents in the family group require redaction of privileged, confidential, or personally identifiable information prior to production.']
  redaction_status varchar [note: 'Current status of redaction processing for the family group.']
  retention_category varchar [note: 'Classification code indicating the records retention schedule applicable to this family group based on document type and regulatory requirements.']
  retention_trigger_date date [note: 'The date from which the retention period begins, typically the date of document creation, matter closure, or regulatory event.']
  review_priority varchar [note: 'Priority level assigned to the family group for review workflow sequencing, based on relevance scoring, custodian importance, or date range.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'The originating document management system or repository from which the family group was extracted (e.g., iManage Work, NetDocuments, Relativity, SharePoint).']
  source_system_identifier varchar [note: 'The native identifier or reference key for the family group in the source system, supporting traceability and reconciliation.']
  technology_assisted_review_score decimal(5,4) [note: 'Predictive coding or continuous active learning (CAL) relevance score assigned to the family group by machine learning algorithms, ranging from 0.0000 to 1.0000.']
  version_count varchar [note: 'Number of distinct versions of documents within the family group, tracking document evolution.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or system process that created the family group record.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for family_group. Referenced by family_group_id.'
}

Table document.review_batch {
  review_batch_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for review_batch']
  assigned_team_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the review team responsible for this batch.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the legal professional or reviewer assigned to this batch.']
  parent_review_batch_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on review_batch (parent_review_batch_id)']
  primary_matter_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the legal matter this review batch is associated with.']
  review_case_matter_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the litigation case or investigation this review batch supports.']
  review_created_by_user_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the user who created this review batch.']
  review_last_modified_by_user_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the user who last modified this review batch record.']
  review_protocol_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the review protocol or guidelines governing how documents in this batch should be reviewed.']
  tar_model_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the TAR or predictive coding model used to prioritize or select documents for this batch.']
  actual_completion_date date [note: 'Actual date when review of this batch was completed.']
  actual_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'Actual number of hours spent reviewing this batch.']
  assigned_date date [note: 'Date when the batch was assigned to a reviewer or team.']
  batch_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name for the review batch, often indicating the document set or review focus area.']
  batch_number varchar [note: 'Business-facing unique identifier for the review batch, typically used in communications and reporting.']
  batch_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the review batch indicating the review methodology or purpose (e.g., first pass review, quality control, Technology Assisted Review seed set, Continuous Active Learning training).']
  cal_round_number varchar [note: 'The CAL training round number this batch belongs to, if part of an iterative machine learning review workflow.']
  completion_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time when the batch review was marked as complete.']
  confidentiality_level varchar [note: 'Data classification level assigned to this review batch based on the sensitivity of documents it contains.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the review batch was created in the system.']
  custodian_list varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of custodians whose documents are included in this batch.']
  date_range_end date [note: 'Latest document date included in this review batch, defining the temporal scope.']
  date_range_start date [note: 'Earliest document date included in this review batch, defining the temporal scope.']
  document_count varchar [note: 'Total number of documents included in this review batch.']
  estimated_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'Estimated number of hours required to complete review of this batch.']
  hot_document_count varchar [note: 'Number of documents flagged as hot or key documents requiring special attention or escalation.']
  is_billable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the review work on this batch is billable to the client.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the review batch record was last updated.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments about this review batch, including special instructions or observations.']
  page_count varchar [note: 'Total number of pages across all documents in this review batch.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Business priority assigned to this review batch, influencing resource allocation and scheduling.']
  privileged_document_count varchar [note: 'Number of documents designated as privileged (attorney-client privilege, work product, etc.) within this batch.']
  quality_control_pass_count varchar [note: 'Number of documents that passed quality control review without discrepancies.']
  quality_control_sample_size varchar [note: 'Number of documents selected for quality control review from this batch.']
  responsive_document_count varchar [note: 'Number of documents coded as responsive to the matter or case within this batch.']
  review_batch_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the review batch.']
  review_platform varchar [note: 'The eDiscovery or document review platform where this batch is hosted and reviewed.']
  review_rate_documents_per_hour decimal(8,2) [note: 'Average number of documents reviewed per hour for this batch, used for productivity tracking.']
  reviewed_document_count varchar [note: 'Number of documents that have been reviewed within this batch.']
  search_terms_applied varchar [note: 'Search terms or queries used to identify and populate documents in this batch.']
  start_date date [note: 'Date when review work on this batch commenced.']
  target_completion_date date [note: 'Planned or required date by which this batch review should be completed.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for review_batch. Referenced by review_batch_id.'
}

Table document.production_batch {
  production_batch_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for production_batch']
  matter_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the legal matter or case to which this production batch belongs.']
  parent_production_batch_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on production_batch (parent_production_batch_id)']
  production_specification_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the production specification or protocol document that defines the technical and legal requirements for this production batch.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date on which this production batch received formal approval for delivery.']
  approval_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether formal approval from counsel or client is required before this production batch can be delivered.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name of the attorney or authorized individual who approved this production batch for delivery.']
  batch_number varchar [note: 'Business-facing unique identifier or reference number assigned to the production batch for external communication and tracking.']
  bates_prefix varchar [note: 'Alphanumeric prefix applied to Bates numbers in this production batch, typically identifying the producing party or matter.']
  bates_range_end varchar [note: 'The ending Bates number or document control number for this production batch, establishing the end of the sequential numbering range.']
  bates_range_start varchar [note: 'The starting Bates number or document control number for this production batch, establishing the beginning of the sequential numbering range.']
  confidentiality_designation varchar [note: 'Protective order designation applied to the production batch indicating the level of confidentiality and access restrictions.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this production batch record was first created in the system.']
  custodian_count varchar [note: 'Number of distinct custodians whose documents are included in this production batch.']
  date_range_end date [note: 'Latest document date among all documents included in this production batch, defining the temporal scope of the production.']
  date_range_start date [note: 'Earliest document date among all documents included in this production batch, defining the temporal scope of the production.']
  delivery_date date [note: 'The date on which the production batch was delivered or transmitted to the requesting party or recipient.']
  delivery_method varchar [note: 'The mechanism or channel used to deliver the production batch to the recipient (secure FTP, USB drive, cloud link, email, API integration, or web portal).']
  document_count varchar [note: 'Total number of documents included in this production batch.']
  encryption_applied boolean [note: 'Indicates whether encryption was applied to the production batch files for secure transmission and storage.']
  encryption_method varchar [note: 'Cryptographic algorithm or method used to encrypt the production batch (e.g., AES-256, RSA, PGP).']
  hash_algorithm varchar [note: 'Cryptographic hash algorithm used to generate file integrity checksums for documents in this production batch.']
  image_format varchar [note: 'Image file format used for document renderings in this production batch (single-page TIFF, multi-page PDF, or other image formats).']
  load_file_format varchar [note: 'Format specification for the metadata load file accompanying this production batch (DAT, OPT, LFP, CSV, or platform-specific format).']
  media_type varchar [note: 'Physical or digital media type used to deliver the production batch.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this production batch record was last modified or updated.']
  native_file_count varchar [note: 'Number of documents produced in their native file format within this batch.']
  page_count varchar [note: 'Total number of pages across all documents in this production batch.']
  privileged_document_count varchar [note: 'Number of documents withheld from production due to attorney-client privilege, work product doctrine, or other legal privilege.']
  producing_party varchar [note: 'Name of the party or organization producing and delivering this batch of documents.']
  production_date date [note: 'The date on which the production batch was officially produced or finalized for delivery to the requesting party.']
  production_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes documenting special instructions, exceptions, issues, or other relevant information about this production batch.']
  production_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the production batch indicating its stage in the production workflow.']
  production_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the production batch format and content type (native files, TIFF images, load file only, metadata extract, redacted set, or privilege log).']
  quality_control_date date [note: 'Date on which quality control validation was completed for this production batch.']
  quality_control_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes documenting quality control findings, issues identified, and remediation actions taken for this production batch.']
  quality_control_performed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether quality control validation was performed on this production batch prior to delivery.']
  redacted_document_count varchar [note: 'Number of documents in this batch that contain redactions for privilege, confidentiality, or privacy protection.']
  requesting_party varchar [note: 'Name of the party, organization, or entity that requested this production batch (opposing counsel, regulatory agency, or internal stakeholder).']
  review_platform varchar [note: 'Name of the eDiscovery or document review platform used to prepare and export this production batch (e.g., Relativity, Everlaw, Logikcull).']
  text_extraction_method varchar [note: 'Method used to extract searchable text from documents in this batch (native text extraction, OCR processing, hybrid approach, or no text extraction).']
  total_size_bytes varchar [note: 'Total storage size of all files in the production batch measured in bytes.']
  tracking_number varchar [note: 'Shipment or transmission tracking number for physical or electronic delivery of the production batch.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for production_batch. Referenced by production_batch_id.'
}

Table document.tar_model {
  tar_model_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for tar_model']
  matter_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the legal matter or case for which this TAR model was developed and is being applied.']
  seed_tar_model_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on tar_model (seed_tar_model_id)']
  accuracy_score decimal(5,4) [note: 'Overall proportion of correct classifications (both relevant and non-relevant) made by the model, expressed as a decimal between 0 and 1.']
  algorithm_type varchar [note: 'The specific machine learning algorithm employed by the TAR model for document classification and relevance prediction.']
  confidence_interval decimal(5,4) [note: 'Statistical confidence level for the models performance metrics, typically expressed as a percentage (e.g., 0.95 for 95% confidence).']
  control_set_size varchar [note: 'Number of documents in the control set used for ongoing validation and quality assurance of model performance.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the TAR model record was first created in the system.']
  cutoff_threshold decimal(5,4) [note: 'The relevance score threshold above which documents are classified as relevant for review or production, expressed as a decimal between 0 and 1.']
  defensibility_documentation_url varchar [note: 'URL reference to the comprehensive defensibility documentation package supporting the TAR models methodology and results for potential court challenges.']
  deployment_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the TAR model was deployed to production and made available for document classification.']
  deprecation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the TAR model was deprecated and removed from active use.']
  documents_classified varchar [note: 'Total number of documents that have been scored and classified by this TAR model during its operational use.']
  documents_reviewed varchar [note: 'Total number of documents that have undergone human review as part of the TAR workflow, including training and quality control.']
  f1_score decimal(5,4) [note: 'Harmonic mean of precision and recall providing a single balanced measure of model performance, expressed as a decimal between 0 and 1.']
  feature_engineering_approach varchar [note: 'Description of the feature extraction and engineering methodology applied to prepare document data for model training.']
  language_support varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of languages supported by the TAR model for multilingual document collections.']
  last_modified_by varchar [note: 'Name of the user who most recently modified the TAR model configuration or parameters.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the TAR model record was most recently updated or modified.']
  last_training_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the most recent training round was completed for this TAR model.']
  model_code varchar [note: 'Unique business identifier code for the TAR model following the pattern TAR-XXXXXX for external reference and tracking.']
  model_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name assigned to the TAR model for identification and reference purposes.']
  model_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the TAR methodology employed, distinguishing between TAR 1.0 (traditional predictive coding), TAR 2.0 (continuous active learning), and other machine learning approaches.']
  model_version varchar [note: 'Semantic version number of the TAR model following the pattern vX.Y.Z to track iterations and updates.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for capturing additional context, observations, or special considerations related to the TAR models development and use.']
  precision_score decimal(5,4) [note: 'Statistical measure of the proportion of documents classified as relevant by the model that are actually relevant, expressed as a decimal between 0 and 1.']
  privilege_detection_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicator of whether the TAR model includes specialized detection capabilities for attorney-client privilege and work product protection.']
  recall_score decimal(5,4) [note: 'Statistical measure of the proportion of actually relevant documents that were correctly identified by the model, expressed as a decimal between 0 and 1.']
  review_platform varchar [note: 'The eDiscovery software platform in which the TAR model is deployed and executed for document review.']
  richness_estimate decimal(5,4) [note: 'Estimated proportion of relevant documents within the entire document collection, used for sampling and review planning.']
  seed_set_strategy varchar [note: 'The methodology used to select the initial seed documents for model training, balancing representativeness and efficiency.']
  stabilization_achieved boolean [note: 'Indicator of whether the TAR model has reached a stable state where additional training produces minimal performance improvement.']
  tar_model_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the TAR model indicating its operational readiness and usage authorization.']
  training_rounds_completed varchar [note: 'Number of iterative training cycles completed in the development and refinement of the TAR model.']
  training_set_size varchar [note: 'Total number of documents used in the training dataset to build and refine the TAR models classification capabilities.']
  validated_by varchar [note: 'Name of the senior attorney or eDiscovery expert who performed and certified the model validation.']
  validation_date date [note: 'Date on which the TAR model successfully completed validation testing and was certified for production use.']
  validation_method varchar [note: 'The statistical validation approach used to assess and verify the TAR models performance and reliability.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'Name of the eDiscovery specialist or data scientist who created and configured the TAR model.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for tar_model. Referenced by tar_model_id.'
}

Table document.review_project {
  review_project_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for review_project']
  matter_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent legal matter or case to which this review project belongs.']
  parent_review_project_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on review_project (parent_review_project_id)']
  actual_cost_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Cumulative actual costs incurred for the review project to date, including all labor and technology expenses.']
  chain_of_custody_maintained boolean [note: 'Indicates whether formal chain of custody documentation has been maintained for evidentiary integrity and admissibility.']
  confidentiality_level varchar [note: 'Classification of the sensitivity and access restrictions for the review project and its documents.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the review project record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts in this review project (e.g., USD, GBP, EUR).']
  custodian_count varchar [note: 'Number of data custodians (individuals whose documents are being reviewed) included in the project scope.']
  data_source_count varchar [note: 'Number of distinct data sources or repositories from which documents were collected for review (e.g., email servers, file shares, databases).']
  deduplication_applied boolean [note: 'Indicates whether deduplication processing was applied to remove exact duplicate documents from the review set.']
  email_threading_applied boolean [note: 'Indicates whether email threading analysis was applied to identify and group related email conversations.']
  estimated_budget_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Projected total cost for completing the review project, including attorney time, technology costs, and vendor fees.']
  jurisdiction varchar [note: 'Primary legal jurisdiction or country code governing the review project and its legal obligations.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the review project record was last updated or modified.']
  nlp_processing_applied boolean [note: 'Indicates whether Natural Language Processing or advanced analytics were applied for concept extraction or sentiment analysis.']
  ocr_processing_applied boolean [note: 'Indicates whether Optical Character Recognition was applied to extract text from image-based documents.']
  privilege_protocol_applied boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a formal privilege review protocol or agreement has been applied to this project.']
  privileged_document_count varchar [note: 'Number of documents designated as privileged or protected under attorney-client privilege or work product doctrine.']
  production_deadline_date date [note: 'Court-ordered or agreed-upon deadline for producing responsive documents to opposing counsel or regulatory authority.']
  production_format varchar [note: 'Format in which responsive documents will be produced to requesting party (e.g., native files, TIFF images, PDF, or load files).']
  project_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the review project scope, objectives, and special considerations.']
  project_end_date date [note: 'Date when the review project was completed or is scheduled to be completed. Nullable for ongoing projects.']
  project_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name or title of the review project, typically reflecting the matter or case name.']
  project_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique business identifier for the review project, used for client communication and billing.']
  project_start_date date [note: 'Date when the review project officially commenced or was initiated.']
  project_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the review project indicating its operational state.']
  project_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the review project based on its legal purpose and workflow requirements.']
  quality_control_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of reviewed documents subject to secondary quality control review, typically ranging from 5% to 100%.']
  redaction_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether document redaction is required to protect privileged information or personally identifiable information (PII) before production.']
  responsive_document_count varchar [note: 'Number of documents identified as responsive to the discovery request or review criteria.']
  review_methodology varchar [note: 'Approach or technique used for document review, such as linear review, Technology Assisted Review (TAR), Continuous Active Learning (CAL), or hybrid methods.']
  review_platform varchar [note: 'Technology platform or system used to conduct the document review (e.g., Relativity, Nuix, Everlaw).']
  reviewed_document_count varchar [note: 'Number of documents that have been reviewed and coded by reviewers as of the current date.']
  special_instructions varchar [note: 'Additional instructions, protocols, or requirements specific to this review project that reviewers must follow.']
  target_completion_date date [note: 'Planned or contractual deadline for completing the review project, used for scheduling and resource planning.']
  total_data_volume_gb decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total volume of electronically stored information (ESI) collected for the review project, measured in gigabytes.']
  total_document_count varchar [note: 'Total number of documents or electronically stored information (ESI) items included in the review project scope.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for review_project. Referenced by project_id.'
}

Table document.production_specification {
  production_specification_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for production_specification']
  modified_by_user_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key to workforce.user.user_id']
  parent_production_specification_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on production_specification (parent_production_specification_id)']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the eDiscovery vendor or service provider responsible for executing this production specification.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date on which the production specification was formally approved by all parties or the court.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the attorney, counsel, or court officer who approved this production specification.']
  bates_numbering_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether Bates numbering (sequential document identification) is required for this production.']
  bates_prefix varchar [note: 'Prefix to be applied to Bates numbers for this production, typically identifying the producing party or matter.']
  bates_starting_number varchar [note: 'Starting sequential number for Bates numbering in this production specification.']
  clawback_provision_applies boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a clawback provision allowing retrieval of inadvertently produced privileged documents is in effect.']
  confidentiality_designation varchar [note: 'Default confidentiality designation to be applied to documents in this production per protective order terms.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this production specification record was first created in the system.']
  custodian_scope varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of custodian names or identifiers whose documents are included in this production specification.']
  date_range_end date [note: 'End date of the temporal scope for documents included in this production specification.']
  date_range_start date [note: 'Start date of the temporal scope for documents included in this production specification.']
  deduplication_scope varchar [note: 'Scope of deduplication to be applied: global (across all custodians), per custodian, family-level (email threads), or none.']
  delivery_method varchar [note: 'Method by which the production will be delivered to the requesting party.']
  encryption_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether encryption is required for production delivery to protect confidential information in transit.']
  family_relationship_preserved boolean [note: 'Indicates whether parent-child document relationships (e.g., email and attachments) must be preserved in the production.']
  hash_validation_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether cryptographic hash values (MD5, SHA-256) must be provided for file integrity validation.']
  load_file_format varchar [note: 'Format of the load file to accompany the production, specifying how metadata and document references are structured.']
  metadata_fields_included varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of metadata fields to be included in the production load file (e.g., author, created date, modified date, custodian).']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this production specification record was last modified.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes capturing additional instructions, special handling requirements, or context for this production specification.']
  privilege_log_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a privilege log documenting withheld privileged documents must be produced.']
  producing_party varchar [note: 'Name of the party or organization producing documents under this specification.']
  production_cost_estimate decimal(15,2) [note: 'Estimated total cost in USD for completing the document production under this specification, including review, processing, and delivery.']
  production_deadline date [note: 'Court-ordered or agreed-upon deadline by which the production must be completed and delivered.']
  production_format varchar [note: 'Detailed format specification for document production delivery, including image type and text extraction requirements.']
  production_type varchar [note: 'Type of document production format specified: native files, TIFF images, PDF, load file only, paper, or hybrid combination.']
  production_volume_estimate varchar [note: 'Estimated number of document pages or files to be produced under this specification.']
  quality_control_sampling_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of documents subject to quality control review to ensure production accuracy and completeness.']
  redaction_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether document redaction for privileged or confidential information is required for this production.']
  requesting_party varchar [note: 'Name of the party or organization requesting the document production.']
