  affected_matter_count varchar [note: 'Number of legal matters or cases impacted by the breach. Relevant for assessing operational and reputational impact.']
  breach_category varchar [note: 'High-level categorization of the breach by business domain: financial controls, data privacy, professional conduct, operational compliance, regulatory reporting, or client funds management.']
  breach_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the breach, including what occurred, which controls failed, and the scope of the violation. Confidential business information.']
  breach_reference_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique reference number assigned to the breach for tracking and reporting purposes. Format: BRH-YYYYNNNN.']
  breach_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the breach record. Tracks progression from identification through investigation, remediation, and closure.']
  breach_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the regulatory breach by the governing framework or regulation violated. AML = Anti-Money Laundering, KYC = Know Your Client, GDPR = General Data Protection Regulation, CCPA = California Consumer Privacy Act, DPA = Data Protection Act, SOX = Sarbanes-Oxley Act, SRA = Solicitors Regulation Authority, IOLTA = Interest on Lawyers Trust Accounts, LEDES = Legal Electronic Data Exchange Standard. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: AML|KYC|GDPR|CCPA|DPA|SOX|SRA_CONDUCT|PROFESSIONAL_INDEMNITY|IOLTA|LEDES — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  client_notification_date date [note: 'Date on which affected clients were notified of the breach. Null if notification not yet completed or not required.']
  client_notification_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether affected clients must be directly notified of the breach under GDPR, CCPA, or professional conduct obligations. True = client notification required.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this breach record was first created in the system. Audit trail field.']
  discovery_date date [note: 'Date on which the breach was first identified or discovered by the firm. Critical for determining notification timelines under GDPR (72 hours) and other regulatory frameworks.']
  discovery_method varchar [note: 'Mechanism or channel through which the breach was identified. Used to assess effectiveness of internal controls and detection systems. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: INTERNAL_AUDIT|EXTERNAL_AUDIT|SELF_REPORTED|CLIENT_COMPLAINT|REGULATOR_INSPECTION|SYSTEM_ALERT|WHISTLEBLOWER|THIRD_PARTY_NOTIFICATION — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  dpo_notification_date timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the Data Protection Officer was notified of the breach. Null if DPO notification not applicable or not yet completed.']
  dpo_notified_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the firms Data Protection Officer was notified of the breach. Required under GDPR for data privacy breaches. True = DPO notified.']
  escalated_to_executive_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the breach was escalated to executive leadership (e.g., Managing Partner, General Counsel, Chief Legal Officer). True = executive escalation occurred.']
  escalation_date timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the breach was escalated to executive leadership. Null if no escalation occurred.']
  financial_impact_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Estimated or actual financial cost of the breach to the firm, including fines, penalties, remediation costs, legal fees, and reputational damage. Confidential business information.']
  financial_impact_currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the financial impact amount (e.g., USD, GBP, EUR).']
  impact_assessment varchar [note: 'Documented assessment of the breach impact on clients, firm operations, regulatory standing, and reputation. Includes scope of affected parties and potential harm.']
  is_active boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this breach record is currently active and under management. False = breach has been closed or archived.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this breach record was last updated. Audit trail field for tracking changes over the breach lifecycle.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional free-text notes, observations, or context related to the breach. May include investigator comments, follow-up actions, or lessons learned.']
  notified_regulator varchar [note: 'Name of the regulatory body or supervisory authority to which the breach was reported (e.g., ICO, SRA, State Bar, SEC).']
  occurrence_date date [note: 'Actual or estimated date when the breach occurred. May differ from discovery date if the breach was historical or latent.']
  professional_indemnity_claim_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the breach has triggered or may trigger a professional indemnity insurance claim. True = claim filed or anticipated.']
  professional_indemnity_claim_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number for the professional indemnity insurance claim associated with this breach. Null if no claim filed.']
  regulator_reference_number varchar [note: 'Reference or case number assigned by the regulatory authority upon receipt of the breach notification. Used for tracking regulatory correspondence and follow-up.']
  regulatory_notification_date timestamp [note: 'Actual date and time when the breach was formally notified to the relevant regulatory authority. Null if notification not yet completed.']
  regulatory_notification_deadline timestamp [note: 'Deadline by which the breach must be reported to the regulator. GDPR requires notification within 72 hours of discovery; other frameworks have varying timelines.']
  regulatory_notification_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the breach must be reported to a regulatory authority under applicable law or professional conduct rules. True = notification required.']
  regulatory_penalty_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Actual or potential fine or penalty imposed by the regulatory authority as a result of the breach. Null if no penalty assessed or not yet determined.']
  regulatory_penalty_currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the regulatory penalty amount.']
  remediation_owner varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the individual or department responsible for executing the remediation plan and ensuring corrective actions are completed.']
  remediation_owner_department varchar [note: 'Department or business unit to which the remediation owner belongs (e.g., Compliance, Risk, IT, Finance, Legal Operations).']
  remediation_plan varchar [note: 'Documented plan outlining corrective actions, control enhancements, and process changes to address the breach and prevent recurrence. Confidential business information.']
  remediation_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the remediation effort. Tracks progress toward completion of corrective actions.']
  root_cause varchar [note: 'Identified underlying cause of the breach following investigation. May include process failure, system error, human error, inadequate training, or control gap.']
  severity_rating varchar [note: 'Assessment of the breach severity based on impact to the firm, clients, and regulatory standing. Critical = immediate regulatory notification and senior leadership escalation required.']
  target_remediation_date date [note: 'Planned or committed date by which all remediation actions will be completed and the breach fully resolved.']

  Note: 'Records of identified compliance breaches or regulatory violations at the firm level, distinct from data breaches (which are privacy-specific). Captures breach type (AML, SRA conduct, SOX, GDPR, professional indemnity), discovery date, root cause, severity rating, regulatory notification obligation flag, notification date, remediation plan, remediation owner, target resolution date, and actual resolution date. Feeds into the firms regulatory risk posture and professional indemnity insurance disclosures.'
}

Table compliance.indemnity_policy {
  indemnity_policy_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the professional indemnity insurance policy record.']
  practice_area_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key to service.practice_area.practice_area_id']
  annual_premium_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The total annual premium paid or payable for this professional indemnity insurance policy.']
  broker_contact_email varchar [note: 'Primary email address for the insurance broker handling this policy.']
  broker_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Primary telephone number for the insurance broker handling this policy.']
  broker_name varchar [note: 'Name of the insurance broker or intermediary who arranged the professional indemnity policy.']
  claims_made_basis_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the policy operates on a claims-made basis (true) or occurrence basis (false), determining when coverage is triggered.']
  claims_notification_email varchar [note: 'Email address to which claims and potential claims must be notified under the terms of this policy.']
  claims_notification_phone varchar [note: 'Telephone number for notifying claims and potential claims to the insurer.']
  compliance_certificate_issued_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a certificate of compliance or certificate of insurance has been issued confirming the policy meets regulatory minimum requirements.']
  coverage_period_end_date date [note: 'The date on which the professional indemnity insurance coverage expires or terminates.']
  coverage_period_start_date date [note: 'The date on which the professional indemnity insurance coverage becomes effective and binding.']
  coverage_territory varchar [note: 'Geographic scope of coverage defining where claims arising from professional services will be covered (e.g., worldwide, UK only, EU, excluding USA).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this professional indemnity policy record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts in this policy (limits, excess, premium).']
  excess_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The deductible or self-insured retention amount that the firm must pay before the insurers coverage applies to a claim.']
  exclusions_summary varchar [note: 'Summary of key exclusions, limitations, and circumstances under which coverage does not apply.']
  extended_reporting_period_months varchar [note: 'Number of months of extended reporting period (tail coverage) included in the policy, allowing claims to be reported after the policy expiration date for incidents that occurred during the coverage period.']
  insurer_name varchar [note: 'Legal name of the insurance company providing the professional indemnity coverage.']
  insurer_reference_number varchar [note: 'Internal reference number assigned by the insurer for tracking and correspondence purposes.']
  is_active boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this policy record is currently active and in force.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this professional indemnity policy record was last updated or modified.']
  last_review_date date [note: 'The date on which this policy was last reviewed for adequacy of coverage, compliance with regulatory requirements, and alignment with firm risk profile.']
  limit_of_indemnity_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The maximum amount the insurer will pay for all claims and associated costs during the policy period, representing the aggregate coverage limit.']
  limit_per_claim_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The maximum amount the insurer will pay for any single claim or related series of claims, representing the per-occurrence limit.']
  next_premium_due_date date [note: 'The date on which the next premium payment is due for this policy.']
  next_review_date date [note: 'The scheduled date for the next review of this policys adequacy and compliance.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional notes, comments, or special conditions related to this professional indemnity insurance policy.']
  policy_document_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier or document management system location for the full policy wording, schedule, and endorsements.']
  policy_number varchar [note: 'The externally-known unique policy number assigned by the insurer for this professional indemnity insurance policy.']
  policy_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the professional indemnity insurance policy.']
  policy_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the professional indemnity policy indicating whether it is primary coverage, excess layer, run-off coverage for closed practices, top-up coverage, or successor practice coverage.']
  practice_areas_covered varchar [note: 'Description of the legal practice areas and service types covered under this professional indemnity policy.']
  premium_payment_frequency varchar [note: 'The frequency at which premium payments are made for this policy.']
  premium_payment_status varchar [note: 'Current status of premium payment obligations for this policy period.']
  qualifying_insurer_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the insurer is a qualifying insurer as defined by the SRA or relevant regulatory authority, meeting minimum financial strength and regulatory requirements.']
  regulatory_filing_date date [note: 'The date on which the policy details were filed with or notified to the regulatory authority.']
  regulatory_filing_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number or identifier for the regulatory filing or notification submitted to the SRA or other regulatory authority confirming this policy meets minimum requirements.']
  renewal_date date [note: 'The date on which this policy is scheduled for renewal or replacement with a successor policy.']
  renewal_notice_sent_date date [note: 'The date on which the renewal notice was sent to the firm by the insurer or broker.']
  responsible_partner_name varchar [note: 'Name of the partner or senior officer responsible for managing this professional indemnity insurance policy and ensuring regulatory compliance.']
  retroactive_date date [note: 'The earliest date from which claims arising from professional services will be covered under this policy, establishing the backward coverage limit for claims-made policies.']

  Note: 'Professional indemnity insurance records covering both policy master data and claims lifecycle, as required by SRA, Law Society, and other bar regulators. Policy attributes include insurer, policy number, coverage period, limits, excess, retroactive date, qualifying insurer status, and premium. Claims attributes include notification date, claimant details, matter reference, alleged negligence, exposure estimates, insurer acknowledgement, reserve and settlement amounts, and closure status. Provides the unified PI compliance artifact for regulatory reporting and premium renewal actuarial analysis.'
}

Table compliance.indemnity_claim {
  indemnity_claim_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the professional indemnity insurance claim record. Primary key for the indemnity claim entity.']
  attorney_profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the partner who had primary responsibility for the matter at the time of the alleged negligence.']
  indemnity_policy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.indemnity_policy. Business justification: Claims are made UNDER a policy. N:1 relationship (many claims per policy). indemnity_claim has policy_number and policy_year attributes that reference indemnity_policy. Adding FK resolves silo for ind']
  legal_document_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to document.legal_document. Business justification: Claim notifications, coverage opinions, settlement agreements are legal documents: insurer notification letter, coverage counsel opinion, settlement deed, SRA report of claim. Required for linking ind']
  matter_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the matter that is the subject of the alleged negligence or professional liability claim.']
  practice_area_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to service.practice_area. Business justification: Professional indemnity claims arise from practice-area-specific work. Underwriters price coverage by practice area risk profile. Essential for practice-area-level claims analysis, reserve setting, and']
  primary_indemnity_timekeeper_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the fee earner (associate, solicitor, or other timekeeper) who performed the work that is the subject of the alleged negligence.']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the client who is the claimant or on whose matter the alleged negligence occurred.']
  alleged_negligence_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the alleged professional negligence, error, omission, or breach of duty that forms the basis of the claim. Includes circumstances, timeline, and alleged impact.']
  claim_closure_date date [note: 'Date on which the claim was formally closed by the insurer and the firm, either through settlement, withdrawal, or dismissal.']
  claim_reference_number varchar [note: 'External claim reference number assigned by the insurer or the firms risk management system. Used for correspondence with insurers and tracking across systems.']
  claim_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the indemnity claim. Tracks progression from initial notification through investigation, settlement, and closure. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: notified|acknowledged|investigating|reserved|settled|closed|withdrawn — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  claim_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the nature of the professional indemnity claim based on the type of alleged error or omission. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: negligence|breach_of_duty|conflict_of_interest|missed_deadline|inadequate_advice|document_error|fraud|other — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  claimant_contact_email varchar [note: 'Primary email address for correspondence with the claimant or their legal representative.']
  claimant_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Primary telephone number for the claimant or their legal representative.']
  claimant_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the individual or organization making the claim against the firm. May be the client or a third party affected by the alleged negligence.']
  claimant_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the claimants relationship to the firm and the matter in question.']
  claims_handler_name varchar [note: 'Name of the individual at the insurer or third-party administrator responsible for managing this claim.']
  closure_reason varchar [note: 'Reason for closing the claim, indicating the final outcome or disposition.']
  coverage_confirmation_date date [note: 'Date on which the insurer confirmed or denied coverage for the claim.']
  coverage_confirmation_status varchar [note: 'Status of the insurers determination regarding whether the claim is covered under the professional indemnity policy.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this indemnity claim record was first created in the system.']
  defence_costs_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total costs incurred in defending the claim, including legal fees, expert witness fees, and other litigation expenses. May be covered by the insurer depending on policy terms.']
  defence_costs_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the defence costs amount.']
  estimated_exposure_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Initial estimate of the firms potential financial exposure or liability for this claim, including damages, costs, and interest. Updated as the claim progresses.']
  estimated_exposure_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the estimated exposure amount.']
  incident_date date [note: 'Date on which the alleged negligent act, error, or omission occurred or was discovered.']
  insurer_acknowledgement_date date [note: 'Date on which the insurer formally acknowledged receipt of the claim notification.']
  insurer_name varchar [note: 'Name of the professional indemnity insurance carrier to whom the claim has been notified.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this indemnity claim record was last updated in the system.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional notes, observations, or context regarding the claim, its handling, or its resolution.']
  notification_date date [note: 'Date on which the claim was first notified to the firms risk management or compliance team. Critical for SRA reporting and policy coverage determination.']
  policy_year varchar [note: 'Insurance policy year during which the claim was notified or the incident occurred, depending on policy terms (claims-made vs occurrence basis).']
  reserve_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Financial reserve amount set aside by the insurer or the firm to cover the anticipated cost of settling or defending the claim. Updated as the claim progresses.']
  reserve_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the reserve amount.']
  reserve_last_updated_date date [note: 'Date on which the reserve amount was last reviewed and updated by the insurer or claims handler.']
  risk_rating varchar [note: 'Assessment of the risk level and potential impact of this claim on the firms reputation, finances, and regulatory standing.']
  settlement_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Final settlement amount paid to resolve the claim, including damages, costs, and interest. Null if the claim has not been settled.']
  settlement_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the settlement amount.']
  settlement_date date [note: 'Date on which the claim was formally settled and payment was made or agreed.']
  sra_report_date date [note: 'Date on which the claim was reported to the Solicitors Regulation Authority, if applicable.']
  sra_reportable_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this claim meets the threshold for mandatory reporting to the Solicitors Regulation Authority under professional indemnity insurance rules.']

  Note: 'Professional indemnity insurance claims made against or notified under the firms indemnity policy. Captures claim reference, notification date, claimant details, matter reference, alleged negligence description, estimated exposure, insurer acknowledgement date, coverage confirmation status, reserve amount, settlement amount, and claim closure date. Supports SRA compliance obligations and actuarial reporting for premium renewal.'
}

Table compliance.training_programme {

  compliance_policy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.policy. Business justification: Training programmes implement and educate on compliance policies. N:1 relationship (many programmes per policy). training_programme has related_policy_reference (STRING) which should be formalized as ']
  content_owner_timekeeper_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.timekeeper. Business justification: Compliance training programmes require a named timekeeper as content owner for accountability, version control, and regulatory audit trails. Legal firms track who is responsible for maintaining each c']
  knowledge_asset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to knowledge.knowledge_asset. Business justification: Training programmes reference knowledge assets (precedents, practice notes, research memos) as learning materials. This link enables training content management and tracks which knowledge assets suppo']
  practice_area_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to service.practice_area. Business justification: CPD training is practice-area-specific (IP training for patent attorneys, AML training for corporate). Regulatory bodies require practice-area-aligned training. Essential for CPD compliance tracking a']
  practice_group_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.practice_group. Business justification: Practice-specific compliance training (AML for corporate, GDPR for tech practice, conflicts for litigation) requires linking training programmes to practice groups for targeted delivery and CPD tracki']
  primary_regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'FK to compliance.regulatory_obligation.regulatory_obligation_id — Training programmes exist to satisfy specific regulatory obligations (e.g., FATF requires AML training, GDPR requires privacy awareness). This FK enables compliance officers to demonstrate obligation ']
  accreditation_body varchar [note: 'Name of the professional body or regulatory authority that has accredited or approved this training programme (e.g., SRA, Law Society, ABA, IBA).']
  accreditation_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number or identifier assigned by the accreditation body for this training programme.']
  assessment_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether participants must complete a formal assessment or examination to pass the training programme.']
  certificate_issued_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a completion certificate is issued to participants who successfully complete the training.']
  cost_currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the cost per participant (e.g., GBP, USD, EUR).']
  cost_per_participant decimal(10,2) [note: 'Cost charged per participant for completing the training programme, used for budget planning and cost allocation. Null for internally developed free training.']
  cpd_credits decimal(5,2) [note: 'Number of CPD or CLE credits awarded upon successful completion of the training programme.']

  delivery_format varchar [note: 'Format in which the training is made available to participants, distinct from delivery method.']

  frequency varchar [note: 'Required frequency at which participants must complete or refresh this training programme.']
  internal_system_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier or course code in the internal Learning Management System (LMS) or Human Resources Management (HRM) system.']
  is_active boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the training programme is currently active and available for enrollment and completion.']
  language varchar [note: 'Primary language in which the training programme content is delivered, using ISO 639-2 three-letter language codes (e.g., ENG for English, FRA for French).']
  last_content_update_date date [note: 'Date when the training programme content was last substantively updated or revised.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this training programme record was last modified or updated.']
  launch_date date [note: 'Date when the training programme was first made available to participants.']
  next_review_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next content review and update cycle to ensure continued regulatory compliance and relevance.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional free-text notes, comments, or special instructions related to the training programme administration or content.']
  pass_mark_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Minimum percentage score required to successfully complete the training programme and receive credit. Null if no assessment is required.']
  practice_group_scope varchar [note: 'Specific practice groups or departments for which this training is mandatory or recommended (e.g., Corporate, Litigation, IP, Employment). Null if applicable to all practice groups.']
  prerequisite_programmes varchar [note: 'List or description of other training programmes that must be completed before participants can enroll in this programme. Null if no prerequisites exist.']
  programme_code varchar [note: 'Unique business identifier code for the training programme, used for external reference and system integration.']
  programme_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the training programme content, learning objectives, and scope.']
  programme_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the compliance training programme as displayed to users and in reporting.']
  programme_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the training programme indicating its availability and operational state.']
  provider_name varchar [note: 'Name of the external training provider or vendor if the programme is delivered by a third party. Null for internally developed training.']
  recurrence_interval_months varchar [note: 'Number of months between required completions for recurring training programmes. Null for one-time training.']
  regulatory_framework varchar [note: 'Primary regulatory framework or compliance area that this training programme addresses (e.g., Anti-Money Laundering (AML), General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR), California Consumer Privacy Act (CCPA), Anti-Bribery and Corruption (ABC), Continuing Professional Development (CPD), Continuing Legal Education (CLE)). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: AML|GDPR|CCPA|DPA|SOX|ABC|FATF|SRA|PII|LPP|CPD|CLE|ISO27001|PCI-DSS — 14 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  related_policy_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the firm policy document or control framework that this training programme supports or implements.']
  retirement_date date [note: 'Date when the training programme was or will be retired and no longer available for new completions. Null for active programmes.']
  target_audience varchar [note: 'Description of the intended audience for this training programme, including roles, practice groups, or seniority levels (e.g., all fee earners, partners only, support staff, specific practice groups).']
  target_role_category varchar [note: 'Categorical classification of the primary role groups required to complete this training. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: all-staff|fee-earners|partners|associates|paralegals|support-staff|practice-specific|management — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  training_category varchar [note: 'Classification of the training programme based on requirement level and purpose.']
  version_effective_date date [note: 'Date from which this version of the training programme became effective and available for completion.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version identifier for the training programme content, incremented when material updates are made to reflect regulatory changes or content improvements.']

  Note: 'Compliance training programme records covering mandatory regulatory training requirements such as AML awareness, GDPR/data privacy, anti-bribery and corruption (ABC), sanctions screening, and CPD/CLE compliance obligations. Captures training programme name, regulatory requirement it satisfies, delivery method, frequency, target audience (by role/practice group), pass mark, and version. Distinct from individual completion records tracked in workforce domain — this entity owns the programme definition.'
}

Table compliance.sanctions_check {
  sanctions_check_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for sanctions_check']
  aml_kyc_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.aml_kyc_program. Business justification: Sanctions screening checks are performed as part of the firms AML/KYC programme. N:1 relationship (many checks per program). Connects sanctions_check to aml_kyc_program. No columns to remove - check-s']
  ip_ownership_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ip.ownership. Business justification: Sanctions screening of IP owners and assignees is required for AML/KYC compliance in IP transactions, especially cross-border assignments and licensing. Essential for ensuring IP ownership transfers c']
  license_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ip.license_agreement. Business justification: Sanctions screening of licensors and licensees is required for AML/KYC compliance in IP licensing, especially high-value or cross-border agreements. Essential for ensuring licensing parties are not sa']
  matter_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the matter record associated with this sanctions screening, if the screening was performed in the context of a specific legal matter.']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the client record associated with this sanctions screening, if the subject is a client or related to a client matter.']
  timekeeper_id varchar [note: 'Employee identifier of the compliance officer who reviewed this screening result.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this sanctions screening result record was first created in the system.']
  disposition_date date [note: 'Date on which the final disposition decision was made for this screening result.']
  disposition_rationale varchar [note: 'Detailed explanation and business justification for the disposition decision, including evidence reviewed and reasoning applied.']
  escalation_date date [note: 'Date on which the screening result was escalated to senior compliance management or external authorities. Null if no escalation occurred.']
  escalation_recipient varchar [note: 'Name and role of the individual or authority to whom the screening result was escalated (e.g., Chief Compliance Officer, MLRO, NCA).']
  escalation_required_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether this screening result requires escalation to senior compliance management or external authorities.']
  is_active boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether this screening result record is currently active and valid. False indicates the record has been superseded or archived.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this sanctions screening result record was last updated or modified.']
  match_details varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the match found, including the matched name from the sanctions list, list entry reference number, and any additional identifying information.']
  match_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numerical confidence score (0.00 to 100.00) indicating the strength of the match between the subject and the sanctions list entry. Higher scores indicate stronger matches.']
  match_type varchar [note: 'Type of match identified during screening: exact name match, fuzzy match, alias match, phonetic match, or no match found.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional free-text notes, observations, or context relevant to this sanctions screening result.']
  regulatory_report_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number of any regulatory report filed as a result of this screening (e.g., SAR reference number). Null if no report was filed.']
  regulatory_reporting_required_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether this screening result requires mandatory reporting to regulatory authorities (e.g., Suspicious Activity Report filing).']
  reviewing_officer_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the compliance officer or authorized personnel who reviewed and made the disposition decision.']
  risk_rating varchar [note: 'Overall risk rating assigned to this screening result based on match score, subject type, jurisdiction, and other risk factors.']
  sanctions_list_screened varchar [note: 'Identifier of the sanctions list(s) screened against: OFAC SDN (Office of Foreign Assets Control Specially Designated Nationals), HM Treasury (UK), EU Consolidated List, UN Sanctions List, or Combined All.']
  screening_date date [note: 'Date on which the sanctions screening check was performed.']
  screening_disposition varchar [note: 'Final disposition of the screening result: cleared (no action required), escalated (referred to compliance officer), blocked (transaction/engagement prevented), pending review, or false positive (match dismissed).']
  screening_reference_number varchar [note: 'Business-facing unique reference number assigned to this screening check for tracking and audit purposes.']
  screening_system_name varchar [note: 'Name of the automated screening system or platform used to perform the sanctions check (e.g., Dow Jones, World-Check, ComplyAdvantage).']
  screening_system_version varchar [note: 'Version number of the screening system or sanctions list database used at the time of screening, for audit trail purposes.']
  screening_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise timestamp when the sanctions screening check was executed, including time zone information.']
  screening_trigger_event varchar [note: 'Business event that triggered this sanctions screening check: client onboarding, matter opening, periodic review, transaction review, manual request, or system alert.']
  subject_country_of_residence varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code representing the country of residence or incorporation of the subject being screened.']
  subject_date_of_birth date [note: 'Date of birth of the individual subject being screened, used for enhanced matching accuracy. Null for non-individual subjects.']
  subject_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the individual or entity being screened against sanctions lists.']
  subject_national_identifier varchar [note: 'National identification number, passport number, or registration number of the subject being screened, used for enhanced matching.']
  subject_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the subject being screened: individual person, organisation, beneficial owner, counterparty, related party, or trust.']

  Note: 'Records of sanctions list screening checks performed against clients, counterparties, beneficial owners, and related parties against OFAC SDN, HM Treasury, EU Consolidated, and UN sanctions lists. Captures screening date, subject name, subject type, list screened, match type (exact, fuzzy, alias), match score, disposition (cleared, escalated, blocked), reviewing officer, and disposition rationale. Supports FATF obligations and AML programme requirements. Firm-level screening infrastructure distinct from matter-level conflict checks.'
}

  impacted_control_framework_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.control_framework. Business justification: Regulatory changes often necessitate updates to control frameworks (e.g., new GDPR guidance requires ISO 27001 control updates). The regulatory_change table has regulatory_framework as a string, but l']
  practice_area_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to service.practice_area. Business justification: Regulatory changes impact specific practice areas (SRA transparency rules for conveyancing, GDPR for data privacy practice). Impact assessments are practice-area-scoped. Required for practice-area-lev']

  register_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to risk.risk_register. Business justification: Regulatory changes trigger risk assessments and create risk register entries as part of horizon scanning and regulatory change management. Essential for tracking implementation risks and compliance ga']

Business justification: Regulatory changes require a named timekeeper as implementation owner for tracking accountability, workload, and completion. The existing implementation_owner_employee_id(unlinked) should map to timek']
  change_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the regulatory change, including scope, intent, and key provisions that may impact the firm.']
  change_reference_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique reference number or code assigned to this regulatory change by the issuing body or internal tracking system.']
  change_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the regulatory change: proposed (under consultation), published (finalized but not yet effective), effective (in force), superseded (replaced by newer regulation), or withdrawn.']
  change_title varchar [note: 'Short descriptive title or name of the regulatory change, legislative update, or guidance document.']
  change_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the nature of the regulatory change: new regulation, amendment to existing regulation, repeal, guidance, interpretation, advisory, or consultation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: new_regulation|amendment|repeal|guidance|interpretation|advisory|consultation — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']

  impact_assessment_date date [note: 'Date on which the impact assessment was completed and approved.']
  impact_assessment_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the internal impact assessment process to evaluate the implications of this regulatory change on the firms operations and compliance posture.']
  impact_severity varchar [note: 'Assessed severity level of the regulatory changes impact on the firms compliance posture, operations, and risk exposure.']
  impacted_business_functions varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of internal business functions or departments affected by this regulatory change (e.g., Client Intake, Matter Management, Billing, Knowledge Management, Trust Accounting).']
  implementation_deadline date [note: 'Internal deadline by which the firm must complete all required actions to comply with the regulatory change.']
  is_active boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this regulatory change record is currently active and relevant for compliance tracking. Inactive records represent historical or superseded changes.']
  jurisdiction varchar [note: 'Geographic or legal jurisdiction to which this regulatory change applies (e.g., USA, GBR, EUR, state-specific, or international).']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this regulatory change record was last updated or modified.']
  legal_counsel_review_date date [note: 'Date on which internal legal counsel or General Counsel (GC) completed their review of this regulatory change.']
  legal_counsel_review_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this regulatory change requires review by internal legal counsel or General Counsel (GC) for legal interpretation and compliance strategy.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional comments, observations, or context related to this regulatory change.']
  policy_update_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether existing firm policies, procedures, or governance documents require updates to reflect this regulatory change.']
  publication_date date [note: 'Date on which the regulatory change was officially published or announced by the governing body.']
  regulatory_framework varchar [note: 'Name of the overarching regulatory framework or compliance regime to which this change belongs (e.g., General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR), California Consumer Privacy Act (CCPA), Sarbanes-Oxley Act (SOX), Anti-Money Laundering (AML), Know Your Client (KYC), Legal Professional Privilege (LPP)).']
  regulatory_obligation_created_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this regulatory change has resulted in the creation of a new entry in the regulatory obligation register.']
  related_policy_references varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of internal policy document references or identifiers that are affected by or related to this regulatory change.']
  required_control_updates varchar [note: 'Description of the control framework updates, policy changes, or procedural modifications required to achieve compliance with this regulatory change.']
  responsible_department varchar [note: 'Name of the department or business unit with primary accountability for implementing and maintaining compliance with this regulatory change.']
  risk_rating varchar [note: 'Overall risk rating assigned to non-compliance with this regulatory change, considering likelihood and impact of regulatory breach.']
  source_document_reference varchar [note: 'Official citation or reference number of the source legislation, regulation, or guidance document (e.g., Public Law number, Statutory Instrument number, Directive number).']
  source_document_url varchar [note: 'Web address or hyperlink to the official source document, legislation text, or guidance publication from the governing body.']
  system_changes_description varchar [note: 'Description of the system, technology, or data management changes required to achieve compliance with this regulatory change.']
  system_changes_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether changes to operational systems, technology platforms, or data management systems are required to support compliance with this regulatory change.']
  training_completion_deadline date [note: 'Deadline by which all required training on this regulatory change must be completed by relevant staff.']
  training_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether firm-wide or targeted training is required to educate staff on the implications and requirements of this regulatory change.']

  Note: 'Tracks incoming regulatory changes, legislative updates, and new guidance from governing bodies (ABA, SRA, IBA, FATF, ICO, SEC, FCA) that may impact the firms compliance posture. Captures change source, publication date, effective date, impacted regulatory frameworks, impacted practice areas, impact assessment status, required control updates, implementation owner, and implementation deadline. Feeds the regulatory obligation register when new obligations are confirmed.'
}

Table compliance.policy {
  policy_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for policy']
  implementing_control_framework_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.control_framework. Business justification: Policies are often written to implement specific control framework requirements (e.g., Information Security Policy implements ISO 27001, Data Retention Policy implements GDPR requirements). The po']
  practice_area_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to service.practice_area. Business justification: Compliance policies are scoped to practice areas (data retention for litigation, conflict policies for M&A). Practice groups implement policies differently. Required for practice-area-level policy att']
  regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'FK to compliance.regulatory_obligation.regulatory_obligation_id — Compliance policies are written to satisfy regulatory obligations. This FK enables gap analysis — which obligations lack a governing policy — and is fundamental to policy governance.']
  timekeeper_id varchar [note: 'Employee identifier of the policy owner.']
  applicability_scope varchar [note: 'Description of the organizational scope to which the policy applies (e.g., All Partners and Employees, Client-Facing Staff, IT Department, Global Operations).']
  approval_authority varchar [note: 'Name or title of the individual or committee with authority to approve the policy (e.g., Chief Legal Officer, Compliance Committee, Board of Directors).']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date when the policy was formally approved by the designated authority.']
  breach_reporting_procedure varchar [note: 'Description of the procedure for reporting breaches or violations of this policy.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the policy record was first created in the system.']
  document_location varchar [note: 'File path, DMS reference, or URL where the full policy document is stored (e.g., iManage workspace ID, NetDocuments link).']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date when the policy becomes binding and enforceable within the firm.']
  exemption_applicable_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether exemptions or waivers from this policy are permitted under certain conditions.']
  exemption_details varchar [note: 'Description of circumstances under which exemptions may be granted and the approval process required.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date when the policy ceases to be in force, nullable for policies without a defined end date.']
  external_audit_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the policy requires external audit or independent review for compliance verification.']
  is_active boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the policy record is currently active and in use.']
  jurisdiction varchar [note: 'Geographic or legal jurisdiction to which the policy applies (e.g., GBR, USA, EUR, Global).']
  last_external_audit_date date [note: 'Date when the policy was last audited by an external party.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the policy record was last updated.']
  last_review_date date [note: 'Date when the policy was most recently reviewed and approved.']
  next_external_audit_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next external audit of policy compliance.']
  next_review_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next mandatory policy review.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional free-text notes, comments, or context regarding the policy.']
  objective varchar [note: 'High-level business objective or purpose of the policy, explaining what regulatory or operational risk it mitigates.']
  owner_department varchar [note: 'Department or business unit responsible for the policy (e.g., Compliance, Risk Management, Legal Operations).']
  penalty_for_non_compliance varchar [note: 'Description of internal disciplinary actions or external penalties that may result from policy violations.']
  policy_code varchar [note: 'Unique business identifier code for the policy, typically following firm naming convention (e.g., AML-001, GDPR-002, SOX-003).']
  policy_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the compliance policy (e.g., Anti-Money Laundering Policy, Data Protection Policy, Conflicts of Interest Policy).']
  policy_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the policy indicating its approval and operational state.']
  policy_type varchar [note: 'Category of compliance policy indicating the regulatory domain it addresses.']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'Operational priority assigned to the policy for resource allocation and monitoring.']
  regulatory_framework varchar [note: 'Primary regulatory framework or legislation that the policy addresses (e.g., GDPR, CCPA, SOX, FATF, SRA Code of Conduct).']
  regulatory_obligations_satisfied varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list or description of specific regulatory obligations that this policy satisfies (e.g., GDPR Article 5, FATF Recommendation 10, SOX Section 404).']
  related_policy_references varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of related policy codes or names that should be read in conjunction with this policy.']
  review_cycle_months varchar [note: 'Frequency in months at which the policy must be reviewed and reaffirmed (e.g., 12, 24, 36).']
  risk_rating varchar [note: 'Risk severity rating assigned to the policy area, indicating the potential impact of non-compliance.']
  source_legislation_url varchar [note: 'URL or reference link to the primary legislation, regulation, or guidance document that the policy is based on.']
  superseded_policy_code varchar [note: 'Policy code of the previous version or policy that this policy replaces, establishing version lineage.']
  training_frequency_months varchar [note: 'Frequency in months at which training must be completed by applicable staff (e.g., 12, 24).']
  training_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether mandatory training is required for staff subject to this policy.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version identifier for the policy document following semantic versioning (e.g., 1.0, 2.1, 3.0).']

  Note: 'Master register of the firms internal compliance policies and procedures, including AML policy, data protection policy, anti-bribery policy, sanctions policy, conflicts of interest policy, and information security policy. Captures policy name, policy owner, version, effective date, review cycle, approval authority, regulatory obligations satisfied, and current status (draft, approved, superseded). Provides the policy governance layer above individual controls.'
}

Table compliance.audit_engagement {
  audit_engagement_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the audit engagement record. Primary key for the audit engagement entity.']
  audit_responsible_officer_employee_timekeeper_id varchar [note: 'Employee identifier for the responsible officer accountable for audit remediation.']
  engagement_owner_timekeeper_id varchar [note: 'Employee identifier for the lead auditor if the audit is conducted internally or by a known staff member.']
  practice_area_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to service.practice_area. Business justification: Audits are scoped by practice area (SRA audit of conveyancing, ISO audit of IP practice). Regulatory audits target specific practice areas. Essential for practice-area-level audit planning, resource a']
  primary_control_framework_id varchar [note: 'FK to compliance.control_framework.control_framework_id — Audit engagements are scoped against specific control frameworks (e.g., ISO 27001 surveillance audit, SOX controls audit). This FK defines audit scope and enables framework-level assurance reporting.']
  primary_lead_auditor_timekeeper_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.timekeeper. Business justification: Internal compliance audits are conducted by firm timekeepers (often senior associates or compliance specialists). Linking lead auditor to timekeeper enables workload tracking, CPD credit allocation, a']
  audit_documentation_location varchar [note: 'File path, document management system reference, or storage location for the complete audit engagement documentation and working papers.']
  audit_period_end_date date [note: 'The end date of the period under audit review. Defines the conclusion of the timeframe for which compliance and controls are being assessed.']
  audit_period_start_date date [note: 'The start date of the period under audit review. Defines the beginning of the timeframe for which compliance and controls are being assessed.']
  audit_scope varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the scope of the audit engagement, including the business units, processes, controls, and regulatory obligations covered.']
  audit_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the audit engagement from planning through closure.']
  audit_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the audit engagement by its nature and purpose. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: internal_compliance_review|external_regulatory_inspection|iso_27001_surveillance|aml_audit|gdpr_audit|sox_compliance_audit|sra_inspection|pci_dss_audit|data_protection_audit|professional_indemnity_review — promote to reference product]']
  auditor_type varchar [note: 'Classification indicating whether the audit is conducted by internal staff, external consultants, or a regulatory authority.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this audit engagement record was first created in the system.']
  critical_findings_count varchar [note: 'The number of critical severity findings identified that represent immediate and significant compliance or control failures.']
  draft_report_date date [note: 'The date on which the draft audit report was issued to management for review and response.']
  engagement_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the audit engagement identifying the subject and scope of the audit.']
  engagement_reference_number varchar [note: 'Business-facing unique reference number assigned to the audit engagement for tracking and communication purposes.']
  external_auditor_name varchar [note: 'Name of the external audit firm or regulatory body conducting the audit. Null for internal audits.']
  fieldwork_end_date date [note: 'The date on which the audit fieldwork and evidence gathering activities concluded.']
  fieldwork_start_date date [note: 'The date on which the audit fieldwork and evidence gathering activities commenced.']
  final_report_date date [note: 'The date on which the final audit report was issued incorporating management responses and final audit opinion.']
  findings_closed_count varchar [note: 'The number of audit findings that have been remediated and verified as closed.']
  findings_open_count varchar [note: 'The number of audit findings that remain open and require remediation action.']
  findings_overdue_count varchar [note: 'The number of audit findings that have passed their target remediation date and remain open.']
  follow_up_audit_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a follow-up audit engagement is required to verify remediation of findings.']
  follow_up_audit_scheduled_date date [note: 'The scheduled date for the follow-up audit engagement to verify closure of findings.']
  high_findings_count varchar [note: 'The number of high severity findings identified that represent material compliance or control weaknesses.']
  is_active boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this audit engagement record is currently active in the system.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this audit engagement record was last updated in the system.']
  low_findings_count varchar [note: 'The number of low severity findings identified that represent minor compliance or control observations.']
  management_response_due_date date [note: 'The deadline by which management is required to provide formal responses to audit findings and proposed remediation plans.']
  management_response_received_date date [note: 'The actual date on which management submitted their formal response to the audit findings.']
  medium_findings_count varchar [note: 'The number of medium severity findings identified that represent moderate compliance or control gaps.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional free-text notes, observations, or context relevant to the audit engagement.']
  overall_audit_opinion varchar [note: 'The auditors overall conclusion regarding the effectiveness of controls and compliance with the regulatory framework under review.']
  regulatory_framework varchar [note: 'The regulatory framework or control standard against which the audit is conducted (e.g., GDPR, AML/KYC, ISO 27001, SOX, SRA Code of Conduct).']
  regulatory_report_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number or identifier for any regulatory report filed as a result of this audit engagement.']
  regulatory_report_submission_date date [note: 'The date on which the regulatory report was submitted to the governing authority.']
  regulatory_reporting_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the audit findings require formal reporting to an external regulatory body or governing authority.']
  responsible_department varchar [note: 'The department or business unit primarily responsible for addressing the audit findings and implementing remediation actions.']
  responsible_officer_name varchar [note: 'Name of the senior officer or executive responsible for overseeing the remediation of audit findings and ensuring compliance.']
  total_findings_count varchar [note: 'The total number of audit findings identified during the engagement across all severity levels.']

  Note: 'Records of internal and external compliance audit engagements and their findings, conducted against the firms regulatory obligations and control frameworks. Covers SRA inspections, ISO 27001 surveillance audits, external AML audits, and internal compliance reviews. Each engagement captures audit type, auditor (internal/external), scope, audit period, fieldwork dates, report dates, management responses, overall opinion, and individual findings with severity ratings, root causes, recommended actions, remediation owners, target dates, and closure verification. Serves as the complete audit lifecycle record from planning through finding remediation.'
}

Table compliance.audit_finding {
  audit_finding_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the audit finding record. Primary key.']
  audit_action_owner_timekeeper_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.timekeeper. Business justification: Audit findings require remediation by named timekeepers. Linking action owner to timekeeper enables tracking of open findings by owner, workload balancing, and performance review input for remediation']
  audit_engagement_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent compliance audit engagement during which this finding was raised.']
  auditor_employee_timekeeper_id varchar [note: 'Employee identifier of the auditor who raised the finding.']
  compliance_control_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.compliance_control. Business justification: Audit findings relate to specific compliance controls that were tested. N:1 relationship (many findings per control). audit_finding has control_reference (STRING) which likely refers to compliance_con']
  finding_owner_timekeeper_id varchar [note: 'Employee identifier of the action owner responsible for remediation.']
  ip_asset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ip.asset. Business justification: Audit findings related to IP asset management (valuation errors, recordal failures, inadequate protection measures) require linkage to specific assets. Essential for tracking audit remediation and con']
  legal_document_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to document.legal_document. Business justification: Audit reports, management responses, remediation plans are legal documents: external audit report, management representation letter, corrective action plan, board resolution approving remediation. Req']
  matter_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the matter if the finding relates to a specific legal matter or case.']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the client if the finding relates to a specific client matter or engagement.']
  register_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to risk.risk_register. Business justification: Audit findings identifying control deficiencies create or update risk register entries as part of risk management response cycle. Essential for audit-to-risk remediation tracking and governance report']
  violated_policy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.policy. Business justification: Audit findings often identify policy violations. The finding already has related_policy_reference as a string attribute, which should be replaced by this FK for referential integrity. This provides tr']
  actual_closure_date date [note: 'Actual date on which the finding was verified as resolved and formally closed.']
  agreed_action varchar [note: 'The specific action agreed between auditor and management to remediate the finding.']
  auditor_name varchar [note: 'Name of the auditor who raised the finding.']
  control_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific control or control framework element that is deficient or non-compliant.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this audit finding record was first created in the system.']
  escalation_date date [note: 'Date on which the finding was escalated to higher authority.']
  escalation_recipient varchar [note: 'Name or role of the individual or body to whom the finding was escalated (e.g., General Counsel, Board Audit Committee, SRA).']
  escalation_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the finding requires escalation to senior management, board, or regulatory authority due to severity or nature.']
  evidence_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the audit evidence or documentation supporting the finding (e.g., document ID, file path, sample reference).']
  finding_category varchar [note: 'Classification of the finding type: control gap (missing or ineffective control), policy breach (violation of internal policy), regulatory non-compliance (failure to meet external regulatory requirement), process deficiency (operational process weakness), documentation gap (missing or inadequate documentation), or system weakness (technology control failure).']
  finding_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the audit finding, including the condition observed, the criteria or standard against which it was assessed, and the context.']
  finding_raised_date date [note: 'Date on which the audit finding was formally raised and documented.']
  finding_reference_number varchar [note: 'Business-facing unique reference number assigned to this finding for tracking and reporting purposes.']
  finding_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the finding: open (newly raised, no action started), in progress (remediation underway), pending verification (action completed, awaiting audit verification), closed (verified and resolved), overdue (past target date without closure).']
  finding_title varchar [note: 'Concise title or summary of the audit finding for quick identification and reporting.']
  finding_type varchar [note: 'Nature of the finding: observation (noted fact without immediate action), recommendation (suggested improvement), non-conformance (failure to meet requirement), or opportunity for improvement (enhancement suggestion).']
  impact_assessment varchar [note: 'Assessment of the actual or potential business, regulatory, or operational impact of the finding.']
  is_active boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this finding record is currently active in the system. False if the record has been archived or superseded.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this audit finding record was last updated or modified.']
  management_response varchar [note: 'Managements formal response to the finding, including acceptance, planned actions, or alternative approach.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional notes, comments, or context related to the finding, remediation, or verification process.']
  recommended_action varchar [note: 'Auditors recommended corrective or remedial action to address the finding and mitigate associated risk.']
  regulatory_framework varchar [note: 'The regulatory framework or standard against which the finding was assessed (e.g., GDPR, SOX, SRA Code of Conduct, AML regulations).']
  regulatory_report_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number or identifier of the regulatory report filed in relation to this finding.']
  regulatory_reporting_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the finding must be reported to an external regulatory body or governing authority.']
  related_policy_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the internal policy or procedure that is relevant to this finding.']
  responsible_department varchar [note: 'Department or business unit responsible for addressing the finding.']
  risk_rating varchar [note: 'Overall risk rating associated with the finding if left unaddressed, considering likelihood and impact.']
  root_cause varchar [note: 'Analysis of the underlying root cause(s) that led to the finding, supporting effective remediation.']
  severity varchar [note: 'Severity rating of the finding based on potential impact and urgency: critical (immediate action required, significant regulatory or business risk), high (prompt action needed, material risk), medium (moderate risk, action required within normal cycle), low (minor issue, improvement opportunity).']
  target_remediation_date date [note: 'Target date by which the agreed remediation action should be completed.']
  verification_date date [note: 'Date on which the auditor verified the effectiveness of the remediation action.']
  verification_notes varchar [note: 'Auditors notes and observations from the verification activity, including evidence reviewed and conclusion.']
  verification_status varchar [note: 'Status of the auditors verification of the remediation action: not started, scheduled, in progress, verified (action confirmed effective), or failed verification (action inadequate, finding remains open).']

  Note: 'Individual findings raised during compliance audit engagements, including observations, recommendations, and agreed management actions. Captures finding reference, parent audit engagement, finding category (control gap, policy breach, regulatory non-compliance), severity (critical, high, medium, low), root cause, recommended action, management response, action owner, target remediation date, actual closure date, and verification status. Supports ongoing compliance assurance and regulatory reporting.'
}

Table compliance.audit_scope {
  audit_scope_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for this audit scope record. Primary key.']
  audit_engagement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the audit engagement that includes this obligation in its scope']
  regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the regulatory obligation being audited within this engagement']
  audit_period_end_date date [note: 'The end date of the audit period for this specific obligation within this engagement. May differ from the overall engagement audit period if obligations have different review cycles.']
  audit_period_start_date date [note: 'The start date of the audit period for this specific obligation within this engagement. May differ from the overall engagement audit period if obligations have different review cycles.']
  auditor_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes and observations recorded by the auditor regarding this obligation during this engagement']
  exceptions_identified varchar [note: 'Number of exceptions or control failures identified during testing of this obligation']
  obligation_compliance_status varchar [note: 'The audit conclusion regarding compliance with this specific obligation within this engagement: compliant, non_compliant, partially_compliant, not_tested, in_progress']
  obligation_findings_count varchar [note: 'The number of audit findings identified that relate specifically to this regulatory obligation within this audit engagement']
  obligation_risk_rating varchar [note: 'Risk rating assigned to this obligation within the context of this audit engagement based on inherent risk, control effectiveness, and audit findings: critical, high, medium, low']
  obligation_test_results_summary varchar [note: 'Summary of the testing procedures performed and results obtained for this specific obligation during this audit engagement']
  sample_size varchar [note: 'Number of items or transactions sampled during testing of this obligation within this audit engagement']
  scope_inclusion_date date [note: 'Date when this regulatory obligation was formally included in the audit engagement scope during planning']
  scope_status varchar [note: 'Current status of audit work for this obligation within this engagement: planned, in_fieldwork, testing_complete, reported, closed']
  testing_approach varchar [note: 'Description of the audit testing methodology and procedures applied to assess compliance with this obligation in this engagement']

  Note: 'This association product represents the scoping relationship between audit engagements and regulatory obligations. It captures which specific regulatory obligations are included in the scope of each audit engagement, along with the compliance assessment results, findings, and test outcomes specific to that obligation within that audit. Each record links one audit engagement to one regulatory obligation with attributes that exist only in the context of this specific audit-obligation pairing.. Existence Justification: Audit engagements are explicitly scoped to cover multiple regulatory obligations, and each regulatory obligation is audited repeatedly across multiple audit engagements over time. Audit teams actively manage the scoping process during audit planning, defining which obligations are in-scope for each engagement. The relationship carries significant operational data including compliance assessment results, findings counts, test results, and risk ratings that are specific to each obligation-engagement pairing.'
}

Table compliance.policy_acknowledgement {
  policy_acknowledgement_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for this policy acknowledgement record. Primary key.']
  compliance_policy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the policy that was acknowledged']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the client who acknowledged the policy']
  acceptance_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the policy acknowledgement. Acknowledged indicates active acceptance, Pending indicates awaiting client response, Declined indicates client refused (may block engagement), Expired indicates acknowledgement has passed renewal date, Superseded indicates a newer version has been acknowledged.']
  acknowledged_by_contact_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the individual who acknowledged the policy. Used for audit trail and for sending renewal notifications.']
  acknowledged_by_contact_name varchar [note: 'Name of the individual at the client organization who provided the acknowledgement. For individual clients, this is the client themselves. For corporate clients, this is the authorized representative.']
  acknowledgement_date date [note: 'Date when the client formally acknowledged and accepted the policy. This is the audit timestamp for regulatory compliance purposes.']
  acknowledgement_document_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the signed acknowledgement document stored in the document management system. Critical for regulatory audit and dispute resolution.']
  attestation_method varchar [note: 'The method by which the client provided acknowledgement. Electronic Signature and Wet Signature provide strongest audit evidence. Implied by Engagement may be used for existing clients under legacy policies.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when this acknowledgement record was created.']
  next_acknowledgement_due_date date [note: 'Scheduled date when the client must re-acknowledge this policy. Calculated based on the policy review cycle and regulatory requirements. Used to trigger renewal workflows.']
  reminder_sent_date date [note: 'Date when the most recent renewal reminder was sent to the client. Used to track compliance workflow progress.']
  required_for_engagement_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether acknowledgement of this policy is mandatory before the firm can accept new matters from this client. AML and conflicts policies are typically mandatory.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when this acknowledgement record was last modified.']
  version_acknowledged varchar [note: 'The specific version number of the policy that was acknowledged by the client. Critical for audit trail as policies evolve over time and clients must re-acknowledge new versions.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the formal acknowledgement event between client_profile and policy. It captures the regulatory requirement for clients to acknowledge and accept specific compliance policies as part of the client onboarding and ongoing compliance management process. Each record links one client to one policy version with the date acknowledged, acceptance status, attestation method, and next renewal date. This is a critical audit trail for regulatory compliance and risk management.. Existence Justification: Legal services firms are required by regulators to obtain and track client acknowledgement of multiple compliance policies (AML, conflicts of interest, data protection, engagement terms, etc.). Each client must acknowledge multiple policies, and each policy must be acknowledged by multiple clients. The business actively manages this relationship through onboarding workflows, renewal cycles, and audit trail maintenance for regulatory examinations.'
}

Table compliance.audit_program {
  audit_program_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for audit_program']
  audit_approved_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the individual who approved this audit program for execution.']
  department_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the department or business unit that owns or is subject to this audit program.']
  parent_audit_program_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on audit_program (parent_audit_program_id)']
  primary_lead_auditor_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the lead auditor assigned to execute this audit program.']
  program_owner_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the individual or role responsible for the overall management and governance of this audit program.']
  target_control_framework_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.control_framework. Business justification: Audit programs are often designed to test compliance with specific control frameworks (e.g., ISO 27001 Annual Audit Program, SOX Controls Testing Program). The audit_program table has compliance_f']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date when the audit program was formally approved.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval status of the audit program by governance or management.']
  audit_criteria varchar [note: 'Set of policies, procedures, standards, or requirements used as a reference against which audit evidence is compared.']
  budget_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Allocated budget for executing this audit program, in the firms base currency (GBP for UK-based legal firms).']
  compliance_framework varchar [note: 'Primary regulatory or compliance framework that this audit program addresses (e.g., SOX, GDPR, AML/KYC, FATF, SRA regulatory returns).']
  confidentiality_level varchar [note: 'Data classification level for the audit program documentation and findings.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this audit program record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the budget amount (e.g., GBP, USD, EUR).']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the audit program is scheduled to conclude or be retired. Null for ongoing programs.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the audit program becomes active and binding for execution.']
  estimated_duration_days varchar [note: 'Estimated number of days required to complete one full cycle of this audit program.']
  frequency varchar [note: 'Scheduled frequency at which this audit program is executed (e.g., annual, quarterly, ad-hoc).']
  is_active boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this audit program is currently active and in use.']
  is_mandatory boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this audit program is mandated by regulation or is voluntary/discretionary.']
  last_execution_date date [note: 'Date when this audit program was last executed or completed.']
  last_review_date date [note: 'Date when the audit program was last reviewed or updated by governance.']
  methodology varchar [note: 'The audit approach or methodology employed in this program (e.g., risk-based, compliance-based, process-based).']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this audit program record was last modified or updated.']
  next_review_date date [note: 'Date when the audit program is scheduled for its next periodic review or update.']
  next_scheduled_date date [note: 'Date when the next execution of this audit program is scheduled to begin.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional notes, comments, or context regarding the audit program.']
  objective varchar [note: 'Primary business objective or goal of the audit program (e.g., verify SOX controls effectiveness, assess GDPR compliance posture, validate AML procedures).']
  priority varchar [note: 'Business priority level assigned to this audit program for resource allocation and scheduling.']
  program_code varchar [note: 'Unique business identifier or reference code for the audit program used in reporting and tracking.']
  program_name varchar [note: 'The official name or title of the audit program (e.g., Annual SOX Compliance Audit, GDPR Data Privacy Review).']
  program_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the audit program indicating its operational state.']
  program_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the audit program based on its origin and purpose (internal firm audit, external regulatory audit, client-requested audit, or combined).']
  regulatory_authority varchar [note: 'Name of the external regulatory body or authority mandating or overseeing this audit program (e.g., Solicitors Regulation Authority, Financial Conduct Authority, Information Commissioners Office).']
  requires_external_auditor boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this audit program requires participation or oversight by an external independent auditor.']
