  requires_calibration boolean [note: 'Indicates whether ratings using this scale must go through a calibration process with peer review or management committee to ensure consistency across evaluators. True if calibration is required, False otherwise.']
  scale_code varchar [note: 'Short alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the rating scale for system integration and reporting (e.g., PERF-5PT, COMP-10PT, PARTNER-EVAL).']
  scale_description varchar [note: 'Detailed business description of the rating scale, its purpose, and usage context within performance management, talent development, or compensation processes.']
  scale_direction varchar [note: 'Indicates whether higher values represent better performance (ascending) or lower values represent better performance (descending). Most legal industry scales are ascending.']
  scale_name varchar [note: 'The business name of the rating scale (e.g., Annual Performance Review Scale, Partner Evaluation Scale, Associate Competency Scale).']
  scale_type varchar [note: 'Categorical classification of the rating scale purpose: performance (annual reviews), competency (skill assessments), potential (succession planning), behavior (conduct evaluations), skill (technical proficiency), client_feedback (client satisfaction).']
  self_assessment_allowed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether timekeepers can use this rating scale to perform self-assessments as part of the performance review process. True if self-assessment is allowed, False otherwise.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'The system of record where this rating scale is defined and maintained (e.g., SAP SuccessFactors, Workday HCM, custom HRIS).']
  usage_context varchar [note: 'The primary business context in which this rating scale is applied: annual_review (year-end performance), mid_year_review (interim check-in), probation_review (new hire evaluation), promotion_review (advancement consideration), compensation_review (bonus and salary decisions), succession_planning (leadership pipeline assessment).']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version identifier for the rating scale to track changes over time (e.g., v1.0, v2.1, 2024.1). Used when scales are updated or refined.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for rating_scale. Referenced by rating_scale_id.'
}

Table service.practice_area {
  practice_area_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the practice area. Primary key for the practice area master taxonomy.']
  category_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to risk.risk_category. Business justification: Practice areas map to inherent risk categories (litigation = PI exposure, regulatory = compliance risk, corporate = conflict risk). Insurance allocation, conflict screening protocols, and risk managem']
  parent_practice_area_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent practice area in the hierarchical taxonomy, enabling roll-up reporting from sub-specializations to broader practice groups. Null for top-level practice areas.']
  average_matter_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Historical average total billed value for matters in this practice area, used for pipeline valuation, revenue forecasting, and business development targeting. Expressed in firm base currency.']
  billable_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether time and disbursements in this practice area are typically billable to clients. False for internal practice areas such as professional development, pro bono coordination, or firm administration.']
  classification_type varchar [note: 'High-level classification of the practice area by the nature of legal work performed. Transactional covers M&A (Mergers and Acquisitions), corporate deals; Advisory covers counseling and opinions; Litigation covers dispute resolution; Regulatory covers compliance and government relations; Hybrid covers mixed-mode practices; Specialized covers niche areas like IP (Intellectual Property) prosecution.']
  cpd_requirement_hours decimal(5,2) [note: 'Annual CPD (Continuing Professional Development) or CLE (Continuing Legal Education) hours required for timekeepers practicing in this area, as mandated by the governing regulatory body. Used for compliance tracking and training program planning.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this practice area record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  default_billing_arrangement varchar [note: 'The most common fee arrangement type for matters in this practice area. Hourly for traditional time-based billing; Fixed Fee for scoped project work; Contingency for plaintiff litigation; AFA (Alternative Fee Arrangement) for value-based or risk-sharing models; Retainer for ongoing advisory; Capped Fee for budget-certainty arrangements; Blended Rate for team-based pricing; Success Fee for outcome-based compensation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: hourly|fixed_fee|contingency|afa|retainer|capped_fee|blended_rate|success_fee — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  effective_from_date date [note: 'Date from which this practice area definition became effective and available for matter classification and timekeeper assignment. Used for historical reporting and taxonomy versioning.']
  effective_to_date date [note: 'Date on which this practice area definition ceased to be effective. Null for currently active practice areas. Used for historical reporting and taxonomy evolution tracking.']
  external_reference_code varchar [note: 'External or client-facing practice area code used in RFP (Request for Proposal) responses, pitch materials, or industry benchmarking surveys. May differ from internal code for marketing or competitive positioning reasons.']
  hierarchy_level varchar [note: 'Numeric depth of this practice area in the hierarchical taxonomy. Level 1 represents top-level practice groups, Level 2 represents major practice areas, Level 3+ represents sub-specializations. Enables hierarchical roll-up queries and reporting.']
  hierarchy_path varchar [note: 'Materialized path representing the full lineage from root to this practice area, typically formatted as slash-separated IDs or codes (e.g., /CORP/MA/CROSSBORDER). Optimizes hierarchical queries and enables efficient ancestor/descendant lookups.']
  insurance_coverage_requirement varchar [note: 'Specific professional indemnity or malpractice insurance coverage requirements for this practice area, including minimum coverage amounts and specialized endorsements. Critical for high-risk areas like securities litigation, M&A (Mergers and Acquisitions), or medical malpractice defense.']
  jurisdiction_scope varchar [note: 'Geographic or legal jurisdiction scope for this practice area. May be a single jurisdiction (e.g., New York, England and Wales), multi-jurisdictional (e.g., US Federal, EU), or global. Critical for conflict checking, regulatory compliance, and timekeeper licensing requirements.']
  knowledge_management_category varchar [note: 'Classification used by the firms knowledge management system to organize precedents, practice notes, standard documents, and checklists for this practice area. Maps to DMS (Document Management System) taxonomy and knowledge repository structure.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this practice area record was last updated. Used for change tracking, audit trail, and data synchronization across systems.']
  practice_area_code varchar [note: 'Short alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the practice area across all firm systems. Used in matter coding, timekeeper assignments, and rate card structures.']
  practice_area_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative describing the scope, services, and typical matters handled within this practice area. Used for RFP (Request for Proposal) responses, business development materials, and knowledge management.']
  practice_area_name varchar [note: 'Full business name of the practice area as recognized by clients and used in marketing materials, engagement letters, and matter classifications.']
  practice_area_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the practice area. Active areas are available for new matter intake and timekeeper assignment. Inactive areas are no longer offered but may have legacy matters. Suspended areas are temporarily unavailable. Archived areas are historical records only.']
  practice_group varchar [note: 'Broader practice group or department to which this practice area belongs, enabling organizational roll-up and resource allocation reporting.']
  primary_service_line varchar [note: 'The primary revenue-generating service line or business unit to which this practice area contributes. Used for P&L (Profit and Loss) allocation, partner compensation, and strategic planning.']
  pro_bono_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this practice area is commonly used for pro bono matters. True for areas like immigration, civil rights, family law; False for highly specialized commercial areas. Used for pro bono reporting and CLE (Continuing Legal Education) credit tracking.']
  regulatory_body_alignment varchar [note: 'Primary regulatory or professional body whose standards, ethics rules, and CPD (Continuing Professional Development) requirements govern this practice area. Examples include ABA (American Bar Association), SRA (Solicitors Regulation Authority), IBA (International Bar Association), state bar associations, or specialized bodies like USPTO for IP (Intellectual Property) practice.']
  requires_security_clearance boolean [note: 'Indicates whether timekeepers working in this practice area typically require government or client security clearance due to the nature of matters (e.g., defense contracting, national security, classified government work).']
  requires_specialization boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this practice area requires specialized certification, licensing, or accreditation beyond general legal practice admission. True for areas like patent prosecution (USPTO registration), tax law (CPA or LLM), or admiralty law (specialized bar admission).']
  short_name varchar [note: 'Abbreviated name or acronym for the practice area used in internal reporting, dashboards, and space-constrained displays.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the operational system of record from which this practice area definition originated. Typically Elite 3E practice area master, but may include legacy systems or manual taxonomy management tools.']
  technology_platform_requirement varchar [note: 'Specialized technology platforms or tools typically required for matters in this practice area. Examples: Relativity for eDiscovery (Electronic Discovery) in litigation; LexisNexis for legal research; CLM (Contract Lifecycle Management) systems for transactional work; IP (Intellectual Property) docketing systems for patent prosecution.']
  typical_matter_duration_days varchar [note: 'Average or median duration in days for matters in this practice area, from matter opening to closure. Used for resource planning, pipeline forecasting, and WIP (Work in Progress) aging analysis. Null for ongoing advisory relationships without defined end dates.']
  utbms_activity_code_prefix varchar [note: 'Standard UTBMS (Uniform Task-Based Management System) activity code prefix applicable to this practice area, providing granular activity-level coding for time entries and expense tracking in client billing.']
  utbms_task_code_prefix varchar [note: 'Standard UTBMS (Uniform Task-Based Management System) task code prefix applicable to this practice area, enabling standardized time entry coding and client billing compliance. Examples: L for Litigation, C for Case Management, A for ADR (Alternative Dispute Resolution), T for Transactional.']

  Note: 'Master taxonomy of legal practice areas and sub-specializations offered by the firm (e.g., M&A, Litigation, IP, Employment Law, Regulatory Compliance, Real Estate). Supports hierarchical parent-child classification enabling roll-up reporting. Each practice area carries a unique code, description, active/inactive status, and regulatory body alignment. Used to categorize matters, timekeepers, service offerings, and rate cards across all firm systems.'
}

Table service.legal_service {
  legal_service_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the legal service offering. Primary key for the legal service catalog.']
  attorney_profile_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the partner responsible for this service offerings definition, quality, pricing, and strategic positioning. Links to the workforce or partner master data.']
  category_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to risk.risk_category. Business justification: Legal services are classified by inherent risk category (M&A = conflict risk, IP = PI exposure). Risk-based pricing, insurance requirements, and partner approval thresholds depend on this classificati']
  delivery_model_id varchar [note: 'FK to service.delivery_model.delivery_model_id — Services are delivered through specific models (in-house, secondment, ALSP). The service definition must reference its standard delivery model for capacity planning and client communication.']
  line_id varchar [note: 'FK to service.service_line.service_line_id — Every legal service rolls up to a service line for P&L reporting and business management. This is the operational grouping FK that enables firm financial reporting by service line.']
  primary_practice_area_id varchar [note: 'FK to service.practice_area.practice_area_id — Every legal service offering must be classified under a practice area. This is the primary taxonomic relationship in the domain and is referenced in every RFP, engagement letter, and matter opening. W']
  allows_afa boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this service offering is eligible for Alternative Fee Arrangement (AFA) pricing models including fixed fees, capped fees, success fees, or blended rates.']
  approval_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether engagement for this service requires formal approval from practice group leadership, risk committee, or conflicts committee before client commitment.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this service offering record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage.']
  cross_sell_services varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of related service codes that are commonly cross-sold or bundled with this offering. Used in business development, client relationship management, and revenue optimization.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the standard rate. Supports multi-currency pricing for international service offerings.']
  deliverable_types varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of typical deliverable types produced by this service. Examples include legal opinions, contracts, court filings, compliance reports, due diligence memoranda, or advisory presentations.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date from which this service offering became or will become available for client engagement. Controls visibility in service catalogs and business development systems.']
  eligible_client_segments varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of client segments or types eligible for this service offering. Used to filter service catalog visibility in business development and RFP response systems. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: corporate|government|individual|nonprofit|startup|enterprise|sme — promote to reference product]']
  estimated_duration_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'Typical or average duration in hours required to deliver this service. Used for resource planning, capacity management, and client expectation setting in engagement letters.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date on which this service offering will be or was retired from the active catalog. Null for ongoing services. Used for service lifecycle management and historical analysis.']
  insurance_coverage_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether specialized professional indemnity insurance coverage is required for this service offering beyond standard firm coverage. Relevant for high-risk or specialized services.']
  is_billable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this service is typically billed to clients or provided as a non-billable internal or pro bono service. Controls time entry classification and revenue recognition.']
  jurisdiction_coverage varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of legal jurisdictions or geographic regions where the firm is qualified and authorized to deliver this service. Critical for regulatory compliance and engagement eligibility.']
  knowledge_assets varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of knowledge management asset identifiers (practice notes, precedent documents, checklists, templates) from the knowledge management system that support delivery of this service.']
  last_review_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent formal review of this service offerings definition, pricing, scope, and compliance requirements. Supports governance and quality assurance processes.']
  matter_type_mapping varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of matter types in the practice management system that typically utilize this service offering. Enables service-to-matter linkage for analytics and cross-selling.']
  modified_by_user varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the user who last modified this service offering record. Supports audit trail and accountability.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this service offering record was last modified. Used for audit trail, change tracking, and data synchronization.']
  next_review_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next formal review of this service offering. Ensures periodic validation of service relevance, pricing accuracy, and regulatory compliance.']
  practice_area varchar [note: 'Primary practice area or legal discipline to which this service belongs. Aligns service offerings with firm practice group structure and expertise areas.']
  regulatory_framework varchar [note: 'Primary regulatory or compliance framework applicable to this service offering. Examples include General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR), Sarbanes-Oxley Act (SOX), Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) regulations, or industry-specific compliance regimes.']
  required_expertise_level varchar [note: 'Minimum attorney seniority or expertise level required to lead delivery of this service. Used for resource allocation, staffing decisions, and quality assurance.']
  requires_conflict_check boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this service offering mandates a formal conflict check through the conflict checking system before engagement. Ensures compliance with ethical rules and Legal Professional Privilege (LPP) requirements.']
  requires_kyc boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this service offering requires Know Your Client (KYC) and Anti-Money Laundering (AML) verification before engagement. Ensures compliance with Financial Action Task Force (FATF) standards.']
  service_category varchar [note: 'Commercial category of the service offering indicating complexity, customization level, and typical engagement model. Used for pricing strategy and client segmentation.']
  service_code varchar [note: 'Unique alphanumeric code identifying the service offering in business development systems, Request for Proposal (RFP) responses, and Letter of Engagement (LOE) documents. Used as the external business identifier.']
  service_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the legal service offering, including scope, deliverables, typical activities, and value proposition. Used in RFP responses and client presentations.']
  service_name varchar [note: 'Full business name of the legal service offering as presented to clients and used in marketing materials, RFP responses, and engagement letters.']
  service_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the service offering. Controls visibility in business development systems, RFP response tools, and client-facing service catalogs.']
  service_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the service by delivery model and nature of legal work performed. Distinguishes between advisory, transactional, contentious, and support services.']
  sla_delivery_time_days varchar [note: 'Committed delivery timeframe in business days from engagement to completion, as defined in Service Level Agreement (SLA) terms. Used in client commitments and matter planning.']
  sla_response_time_hours varchar [note: 'Committed response time in hours from service request to initial engagement or acknowledgment, as defined in Service Level Agreement (SLA) terms for this offering.']
  standard_rate decimal(15,2) [note: 'Standard billing rate or base fee for this service offering in the firms primary operating currency. For hourly services, represents the blended or standard hourly rate. For fixed-fee services, represents the base package price.']
  technology_requirements varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of specialized technology platforms or tools required to deliver this service. Examples include eDiscovery platforms, contract lifecycle management systems, legal research databases, or document automation tools.']
  utbms_task_codes varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of Uniform Task-Based Management System (UTBMS) task codes associated with this service offering. Used for time entry classification, billing, and client reporting in LEDES format.']

  Note: 'Core catalog of legal service offerings available from the firm, including M&A advisory, litigation representation, IP counsel, regulatory compliance counseling, employment law advisory, and structured advisory packages. Each record defines a distinct service offering with its scope, practice area alignment, delivery model, and eligibility criteria. This is the SSOT for what the firm offers commercially and operationally, used in RFP responses, engagement letters (LOE), and business development pipelines in Microsoft Dynamics 365.'
}

Table service.tier {
  tier_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the service tier. Primary key.']
  appetite_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to risk.risk_appetite. Business justification: Service tiers define firm risk appetite boundaries (premium tier = higher complexity tolerance, standard tier = conservative limits). Engagement acceptance workflows verify matter risk profile against']
  practice_area_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to service.practice_area. Business justification: Service tiers are defined per practice area. Normalize by linking to practice_area master. The practice_area STRING column is just a code/name reference.']
  segment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to client.client_segment. Business justification: Service tiers explicitly target client segments (premium tier for top-tier clients, standard for mid-market). Tier eligibility, SLA commitments, and pricing are segment-driven. Removes denormalized cl']
  afa_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this tier is eligible for Alternative Fee Arrangements (AFA) such as fixed fees, capped fees, or success-based pricing. True if AFA options are available; False if only hourly billing applies.']
  afa_models_supported varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of AFA models supported for this tier (e.g., Fixed Fee, Capped Fee, Blended Rate, Success Fee, Retainer). Used in pricing discussions and engagement structuring.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date on which this service tier configuration was approved. Used for governance and audit trail.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval status of this service tier configuration. Only approved tiers are available for engagement assignment. Used in governance workflow.']
  approved_by_partner varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the partner who approved this service tier configuration. Used for governance and accountability.']
  base_hourly_rate decimal(10,2) [note: 'Standard hourly billing rate for this tier in the firms base currency. Used as the starting point for rate negotiations and engagement pricing. May vary by timekeeper seniority within the tier.']
  conflict_check_level varchar [note: 'Level of conflict checking required for engagements under this tier (standard, enhanced, comprehensive). Higher tiers may require more rigorous conflict analysis and ethical wall procedures.']
  created_by_user varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the user who created this service tier record. Used for audit trail and accountability.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this service tier record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the base hourly rate and pricing (e.g., USD, GBP, EUR). Used in multi-currency billing and financial reporting.']
  data_protection_classification varchar [note: 'Data protection and confidentiality classification level for matters under this tier (standard, confidential, highly_confidential). Determines document handling, access controls, and encryption requirements.']
  document_retention_years varchar [note: 'Minimum document retention period in years for matters under this tier, as required by regulatory obligations and professional standards. Used in records management and DMS configuration.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date on which this service tier is no longer available for new engagements. Null for currently active tiers. Used in service catalog versioning and deprecation management.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date from which this service tier becomes available for new engagements. Used in service catalog versioning and pricing transitions.']
  included_deliverables varchar [note: 'Comma-separated or structured list of standard deliverables included in the tier (e.g., legal opinions, memoranda, contract drafts, compliance checklists). Used in RFP responses and engagement scoping.']
  insurance_coverage_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether specific professional indemnity insurance coverage or coverage limits are required for engagements under this tier. True if additional coverage is mandatory; False otherwise.']
  kyc_aml_level varchar [note: 'Level of KYC and AML due diligence required for clients engaging under this tier (basic, standard, enhanced, high_risk). Higher tiers or higher-risk practice areas may require enhanced due diligence.']
  last_modified_by_user varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the user who last modified this service tier record. Used for audit trail and accountability.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this service tier record was last modified. Used for audit trail and change tracking.']
  matter_type_applicability varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of matter types or case categories to which this tier applies (e.g., Transactional, Litigation, Advisory, Compliance). Used in matter intake and service assignment.']
  maximum_engagement_value decimal(15,2) [note: 'Maximum total engagement value or cap applicable to this tier (if any). Null for uncapped tiers. Used in pricing and risk management.']
  minimum_engagement_value decimal(15,2) [note: 'Minimum total engagement value or retainer amount required to qualify for this tier. Used in client segmentation and engagement acceptance decisions.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments regarding this service tier, including special terms, exceptions, or usage guidance. Used by practice managers and business development teams.']
  partner_approval_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether partner-level approval is required to assign a matter to this tier. True if approval is mandatory; False if associates or practice managers can assign. Used in engagement workflow and governance.']
  practice_area varchar [note: 'Primary practice area or legal service line to which this tier applies (e.g., M&A, Litigation, IP Counsel, Regulatory Compliance, Employment Law). Aligns with firm practice area taxonomy. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: M&A|Litigation|IP Counsel|Regulatory Compliance|Employment Law|Corporate Transactions|Dispute Resolution|Real Estate|Tax|Labor & Employment — promote to reference product]']
  response_time_commitment_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'Maximum response time commitment in hours for initial client inquiries or urgent requests under this tier. Forms part of the Service Level Agreement (SLA) terms.']
  scope_of_coverage varchar [note: 'Detailed definition of the legal services and deliverables included in this tier (e.g., advisory opinions, document review, representation in proceedings, regulatory filings). Used to set client expectations and scope engagement letters (LOE).']
  sla_terms varchar [note: 'Full text or reference to the Service Level Agreement (SLA) terms applicable to this tier, including response times, escalation procedures, and performance guarantees. Used in engagement letters (LOE) and client contracts.']
  tier_code varchar [note: 'Short alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the service tier (e.g., STD, PREM, ENT, BESPOKE). Used in system integrations and billing configurations.']
  tier_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the service tier, including scope of coverage, key differentiators, and target client segments. Used in business development materials and RFP responses.']
  tier_level varchar [note: 'Numeric ranking of the tier within the service hierarchy (e.g., 1=Standard, 2=Premium, 3=Enterprise, 4=Bespoke). Used for sorting and eligibility logic.']
  tier_name varchar [note: 'Full business name of the service tier (e.g., Standard, Premium, Enterprise, Bespoke). Used in client-facing documents, RFPs, and engagement letters (LOE).']
  tier_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the service tier. Active tiers are available for new engagements; deprecated tiers are honored for existing matters but not offered to new clients.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version number of this service tier configuration. Incremented with each substantive change to tier terms, pricing, or scope. Used in change management and historical analysis.']

  Note: 'Defines the tiered packaging levels for legal services (e.g., Standard, Premium, Enterprise, Bespoke). Each tier specifies the scope of coverage, included deliverables, response commitments, and eligibility for alternative fee arrangements (AFA). Used to structure RFP responses and engagement letter (LOE) terms. Enables differentiated pricing and SLA assignment across client segments.'
}

Table service.pricing_model {
  pricing_model_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the pricing model record. Primary key.']
  attorney_profile_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the partner who approved this pricing model for use. Null if not yet approved or approval not required.']
  category_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to risk.risk_category. Business justification: AFA pricing models require risk classification (contingency = outcome risk, fixed fee = scope risk, capped fee = budget overrun risk). Pricing committees evaluate risk category before approving non-st']
  legal_service_id varchar [note: 'FK to service.legal_service.legal_service_id — Pricing models are configured per legal service offering. Each service has one or more applicable pricing models (hourly, fixed, capped, etc.).']
  model_owner_timekeeper_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user who created this pricing model record.']
  pricing_last_modified_by_user_timekeeper_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user who last modified this pricing model record.']
  segment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to client.client_segment. Business justification: Pricing models are often segment-specific (volume discounts for enterprise clients, standard rates for individuals). Rate cards, AFA eligibility, and discount structures are determined by client segme']
  advance_payment_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Required advance payment or retainer deposit amount. Null if no advance payment is required.']
  advance_payment_required boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether an advance payment or retainer deposit is required before work commences under this pricing model. True = advance required; False = no advance.']
  afa_indicator boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this pricing model represents an Alternative Fee Arrangement (non-traditional hourly billing). True = AFA; False = traditional hourly.']
  applicable_jurisdictions varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of jurisdictions where this pricing model is approved for use (e.g., USA, GBR, CAN). Ensures compliance with local bar association fee rules.']
  applicable_practice_areas varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of practice areas where this pricing model is typically applied (e.g., M&A, Corporate Transactions, Litigation, Dispute Resolution). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: litigation|corporate|ip|employment|regulatory|real_estate|tax|bankruptcy|family|immigration — promote to reference product]']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date on which this pricing model was approved by authorized partner or management. Null if not yet approved.']
  approval_required boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether partner or management approval is required before applying this pricing model to a matter. True = approval required; False = no approval needed.']
  base_rate_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'The standard rate amount for this pricing model. For hourly models, this is the hourly rate; for fixed fee, this is the base fee amount. Null if rate varies by timekeeper or matter.']
  billing_increment_minutes varchar [note: 'Time increment used for rounding billable time entries (e.g., 6 minutes = 0.1 hour, 15 minutes = 0.25 hour). Common values: 6, 10, 15.']
  blended_rate_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Single averaged hourly rate applied across all timekeepers on a matter, regardless of individual seniority. Used in blended rate pricing models.']
  calculation_basis varchar [note: 'The foundational method used to calculate fees under this model. Time-based = hours worked; Matter-based = per matter/case; Transaction Value = percentage of deal value; Outcome-based = tied to result; Hybrid = combination of methods.']
  contingency_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'The percentage of recovery or transaction value that constitutes the fee for contingency-based pricing models. Null for non-contingency models.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this pricing model record was first created in the system.']
  discount_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Standard discount percentage applied to standard rates under this pricing model. Null if no discount applies.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date on which this pricing model expires or is no longer available for new matters. Null for open-ended models.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date from which this pricing model becomes effective and available for use in client engagements.']
  expense_markup_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage markup applied to reimbursable expenses (e.g., 10% administrative fee on disbursements). Null if no markup applies.']
  expense_reimbursement_policy varchar [note: 'Policy governing how disbursements and expenses are handled under this pricing model. Full = all expenses billed separately; Capped = expenses up to limit; Included = expenses absorbed in fee; No Reimbursement = client pays directly.']
  internal_notes varchar [note: 'Internal notes and comments about this pricing model for firm use only. May include strategic considerations, client negotiation history, or profitability analysis.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this pricing model record was last updated.']
  late_payment_interest_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Annual interest rate applied to overdue invoices under this pricing model. Null if no late payment interest applies.']
  ledes_format_version varchar [note: 'LEDES billing format version supported by this pricing model (e.g., LEDES1998BI, LEDES1998BIV2). Ensures billing data compatibility with client systems.']
  maximum_fee_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'The maximum fee cap for capped fee arrangements. Fees will not exceed this amount regardless of time spent. Null if no cap applies.']
  minimum_fee_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'The minimum fee that will be charged under this pricing model, regardless of time or effort expended. Null if no minimum applies.']
  model_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier code for the pricing model, used in billing systems and client communications.']
  model_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the pricing model (e.g., Standard Hourly Billing, Fixed Fee M&A Advisory, Blended Rate Litigation).']
  model_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the fee structure type. Hourly = time-based billing; Fixed Fee = predetermined amount; Capped Fee = hourly with maximum limit; Blended Rate = averaged rate across timekeepers; Retainer = recurring periodic fee; Contingency = success-based percentage.']
  payment_terms_days varchar [note: 'Standard payment terms in days from invoice date (e.g., 30 days net). Defines when payment is due under this pricing model.']
  pricing_model_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the pricing model, including its intended use cases, calculation methodology, and any special terms or conditions.']
  pricing_model_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the pricing model. Active = in use; Inactive = not available for new matters; Draft = under development; Archived = historical; Under Review = pending approval; Approved = ready for activation.']
  rate_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the rate amount (e.g., USD, GBP, EUR).']
  retainer_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'The recurring retainer fee amount charged per billing period. Null for non-retainer models.']
  retainer_frequency varchar [note: 'Billing frequency for retainer-based pricing models. Indicates how often the retainer fee is charged.']
  sla_resolution_time_days varchar [note: 'Target resolution or delivery time in days for matters under this pricing model. Part of service level commitments.']
  sla_response_time_hours varchar [note: 'Guaranteed response time in hours for client inquiries under this pricing model. Part of service level commitments.']
  success_fee_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Fixed or additional fee payable upon successful outcome (e.g., case won, deal closed). Null if no success fee applies.']
  utbms_task_code varchar [note: 'UTBMS task code alignment for this pricing model, enabling standardized billing code reporting (e.g., L110 for Case Assessment, L310 for Discovery).']

  Note: 'Defines the fee structures and alternative fee arrangements (AFA) applicable to legal service offerings, including hourly billing, fixed fee, capped fee, blended rate, retainer, contingency, and success fee models. Each record captures the model type, calculation basis, currency, applicable jurisdictions, and LEDES/UTBMS billing code alignment. Serves as the authoritative pricing configuration referenced by billing and matter domains.'
}

Table service.rate_card {
  rate_card_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the rate card record. Primary key.']
  matter_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific matter for which this rate applies. Null indicates the rate applies at the client level or as a standard rate.']
  primary_practice_area_id varchar [note: 'FK to service.practice_area.practice_area_id — Rate cards are structured by practice area (litigation rates differ from corporate rates). This is a fundamental billing configuration relationship.']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the client for whom this rate is defined. Null indicates a standard rate applicable to all clients unless overridden.']
  superseded_by_rate_card_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the rate card that supersedes this one. Null if this is the current active rate card.']
  timekeeper_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the timekeeper (attorney, paralegal, or other legal professional) to whom this rate applies.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date on which this rate card was approved for use.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval status of the rate card in the rate management workflow (draft = under construction, pending_approval = submitted for review, approved = active and usable, rejected = not approved, expired = past effective end date).']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the individual who approved this rate card (typically a partner, billing manager, or finance director).']
  billing_guideline_compliant boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this rate complies with the clients outside counsel billing guidelines (True = compliant, False = non-compliant or requires review).']
  client_segment varchar [note: 'Classification of the client segment to which this rate applies (e.g., Fortune 500, mid-market, small business, government, non-profit, individual).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this rate card record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which the rate is denominated (e.g., USD, GBP, EUR, JPY).']
  discount_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage discount applied to the standard rate to derive this rate (e.g., 10.00 for a 10% discount). Null if no discount applies.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'The date on which this rate ceases to be effective. Null indicates the rate is currently active with no defined end date.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'The date from which this rate becomes effective and applicable for billing purposes.']
  firm_office_location_code varchar [note: 'Code representing the office location from which the timekeeper operates (e.g., NYC, LON, SFO, HKG).']
  hourly_rate decimal(12,2) [note: 'The billing rate per hour for the timekeepers services under this rate card. Used for time-based billing calculations in Elite 3E and Aderant Expert.']
  is_active boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this rate card is currently active and available for use in billing (True = active, False = inactive).']
  is_default_rate boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this is the default rate to be applied when no client-specific or matter-specific rate is defined (True = default, False = not default).']
  jurisdiction_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code or state/province code representing the jurisdiction where the legal services are provided (e.g., USA, GBR, NY, CA).']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this rate card record was last updated or modified.']
  minimum_charge_increment_minutes varchar [note: 'Minimum time increment for billing purposes (e.g., 6 minutes for 0.1 hour increments, 15 minutes for 0.25 hour increments). Used in time entry rounding.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments regarding this rate card, including special terms, exceptions, or context for the rate structure.']
  rate_cap_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Maximum hourly rate cap agreed with the client, regardless of timekeeper seniority or standard rate. Null if no cap applies.']
  rate_card_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the rate card, including its purpose, scope, and any special terms or conditions.']
  rate_card_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name or label for this rate card (e.g., 2024 Standard Corporate Rates, Client XYZ Preferred Rates, Litigation Blended Rate).']
  rate_floor_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Minimum hourly rate floor below which billing cannot fall. Null if no floor applies.']
  rate_source varchar [note: 'Origin or basis for this rate (firm_standard = firms published standard rates, client_negotiated = negotiated with client, engagement_letter = specified in Letter of Engagement (LOE), rfp_response = proposed in RFP response, afa_agreement = part of Alternative Fee Arrangement, market_benchmark = based on market survey).']
  rate_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the billing rate structure (standard = firm standard rate, preferred = client-specific preferred rate, blended = averaged rate across multiple timekeepers, discounted = reduced rate, negotiated = custom negotiated rate, afa = Alternative Fee Arrangement rate).']
  rate_version varchar [note: 'Version number of this rate card, incremented with each revision or update. Used for rate history tracking and audit purposes.']
  seniority_level varchar [note: 'Seniority classification of the timekeeper (e.g., equity partner, non-equity partner, of counsel, senior associate, mid-level associate, junior associate, paralegal, legal assistant, contract attorney). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: equity_partner|non_equity_partner|of_counsel|senior_associate|mid_level_associate|junior_associate|paralegal|legal_assistant|contract_attorney — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  utbms_activity_code varchar [note: 'UTBMS activity code providing granular detail on the type of legal work performed under this rate (e.g., A101 for Plan and Prepare for Issue Development).']
  utbms_task_code varchar [note: 'UTBMS task code aligned with this rate for standardized legal billing and matter management (e.g., L110 for Case Assessment, L120 for Investigation).']

  Note: 'Stores the standard and negotiated timekeeper billing rates for legal services by practice area, seniority level (partner, associate, paralegal), jurisdiction, and client segment. Captures effective date ranges, currency, rate type (standard, preferred, blended), and UTBMS task code alignment. Feeds directly into Elite 3E and Aderant Expert rate management modules for WIP calculation and prebilling. Distinct from pricing_model which defines the fee structure type.'
}

Table service.sla_template {
  sla_template_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the SLA template. Primary key.']
  legal_service_id varchar [note: 'FK to service.legal_service.legal_service_id — SLA templates are defined per service offering — response time commitments for litigation differ from advisory. This link is required for LOE generation and matter SLA assignment.']
  offering_version_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the legal service offering catalog entry to which this SLA template is associated.']
  practice_area_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to service.practice_area. Business justification: SLA templates are scoped by practice area. Normalize by linking to practice_area master. The practice_area STRING column is a code/name reference.']
  pricing_model_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to service.pricing_model. Business justification: SLA templates may reference a pricing model. Normalize by linking to pricing_model master. The pricing_model STRING column is a code/name reference.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date on which this SLA template was formally approved for use in client engagements.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval and lifecycle status of the SLA template within the firms governance process.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the partner or practice group leader who approved this SLA template for use.']
  availability_commitment varchar [note: 'Timekeeper availability commitment specified in the SLA (e.g., 24/7 emergency access, business hours only, named partner availability).']
  breach_count varchar [note: 'Historical count of SLA breaches across all matters using this template, used for template refinement and risk assessment.']
  breach_threshold_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage threshold over target time that constitutes an SLA breach (e.g., 110.00 means 10% over target triggers breach).']
  client_tier varchar [note: 'Client segmentation tier for which this SLA template is designed, reflecting service level differentiation in client relationship management.']
  communication_protocol varchar [note: 'Specified communication channels and protocols for SLA-governed matters (e.g., email, secure portal, phone, video conference, encrypted messaging).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this SLA template record was first created in the system.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date from which this SLA template becomes effective and available for use in new Letter of Engagement (LOE) drafting and matter setup.']
  escalation_threshold varchar [note: 'Threshold value (in same unit as response/turnaround time) at which SLA breach triggers escalation to senior counsel or practice group leader.']
  escalation_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for escalation threshold: hours, business days, or calendar days.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date on which this SLA template expires or is superseded by a newer version. Null indicates no expiration.']
  fee_adjustment_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage fee adjustment (reduction or credit) applied upon SLA breach as specified in remediation terms.']
  jurisdiction varchar [note: 'Legal jurisdiction or geographic scope to which this SLA template applies (e.g., USA, GBR, multi-jurisdictional). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: USA|GBR|CAN|AUS|DEU|FRA|CHN|IND|multi_jurisdictional — promote to reference product]']
  matter_complexity varchar [note: 'Target matter complexity level for which this SLA template is appropriate, enabling proper SLA-to-matter matching.']
  modified_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or system that last modified this SLA template record.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this SLA template record was last modified, supporting change tracking and audit requirements.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional notes, guidance, or special instructions for applying this SLA template in client engagements and RFP responses.']
  practice_area varchar [note: 'Legal practice area taxonomy classification to which this SLA template applies (e.g., M&A, Litigation, IP Counsel, Regulatory Compliance, Employment Law). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: corporate|litigation|intellectual_property|employment|regulatory_compliance|real_estate|tax|antitrust|banking_finance|environmental — promote to reference product]']
  quality_standard varchar [note: 'Description of the quality standards and deliverable expectations associated with this SLA template (e.g., peer review required, cite-checking standards, document formatting requirements).']
  regulatory_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this SLA template includes specific regulatory compliance commitments (e.g., GDPR response times, SOX filing deadlines, CCPA data subject request turnaround).']
  remediation_terms varchar [note: 'Contractual remediation terms and client remedies in the event of SLA breach (e.g., fee reduction, credit, expedited service on next matter).']
  reporting_frequency varchar [note: 'Frequency of status reporting and matter updates committed in the SLA template.']
  response_time_target varchar [note: 'Target response time commitment in the unit specified by response_time_unit (e.g., 24 hours for initial client inquiry response).']
  response_time_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for response time target: hours, business days, or calendar days.']
  sla_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the SLA template by commitment category: matter response time, document turnaround, court filing deadline adherence, regulatory submission timeliness, client communication response, or legal research delivery.']
  template_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier code for the SLA template used in Letter of Engagement (LOE) drafting and Request for Proposal (RFP) responses.']
  template_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the SLA template including scope, applicability, and business context for use in client engagements.']
  template_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the SLA template describing the service commitment level.']
  turnaround_time_target varchar [note: 'Target turnaround time for deliverable completion in the unit specified by turnaround_time_unit (e.g., 5 business days for contract review).']
  turnaround_time_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for turnaround time target: hours, business days, calendar days, or weeks.']
  usage_count varchar [note: 'Number of times this SLA template has been applied to active matters or included in Letter of Engagement (LOE) documents, used for template effectiveness analysis.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version number of the SLA template following semantic versioning (e.g., 1.0, 2.1) for template change management and audit trail.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or system that created this SLA template record.']

  Note: 'Defines the service level agreement (SLA) templates governing response times, turnaround commitments, escalation thresholds, and quality standards for each legal service offering. Captures SLA type (matter response, document turnaround, court filing deadline, regulatory submission), target metrics, breach thresholds, and remediation terms. Used in LOE drafting, RFP responses, and matter management SLA monitoring. Distinct from individual matter-level SLA instances.'
}

Table service.package {
  package_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the service package. Primary key.']
  appetite_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to risk.risk_appetite. Business justification: Service packages are designed within firm risk appetite boundaries. Package approval workflows verify that included services and pricing terms align with risk tolerance thresholds. Prevents offering p']
  package_last_modified_by_user_timekeeper_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the user who most recently modified this service package record.']
  package_owner_timekeeper_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the user who created this service package record in the system.']
  practice_area_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to service.practice_area. Business justification: Service packages are scoped by practice area. Normalize by linking to practice_area master. The practice_area STRING column is a code/name reference.']
  pricing_model_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to service.pricing_model. Business justification: Service packages reference a pricing model via pricing_basis attribute (synonym in legal services context). Normalize by linking to pricing_model master.']
  primary_package_approved_by_partner_timekeeper_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the partner who provided final approval for this service package to be offered to clients.']
  primary_superseded_by_package_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the newer service package that replaces this one, enabling version lineage tracking and client migration paths.']
  active_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the service package is currently active and available for new client engagements. True = active and available.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval workflow status of the service package within internal governance processes.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this service package received final approval and became available for client engagements.']
  average_engagement_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Average revenue per engagement for this service package calculated from historical billing data. Used for forecasting and business development.']
  base_price_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Standard base price for the service package before any client-specific adjustments or discounts. Used as starting point for pricing negotiations.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this service package record was first created in the system.']
  cross_sell_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this package is eligible for cross-selling to existing clients with other active engagements. True = eligible for cross-sell campaigns.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the base price amount (e.g., USD, GBP, EUR).']
  document_management_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this package requires use of firm DMS (iManage Work or NetDocuments) for document storage, version control, and collaboration.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date after which this service package is no longer available for new engagements. Null indicates no planned end date.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date from which this service package becomes available for client engagements and RFP responses.']
  estimated_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'Estimated total professional hours required to deliver all services within the package. Used for resource planning and capacity management.']
  includes_ediscovery_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the package includes electronic discovery services using platforms such as Relativity for document review and production.']
  includes_legal_research_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the package includes access to legal research platforms such as LexisNexis or Thomson Reuters Practical Law.']
  internal_notes varchar [note: 'Internal commentary, pricing rationale, competitive positioning notes, or strategic considerations for firm use only.']
  jurisdiction_coverage varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of jurisdictions or countries where this package can be delivered based on firm licensing and practice rights (e.g., USA, GBR, DEU).']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this service package record was most recently updated.']
  marketing_collateral_url varchar [note: 'URL or document path to client-facing marketing materials, brochures, or pitch decks for this service package.']
  minimum_engagement_term_months varchar [note: 'Minimum commitment period in months required for this package. Null indicates no minimum term requirement.']
  package_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique business identifier for the service package used in RFP responses, client proposals, and billing systems.']
  package_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the service package scope, objectives, and value proposition for client-facing materials and internal knowledge management.']
  package_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the service package (e.g., Corporate Governance Bundle, M&A Full-Service Package, IP Portfolio Management Suite).']
  package_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the service package structure determining engagement model and delivery approach.']
  practice_area varchar [note: 'Primary practice area taxonomy classification for the service package aligning with firm organizational structure and expertise domains.']
  pricing_basis varchar [note: 'Pricing model for the package. AFA = Alternative Fee Arrangement. Determines how the package is billed to clients.']
  regulatory_framework varchar [note: 'Primary regulatory or compliance framework applicable to services within this package (e.g., GDPR, SOX, CCPA, FRAND).']
  requires_conflict_check_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether engagement under this package mandates formal conflict checking through Intapp Conflicts or equivalent system before matter intake.']
  requires_kyc_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this package requires formal KYC and AML compliance checks before engagement commencement per regulatory obligations.']
  sla_resolution_time_days varchar [note: 'Target resolution time in business days for standard matters covered by this package under SLA commitments.']
  sla_response_time_hours varchar [note: 'Maximum response time in hours guaranteed under the SLA terms for client inquiries or urgent matters within this package.']
  target_client_segment varchar [note: 'Intended client segment for this package enabling targeted business development and marketing campaigns.']
  total_engagements_count varchar [note: 'Cumulative count of client engagements or matters that have utilized this service package since inception. Used for performance tracking.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version number of the service package definition enabling tracking of changes to scope, pricing, or terms over time.']

  Note: 'Defines bundled legal service packages combining multiple legal_service offerings into structured advisory packages (e.g., Corporate Governance Bundle, M&A Full-Service Package, IP Portfolio Management Suite). Modeled as header+line: header captures package name, target client segment, minimum engagement term, pricing basis, cross-sell eligibility, and active/inactive status; line-level rows specify each constituent legal_service, its quantity or scope allocation within the package, sequencing order, optionality (mandatory vs. elective component), and any component-level pricing override. This is the SSOT for all package composition data. Enables cross-selling, structured RFP responses, and downstream matter-to-service mapping.'
}

Table service.jurisdiction_coverage {
  jurisdiction_coverage_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the jurisdiction coverage record. Primary key.']
  jurisdiction_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key to matter.jurisdiction.jurisdiction_id']
  primary_legal_service_id varchar [note: 'FK to service.legal_service.legal_service_id — Jurisdictional availability is defined per service offering. A firm may offer M&A advisory in the US and UK but not in jurisdictions where it lacks local counsel. Critical for RFP eligibility.']
  admission_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether formal admission or bar membership is required to practice in this jurisdiction.']
  admission_requirements varchar [note: 'Detailed description of admission or licensing requirements for practicing in this jurisdiction (e.g., bar exam, character and fitness review, Continuing Legal Education requirements).']
  adr_services_available boolean [note: 'Indicates whether Alternative Dispute Resolution services (arbitration, mediation, conciliation) are available in this jurisdiction.']
  aml_kyc_compliance_available boolean [note: 'Indicates whether AML and KYC compliance counseling services are available in this jurisdiction.']
  conflict_check_scope varchar [note: 'Indicates the scope of conflict checking required for this jurisdiction coverage: full (comprehensive conflict check required), limited (partial check), or excluded (no conflict check needed).']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code for the jurisdiction (e.g., USA, GBR, CAN).']
  court_access_level varchar [note: 'Level of court access available in this jurisdiction: full (can appear in all courts), limited (specific courts only), none (no court appearances), advisory_only (counsel only, no litigation).']
  coverage_notes varchar [note: 'Additional notes, comments, or guidance regarding service coverage in this jurisdiction, including special considerations, recent regulatory changes, or operational caveats.']
  coverage_status varchar [note: 'Current status of service coverage in this jurisdiction. Active indicates full service availability; restricted indicates limited scope; suspended indicates temporary unavailability.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this jurisdiction coverage record was first created in the system.']
  data_privacy_compliance_scope varchar [note: 'Description of data privacy and protection compliance counseling available in this jurisdiction (e.g., GDPR, CCPA, DPA, sector-specific privacy laws).']
  ecf_access boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the firm has Electronic Case Filing access in this jurisdiction for federal or state court filings.']
  effective_from_date date [note: 'Date from which the service coverage in this jurisdiction became or will become effective.']
  effective_to_date date [note: 'Date until which the service coverage in this jurisdiction remains valid. Null indicates indefinite coverage.']
  international_arbitration_venue boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this jurisdiction is recognized as an international arbitration venue under ICC, LCIA, or other international arbitration frameworks.']
  ip_portfolio_management_available boolean [note: 'Indicates whether IP portfolio management services (patent, trademark, copyright prosecution and maintenance) are available in this jurisdiction.']
  jurisdictional_restrictions varchar [note: 'Detailed description of any restrictions, caveats, or limitations on service delivery in this jurisdiction (e.g., cannot appear in court, advisory only, regulatory compliance counseling excluded).']
  language_requirements varchar [note: 'Languages in which legal services must or can be delivered in this jurisdiction (e.g., English, French, Spanish). Relevant for multi-lingual jurisdictions or international matters.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this jurisdiction coverage record was last modified.']
  local_counsel_notes varchar [note: 'Additional notes or guidance on local counsel requirements, including circumstances when local counsel may be waived or specific qualifications needed.']
  local_counsel_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether local counsel must be engaged for matters in this jurisdiction when the primary attorney is not admitted.']
  matter_staffing_priority varchar [note: 'Priority ranking for staffing matters in this jurisdiction (1 = highest priority, higher numbers = lower priority). Used for multi-jurisdictional matter staffing decisions.']
  multi_jurisdictional_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this coverage is part of a multi-jurisdictional service offering spanning multiple states, provinces, or countries.']
  pacer_access boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the firm has PACER access for retrieving court records in this jurisdiction.']
  patent_bar_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether patent bar admission (USPTO registration for patent prosecution) is required for IP services in this jurisdiction.']
  practice_area_restrictions varchar [note: 'Description of any practice area limitations or restrictions in this jurisdiction (e.g., certain IP matters require patent bar admission, criminal defense requires state bar membership).']
  pro_hac_vice_available boolean [note: 'Indicates whether pro hac vice admission (temporary admission for a specific case) is available in this jurisdiction for attorneys not admitted to the local bar.']
  pro_hac_vice_requirements varchar [note: 'Description of requirements and procedures for obtaining pro hac vice admission in this jurisdiction (e.g., motion filing, local counsel sponsorship, fee payment).']
  regulatory_body varchar [note: 'Name of the primary regulatory or licensing body governing legal practice in this jurisdiction (e.g., State Bar of California, Solicitors Regulation Authority, American Bar Association).']
  regulatory_compliance_scope varchar [note: 'Description of the regulatory compliance counseling scope available in this jurisdiction (e.g., GDPR compliance, SOX advisory, AML counseling, sector-specific regulations).']
  rfp_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this jurisdiction coverage qualifies the firm to respond to RFPs requiring service delivery in this jurisdiction.']
  state_province_code varchar [note: 'Standard code for the state or province within the country (e.g., NY, CA, ON). Null for country-level or international jurisdictions.']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or system that last updated this jurisdiction coverage record.']

  Note: 'Defines the jurisdictional scope in which each legal service offering is available and licensed for delivery. Captures country, state/province, regulatory body (ABA, SRA, IBA, state bar), admission requirements, local counsel requirements, and any jurisdictional restrictions or caveats. Critical for conflict checking, RFP eligibility, and regulatory compliance counseling scope definition. Enables multi-jurisdictional matter staffing decisions.'
}

Table service.offering_version {
  offering_version_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for this version of the service offering. Primary key for the service offering version record.']
  legal_service_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent service offering entity. Links this version to the master service offering record.']
  offering_created_by_user_timekeeper_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the user who created this service offering version record. Links to workforce/user master data for audit purposes.']
  offering_last_modified_by_user_timekeeper_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the user who last modified this service offering version record. Links to workforce/user master data for audit purposes.']
  practice_area_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to service.practice_area. Business justification: Service offering versions are scoped by practice area. Normalize by linking to practice_area master. Remove practice_area_name as it can be retrieved via JOIN to practice_area.name.']
  pricing_model_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to service.pricing_model. Business justification: Service offering versions reference a pricing model. Normalize by linking to pricing_model master. The pricing_model STRING column is a code/name reference.']
  superseded_offering_version_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the previous service offering version that this version replaces. Nullable for initial versions. Enables version lineage tracking.']
  version_owner_timekeeper_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the partner or senior lawyer who approved this service offering version for use. Links to workforce/partner master data.']
  active_matters_count varchar [note: 'Current count of active matters using this service offering version. Supports impact analysis for version deprecation decisions.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date when this service offering version received formal approval for use in client engagements.']
  approval_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this service version requires formal approval from practice group leadership or management committee before use in client engagements.']
  archive_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this service offering version has been archived and is no longer available for selection in operational systems, while retained for historical audit and compliance review.']
  change_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code indicating why this new version was created. Supports audit trail and version history analysis. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: initial|pricing_update|scope_expansion|scope_reduction|regulatory_change|market_adjustment|client_feedback|quality_improvement|error_correction — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  change_reason_description varchar [note: 'Detailed explanation of the business rationale and specific changes made in this version compared to the previous version.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this service offering version record was first created in the system. Audit trail for version history.']
  deliverables_list varchar [note: 'Structured list or description of tangible outputs and work products included in this service offering (e.g., opinion letters, drafted agreements, research memoranda, court filings).']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when this version ceases to be valid for new engagements. Nullable for currently active versions with no planned end date.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when this version becomes valid and available for use in new engagements. Marks the beginning of the versions active period.']
  engagement_letter_template_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the standard Letter of Engagement (LOE) template document that should be used when this service offering is sold.']
  estimated_hours_max decimal(10,2) [note: 'Maximum estimated hours required to deliver this service. Provides upper bound for resource allocation and budget planning.']
  estimated_hours_min decimal(10,2) [note: 'Minimum estimated hours required to deliver this service. Used for scoping, resource planning, and client expectation setting.']
  exclusions_description varchar [note: 'Explicit statement of what is NOT included in this service offering version. Clarifies scope boundaries and manages client expectations.']
  jurisdiction_scope varchar [note: 'Geographic or legal jurisdictions covered by this service offering (e.g., England and Wales, New York State, Federal US, Multi-jurisdictional). Critical for regulatory compliance and professional licensing.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this service offering version record. Supports change tracking and audit compliance.']
  matter_type_applicability varchar [note: 'Types of legal matters for which this service offering is appropriate. Enables service-to-matter mapping for business development and Request for Proposal (RFP) responses.']
  prerequisites_description varchar [note: 'Required conditions, client inputs, or prior steps that must be satisfied before this service can be delivered (e.g., completed conflict check, signed engagement letter, provided documents).']
  pricing_model varchar [note: 'Fee structure methodology for this service version. Includes traditional hourly billing, Alternative Fee Arrangement (AFA) models, and hybrid approaches. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: hourly|fixed_fee|contingency|retainer|afa_capped|afa_blended|afa_success|value_based|hybrid — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  rate_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the standard rate amount (e.g., USD, GBP, EUR).']
  regulatory_compliance_notes varchar [note: 'Documentation of how this service version complies with applicable regulatory requirements, bar association rules, and professional conduct standards.']
  rfp_template_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to standard Request for Proposal (RFP) response templates or proposal documents associated with this service offering version.']
  risk_rating varchar [note: 'Professional indemnity and malpractice risk assessment for this service offering. Informs insurance coverage requirements and quality control procedures.']
  service_scope_description varchar [note: 'Comprehensive textual description of what is included in this service offering version. Details deliverables, activities, and boundaries of the service.']
  service_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the nature of legal service provided. Distinguishes between advisory, transactional work, dispute resolution, and other service modalities. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: advisory|transactional|litigation|compliance|research|drafting|representation|portfolio_management — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  sla_delivery_time_days varchar [note: 'Committed delivery timeframe in days for completion of standard service deliverables. Part of the Service Level Agreement (SLA) terms.']
  sla_response_time_hours varchar [note: 'Committed response time in hours for initial client inquiries or matter initiation under this service offering. Part of the Service Level Agreement (SLA) terms.']
  sla_terms_description varchar [note: 'Detailed textual description of all Service Level Agreement (SLA) commitments, performance metrics, and remedies for this service version.']
  standard_rate_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Base rate or fee amount for this service version in the firms standard currency. For hourly models, represents the standard hourly rate; for fixed-fee models, represents the package price.']
  target_client_segment varchar [note: 'Intended client profile or market segment for this service offering (e.g., Fortune 500 Corporates, Mid-Market Private Equity, Government Entities, Individual High Net Worth).']
  version_name varchar [note: 'Business-friendly name or label for this version (e.g., Standard M&A Package 2024, Enhanced IP Counsel Q1). Used in client-facing documents and internal communications.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Semantic version number of this service offering iteration (e.g., 1.0, 2.1, 3.0.1). Follows semantic versioning convention to track major and minor changes.']
  version_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of this service offering version. Tracks progression from draft through approval to active use and eventual retirement. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|pending_approval|approved|active|deprecated|retired|superseded — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']

  Note: 'Tracks the versioned lifecycle of legal service definitions, capturing changes to scope, pricing model, SLA terms, and practice area classification over time. Each version record preserves the full service definition snapshot at a point in time with effective start/end dates, change reason, and approval status. Enables historical audit of what was offered at the time of any given engagement letter or matter opening. Supports regulatory compliance and professional indemnity review.'
}

Table service.matter_service_mapping {
  matter_service_mapping_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the matter-service mapping record. Primary key.']
  clearance_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the conflict clearance record that authorized this service engagement, ensuring ethical compliance.']
  guideline_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the client-specific billing guideline that governs invoicing rules, rate caps, and approval workflows for this service.']
  letter_of_engagement_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the Letter of Engagement (LOE) document that formalizes the terms of this service mapping.']
  mapping_owner_timekeeper_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user who created this service mapping record.']
  matter_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the legal matter to which this service mapping applies. Links to the matter entity.']
  matter_last_modified_by_user_timekeeper_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user who last modified this service mapping record.']
  package_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the bundled service package (if applicable) that groups multiple legal services for this matter.']
  practice_area_id varchar [note: 'FK to service.practice_area']
  primary_legal_service_id varchar [note: 'FK to service.legal_service.legal_service_id — The mapping must reference which legal_service is being delivered on a matter. This is the operational bridge between catalog and execution.']
  primary_matter_approved_by_timekeeper_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the timekeeper (partner or senior attorney) who approved this service mapping.']
  primary_pricing_model_id varchar [note: 'FK to service.pricing_model.pricing_model_id — The mapping must capture which pricing model applies to this specific matter-service combination, as it may differ from the service default due to negotiation.']
  primary_sla_template_id varchar [note: 'FK to service.sla_template.sla_template_id — Each matter-service mapping must reference the SLA template in effect for that engagement, enabling SLA monitoring and breach detection.']
  tertiary_matter_lead_associate_timekeeper_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the lead associate assigned to execute the service work for this matter.']
  tier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to service.service_tier. Business justification: Matter service mappings reference a service tier. Normalize by linking to service_tier master. The service_tier STRING column is a code/name reference.']
  amendment_count varchar [note: 'The number of post-opening amendments made to this service mapping. Tracks scope changes and service adjustments.']
  approval_date date [note: 'The date on which this service mapping was approved.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval status of the service mapping. Indicates whether the mapping is draft, pending approval, approved for use, rejected, suspended, or terminated.']
