  billing_frequency varchar [note: 'The frequency at which invoices are generated for this service mapping (monthly, quarterly, milestone-based, on completion, or ad hoc).']
  budget_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The budgeted amount allocated for this service on this matter. Used for cost tracking and variance analysis.']
  budget_currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the budget amount (e.g., USD, GBP, EUR).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this service mapping record was first created in the system.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'The date on which this service mapping ceases to be active. Null indicates an open-ended mapping.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'The date from which this service mapping becomes active and work under this service can be billed and tracked.']
  estimated_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'Estimated total hours required to deliver this service for the matter. Used for resource planning and capacity management.']
  is_primary_service boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this is the primary service for the matter (true) or a secondary/ancillary service (false).']
  last_amendment_date date [note: 'The date of the most recent amendment to this service mapping. Null if no amendments have been made.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this service mapping record was last modified.']
  matter_pricing_model_fk varchar [note: 'FK to service.pricing_model.pricing_model_id — Each matter-service mapping records the agreed pricing model for that engagement. Required for billing code alignment and WIP calculation.']
  matter_sla_template_fk varchar [note: 'FK to service.sla_template.sla_template_id — Each matter-service mapping references the active SLA template governing service delivery commitments for that engagement.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes capturing additional context, special instructions, or exceptions related to this service mapping.']
  pricing_model varchar [note: 'The pricing model applied to this service mapping. Includes hourly billing, fixed fee, contingency, Alternative Fee Arrangement (AFA) capped, AFA blended rate, or retainer.']
  rfp_reference_number varchar [note: 'Reference number of the RFP that led to this service engagement, if applicable. Used for traceability to business development.']
  service_completion_date date [note: 'The date on which service delivery was completed for this mapping. Null if service is ongoing.']
  service_scope_description varchar [note: 'Detailed textual description of the agreed service scope at matter opening, outlining deliverables, exclusions, and boundaries.']
  service_start_date date [note: 'The actual date on which service delivery commenced for this mapping. May differ from effective_start_date.']
  service_tier varchar [note: 'The tier of service delivery (standard, premium, enterprise, custom) indicating the level of resource allocation and priority.']
  service_tier_fk varchar [note: 'FK to service.service_tier.service_tier_id — The mapping records which service tier was agreed for the engagement, determining scope and pricing.']
  utbms_activity_code varchar [note: 'UTBMS activity code aligned to this service mapping for granular work activity classification per LEDES standards.']
  utbms_task_code varchar [note: 'UTBMS task code aligned to this service mapping for standardized billing and work categorization per LEDES standards.']

  Note: 'Operational association linking active legal matters to the specific legal_service offerings and service_packages under which work is delivered. Each record captures the agreed service scope at matter opening, applied pricing_model, active sla_template, service_tier, effective dates, and approval status. Serves as the runtime bridge between the service catalog and matter execution, enabling service-level WIP attribution, SLA monitoring, billing code alignment per LEDES/UTBMS, and performance analytics by service type. Parent record for service_scope_amendment records that track post-opening changes.'
}

Table service.delivery_model {
  delivery_model_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the legal service delivery model. Primary key.']
  attorney_profile_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the partner or practice group leader responsible for governance and performance of this delivery model.']
  category_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to risk.risk_category. Business justification: Delivery models (offshore, nearshore, hybrid) carry distinct risk profiles: data security, regulatory compliance, quality control, ethical wall requirements. Risk assessments gate delivery model selec']
  timekeeper_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user who last modified this delivery model record.']
  applicable_practice_areas varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of legal practice areas for which this delivery model is suitable (e.g., M&A, Litigation, IP Prosecution, Regulatory Compliance, Employment Law).']
  applicable_service_types varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of service type codes or names that can be delivered using this model (e.g., advisory, transactional, dispute_resolution, compliance_counseling).']
  approval_authority varchar [note: 'Level of authority required to approve use of this delivery model for a new engagement: partner (individual partner discretion), practice_group_head (practice leader approval), managing_partner (firm leadership approval), executive_committee (board-level approval).']
  capacity_utilization_target_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target utilization rate (percentage of billable hours to total available hours) for resources operating under this delivery model, used for capacity planning and profitability analysis.']
  client_interaction_model varchar [note: 'Level and frequency of direct client engagement: high_touch (frequent partner/senior attorney interaction), medium_touch (periodic check-ins), low_touch (minimal direct contact), self_service (client portal-driven), or hybrid (varies by phase).']
  conflict_check_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether formal conflict checking is mandatory before engaging this delivery model (True for traditional attorney-led models, may be False for certain ALSP or administrative services).']
  cost_structure_basis varchar [note: 'Primary pricing and cost structure associated with this delivery model: hourly_rate (traditional billable hour), fixed_fee (project-based), capped_fee (not-to-exceed), contingency (success-based), retainer (ongoing access), subscription (periodic flat fee), outcome_based (performance-linked), or blended_rate (averaged hourly rate across team). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: hourly_rate|fixed_fee|capped_fee|contingency|retainer|subscription|outcome_based|blended_rate — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this delivery model record was first created in the system.']
  data_security_tier varchar [note: 'Level of data security and confidentiality controls applied to this delivery model: standard (baseline firm security), enhanced (additional encryption and access controls), maximum (air-gapped or highly restricted), client_specific (bespoke security per client requirements).']
  delivery_model_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the delivery model, including operational characteristics, typical use cases, and distinguishing features.']
  delivery_model_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the delivery model: active (in production use), inactive (temporarily suspended), pilot (under trial), deprecated (phasing out), retired (no longer offered).']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date on which this delivery model was retired or deprecated, if applicable. Null for currently active models.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date from which this delivery model became available for use in client engagements.']
  ethical_wall_support boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this delivery model supports the establishment of ethical walls (information barriers) to manage conflicts of interest (True for models with segregated teams, False for fully integrated teams).']
  fte_requirement_max decimal(8,2) [note: 'Maximum number of full-time equivalent staff typically allocated to this delivery model for scalability planning.']
  fte_requirement_min decimal(8,2) [note: 'Minimum number of full-time equivalent staff required to operate this delivery model effectively for a typical engagement.']
  innovation_maturity varchar [note: 'Maturity level of innovation and technology adoption in this delivery model: traditional (established methods), emerging (adopting new tools), innovative (leading-edge practices), cutting_edge (experimental or pioneering approaches).']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this delivery model record was last updated.']
  model_code varchar [note: 'Short alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the delivery model for business reference and system integration (e.g., TRAD_PARTNER, ALSP_HYBRID, MLS_ONSITE).']
  model_name varchar [note: 'Full business name of the delivery model (e.g., Traditional Partner-Led Team, Alternative Legal Service Provider (ALSP) Co-Delivery, Managed Legal Services).']
  model_type varchar [note: 'Primary classification of the delivery model: traditional (partner-led attorney teams), ALSP (Alternative Legal Service Provider co-delivery), MLS (Managed Legal Services), LPO (Legal Process Outsourcing), secondment (client-embedded counsel), or hybrid (combination of multiple models).']
  preferred_matter_size varchar [note: 'Optimal matter or engagement size for which this delivery model is best suited: small (low-value, short-duration), medium (moderate complexity and duration), large (high-value, extended engagement), enterprise (multi-year, strategic), or any (flexible across all sizes).']
  primary_delivery_geography varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code representing the primary geographic location from which services are delivered (e.g., USA, GBR, IND for offshore centers).']
  quality_assurance_approach varchar [note: 'Primary quality control mechanism for work delivered under this model: partner_review (senior attorney sign-off), peer_review (colleague validation), automated_qa (technology-driven checks), external_audit (third-party review), iso_certified (ISO 9001 quality management), or none (no formal QA).']
  regulatory_compliance_scope varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of regulatory frameworks and compliance requirements applicable to this delivery model (e.g., GDPR, CCPA, SOX, AML, Legal Professional Privilege (LPP), Solicitors Regulation Authority (SRA) standards).']
  resource_composition varchar [note: 'Textual description of the typical team composition, including ratios of partners, associates, paralegals, legal technologists, and support staff (e.g., 1 partner : 3 associates : 2 paralegals).']
  scalability_rating varchar [note: 'Assessment of how easily this delivery model can scale up or down to meet fluctuating demand: low (fixed team, limited flexibility), medium (moderate scaling with lead time), high (rapid scaling capability), elastic (on-demand scaling with minimal lead time).']
  secondary_delivery_geography varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code for secondary or backup delivery location, if applicable (e.g., nearshore support center).']
  sla_response_time_hours varchar [note: 'Standard response time commitment in hours for client inquiries under this delivery model, as defined in typical Service Level Agreements (SLAs).']
  sla_turnaround_time_days varchar [note: 'Standard turnaround time commitment in business days for deliverable completion under this delivery model, as defined in typical Service Level Agreements (SLAs).']
  staffing_model varchar [note: 'Defines the primary resource composition and leadership structure: partner-led (senior attorney oversight), associate-led (mid-level attorney execution), paralegal-led (support-staff driven), mixed-tier (blended seniority), offshore/nearshore/onshore (geographic staffing), or client-site (embedded at client location). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: partner_led|associate_led|paralegal_led|mixed_tier|offshore|nearshore|onshore|client_site — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  technology_enablement varchar [note: 'Degree of technology integration in the delivery model: manual (minimal tech), standard_tools (DMS and research platforms), advanced_automation (workflow automation and RPA), ai_assisted (AI/ML for document review, contract analysis), or full_digital (end-to-end digital delivery).']

  Note: 'Reference catalog of operational delivery models through which legal services are provided to clients. Covers traditional partner-led attorney teams, client secondments, ALSP (Alternative Legal Service Provider) co-delivery, managed legal services (MLS), legal process outsourcing (LPO), and hybrid models. Each record defines the staffing model type, resource composition, technology enablement approach, primary delivery geography, cost structure basis, and applicable service types. Referenced by legal_service definitions for default delivery configuration and used in capacity planning, RFP staffing proposals, and client reporting on delivery efficiency.'
}

Table service.scope_amendment {
  scope_amendment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the service scope amendment record. Primary key.']
  amendment_owner_timekeeper_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the user who created this amendment record.']
  attorney_profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the partner or senior lawyer who approved the amendment on behalf of the firm.']
  client_engagement_scope_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the original engagement scope that this amendment modifies.']
  matter_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the matter to which this scope amendment applies.']
  primary_scope_client_contact_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the contact (client or internal) who initiated or requested the scope amendment.']
  scope_modified_by_user_timekeeper_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the user who last modified this amendment record.']
  superseded_by_scope_amendment_id varchar [note: 'Reference to a subsequent amendment that supersedes or replaces this amendment. Null if this is the current active amendment.']
  amendment_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative describing the changes being made to the engagement scope, including business rationale and impact summary.']
  amendment_document_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier or Document Management System (DMS) path to the formal amendment document or Letter of Engagement (LOE) addendum.']
  amendment_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the amendment, typically sequential within a matter or engagement (e.g., AMD-001, AMD-002).']
  amendment_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code indicating the primary business reason for the amendment: scope creep (unplanned work expansion), client request (client-initiated change), regulatory change (compliance-driven), cost overrun (budget exceeded), timeline extension (schedule change), or service upgrade (enhanced service level).']
  amendment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the amendment: draft (being prepared), pending approval (awaiting client or partner sign-off), approved (authorized but not yet executed), rejected (declined), executed (in force), or superseded (replaced by a later amendment).']
  amendment_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the nature of the scope amendment: scope expansion (additional work added), scope reduction (work removed), fee renegotiation (pricing changes), SLA modification (service level changes), AFA restructuring (Alternative Fee Arrangement changes), or service addition (new service lines added).']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date on which the amendment was formally approved by the authorized partner or client representative.']
  billing_impact_notes varchar [note: 'Detailed notes on how this amendment affects billing arrangements, including changes to billing frequency, payment terms, or invoicing procedures.']
  client_approval_date date [note: 'Date on which the client formally approved the scope amendment.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this amendment record was first created in the system.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date from which the amended scope terms become binding and applicable to the matter.']
  execution_date date [note: 'Date on which the amendment was formally executed and became binding, typically when all parties have signed.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date on which the amendment terms expire or are scheduled for review, if applicable. Null for indefinite amendments.']
  fee_adjustment_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary value of the fee change resulting from this amendment. Positive for increases, negative for decreases, zero for no change.']
  fee_adjustment_currency varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the fee adjustment amount (e.g., USD, GBP, EUR).']
  fee_impact_type varchar [note: 'Classification of how the amendment affects fees: increase (higher fees), decrease (lower fees), no change (scope change without fee impact), or restructure (fee model changed).']
  indemnity_exposure_impact varchar [note: 'Assessment of how this amendment affects the firms professional indemnity insurance exposure: increased (higher risk), decreased (lower risk), unchanged (no material impact), or under review (being assessed).']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this amendment record was last modified.']
  original_scope_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier or clause number from the original Letter of Engagement (LOE) or engagement scope document that is being amended.']
  request_date date [note: 'Date on which the scope amendment was formally requested.']
  requested_by_party varchar [note: 'Indicates which party initiated the amendment request: client (client-driven), firm (law firm-driven), or mutual (jointly agreed).']
  revised_fee_arrangement_type varchar [note: 'The fee arrangement model after this amendment: hourly (time-based), fixed fee (flat rate), contingency (outcome-based), capped fee (not-to-exceed), blended rate (averaged hourly), success fee (performance bonus), or retainer (ongoing engagement). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: hourly|fixed_fee|contingency|capped_fee|blended_rate|success_fee|retainer — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  revised_sla_terms varchar [note: 'Description of the modified SLA terms, including response times, deliverable deadlines, and performance commitments.']
  risk_assessment_notes varchar [note: 'Internal notes documenting risk considerations, conflict implications, and professional responsibility issues arising from the scope amendment.']
  scope_change_summary varchar [note: 'Concise summary of the specific scope changes, including work added, removed, or modified.']
  sla_change_indicator boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this amendment includes changes to Service Level Agreement (SLA) terms (True) or not (False).']
  wip_adjustment_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary value of Work in Progress (WIP) adjustments resulting from this amendment (write-offs, re-ratings, or accruals).']
  wip_impact_assessment varchar [note: 'Assessment of how this amendment affects existing Work in Progress (WIP), including whether unbilled time will be written off, re-rated, or carried forward under new terms.']

  Note: 'Records formal amendments to the agreed scope of a legal service engagement after matter opening, including scope expansions, reductions, fee renegotiations, SLA modifications, and AFA restructuring. Each amendment captures the original scope reference, the nature of the change, the effective date, approval chain, and impact on the pricing model and WIP. Supports LOE amendment tracking and professional indemnity exposure management.'
}

Table service.eligibility_rule {
  eligibility_rule_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the service eligibility rule. Primary key for this entity.']
  eligibility_last_modified_by_timekeeper_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the user (timekeeper, business development manager, or system administrator) who most recently modified this eligibility rule record.']
  legal_service_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the legal service offering to which this eligibility rule applies (e.g., M&A advisory, litigation, IP counsel, regulatory compliance counseling).']
  practice_area_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key to service.practice_area.practice_area_id']
  primary_eligibility_timekeeper_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the partner, practice group leader, or business development manager responsible for defining and maintaining this eligibility rule.']
  rule_owner_timekeeper_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the user (timekeeper, business development manager, or system administrator) who created this eligibility rule record.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date on which this eligibility rule was formally approved for activation. Null if rule has not been approved or approval is not required.']
  approval_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether formal approval is required before this rule can be activated. True if approval workflow must be completed, false otherwise.']
  client_type_filter varchar [note: 'Pipe-separated list of client types to which this rule applies (e.g., corporate|government, individual, nonprofit). Empty if rule applies to all client types. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: corporate|government|individual|nonprofit|partnership|trust|estate|public_sector|private_sector — promote to reference product]']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this eligibility rule record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date after which this eligibility rule is no longer active. Null for open-ended rules that remain in effect indefinitely.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date from which this eligibility rule becomes active and is enforced during new business intake and RFP qualification processes.']
  enforcement_level varchar [note: 'Severity of the rule enforcement: hard block (prevents engagement from proceeding without exception), advisory warning (requires partner override to proceed), or soft warning (informational only, does not block).']
  evaluation_logic varchar [note: 'Structured expression or pseudo-code defining how the rule is evaluated during intake workflow. May reference client attributes, matter attributes, clearance outcomes, or external data sources.']
  external_reference_code varchar [note: 'Optional reference to an external policy document, regulatory requirement, or business standard that mandates or informs this eligibility rule (e.g., ABA Model Rule reference, SRA Code reference).']
  industry_sector_exclusion varchar [note: 'Pipe-separated list of industry sectors (e.g., NAICS or SIC codes) that are excluded from eligibility for this legal service. Empty if no sector exclusions apply.']
  jurisdiction_filter varchar [note: 'Pipe-separated list of jurisdictions (ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country codes or state/province codes) to which this rule applies. Empty if rule applies globally. Used to enforce jurisdictional licensing constraints.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this eligibility rule record was most recently updated. Used for audit trail and change tracking.']
  matter_value_threshold decimal(18,2) [note: 'Minimum estimated matter value (in base currency) required for the client or matter to qualify for the legal service. Null if no value threshold applies.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes providing additional context, implementation guidance, or historical information about this eligibility rule. Used by business development and intake teams.']
  override_authority_level varchar [note: 'The minimum authority level required to override this rule when enforcement level is advisory warning (e.g., partner, managing partner, practice group leader, general counsel). Null if rule cannot be overridden.']
  practice_area_filter varchar [note: 'Pipe-separated list of practice area codes or identifiers to which this rule applies (e.g., M&A, litigation, IP, employment). Empty if rule applies to all practice areas.']
  prerequisite_clearance_status_required varchar [note: 'The clearance status outcome required from the prerequisite clearance process for the rule to pass (e.g., approved, cleared, passed). Null if no prerequisite applies.']
  prerequisite_clearance_type varchar [note: 'Type of prerequisite clearance required before the legal service can be offered: conflict check (from conflict domain), KYC (Know Your Client), AML (Anti-Money Laundering), sanctions screening, or none if no prerequisite applies.']
  rule_code varchar [note: 'Business-assigned unique code for the eligibility rule, used for reference in intake workflows and RFP qualification processes.']
  rule_description varchar [note: 'Detailed business description of the eligibility rule, including the rationale, business context, and any special considerations for application during new business intake.']
  rule_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the eligibility rule, describing its purpose (e.g., Corporate Client Minimum Matter Value, Government Sector Exclusion).']
  rule_priority varchar [note: 'Numeric priority for rule evaluation order when multiple rules apply to the same service. Lower numbers indicate higher priority. Used by intake workflow engine to sequence rule checks.']
  rule_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the eligibility rule: active (enforced), inactive (not enforced), draft (under development), suspended (temporarily disabled), or retired (no longer used).']
  rule_type varchar [note: 'Category of eligibility rule defining the nature of the constraint: client-type restrictions (corporate, government, individual), minimum matter value thresholds, jurisdictional licensing constraints, industry sector exclusions, prerequisite clearance requirements (conflict check, KYC, AML), or practice area restrictions.']
  rule_version varchar [note: 'Version number of this eligibility rule, incremented with each substantive change to evaluation logic or enforcement parameters. Supports rule change tracking and audit.']
  threshold_currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the matter value threshold (e.g., USD, GBP, EUR). Null if no value threshold applies.']

  Note: 'Defines configurable business rules governing which clients or matters qualify for specific legal service offerings during new business intake. Rule types include client-type restrictions (corporate, government, individual), minimum matter value thresholds, jurisdictional licensing constraints, industry sector exclusions, and prerequisite clearance requirements. Each rule specifies evaluation logic, enforcement level (hard block preventing engagement vs. advisory warning requiring partner override), and the legal_service scope to which it applies. Consumed by the intake workflow engine during RFP qualification and matter opening. Note: this product defines the eligibility gating rules only — actual conflict-of-interest screening execution is owned by the conflict domain, and KYC/AML program management is owned by the compliance domain. This product references their clearance outcomes as prerequisite inputs.'
}

Table service.lpm_template {
  lpm_template_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the Legal Project Management template. Primary key.']
  attorney_profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the partner who owns and maintains this template, responsible for updates and quality.']
  knowledge_asset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to knowledge.knowledge_asset. Business justification: LPM templates reference specific knowledge assets as required inputs for matter phases and deliverables. Used in matter planning, resource estimation, and scope definition. Enables tracking which know']
  practice_area_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to service.practice_area. Business justification: LPM templates are scoped by practice area. Normalize by linking to practice_area master. The practice_area STRING column is a code/name reference.']
  primary_legal_service_id varchar [note: 'FK to service.legal_service.legal_service_id — LPM templates are created for specific service types. A litigation LPM template differs fundamentally from a transactional one. This link enables correct template selection at matter opening.']
  approval_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether formal approval is required before a matter team can instantiate this template for a new matter (True/False).']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the partner or practice group leader who approved this template for use.']
  approved_date date [note: 'Date when this template was formally approved for use in matter management.']
  budget_currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the estimated budget amount (e.g., USD, GBP, EUR).']
  client_reporting_frequency varchar [note: 'Standard frequency for client progress reporting defined in this template (Weekly, Bi-Weekly, Monthly, Quarterly, Milestone-Based, Ad-Hoc).']
  complexity_level varchar [note: 'Complexity classification of matters that should use this template, indicating resource requirements and risk profile (Low, Medium, High, Very High).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this template record was first created in the system.']
  deliverable_count varchar [note: 'Total number of standard deliverables (documents, reports, opinions) defined in this template.']
  document_template_references varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of document template IDs or codes that are standard deliverables for this LPM template.']
  effective_from_date date [note: 'Date from which this template version becomes effective and available for matter instantiation.']
  effective_to_date date [note: 'Date until which this template version remains effective; nullable for templates with no planned end date.']
  estimated_budget_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Standard estimated budget amount for matters using this template, used for initial scoping and client proposals.']
  estimated_duration_days varchar [note: 'Standard estimated duration in calendar days for matters following this template, from matter opening to closure.']
  fee_arrangement_type varchar [note: 'Default fee arrangement type recommended for matters using this template (Hourly, Fixed Fee, Contingency, Capped Fee, Blended Rate, Success Fee).']
  fte_requirement decimal(5,2) [note: 'Estimated total Full-Time Equivalent (FTE) resource requirement for the duration of a matter using this template.']
  jurisdiction_scope varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of jurisdictions or countries where this template is applicable (e.g., USA, GBR, EU, Global).']
  knowledge_article_references varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of knowledge management article IDs or practice notes that support this template.']
  last_modified_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the user who last modified this template record.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this template record was last modified in the system.']
  last_used_date date [note: 'Date when this template was most recently used to instantiate a new matter.']
  milestone_count varchar [note: 'Total number of key milestones defined across all phases in this template.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments about this template, including usage guidance, special considerations, or change history.']
  phase_count varchar [note: 'Total number of project phases defined in this template (e.g., Intake, Planning, Execution, Review, Closure).']
  practice_area varchar [note: 'Primary practice area classification for this template (e.g., Corporate, Litigation, Intellectual Property, Employment, Regulatory Compliance, Real Estate).']
  practice_group_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the practice group that owns and maintains this template (e.g., CORP, LIT, IP, EMP).']
  regulatory_framework varchar [note: 'Applicable regulatory frameworks or compliance requirements this template addresses (e.g., GDPR, SOX, CCPA, AML).']
  resource_role_requirements varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of required resource roles for this template (e.g., Partner, Senior Associate, Junior Associate, Paralegal, Legal Secretary).']
  risk_checkpoint_count varchar [note: 'Number of formal risk assessment checkpoints defined in the template for matter governance and quality control.']
  service_type varchar [note: 'Type of legal service this template supports (e.g., Advisory, Transactional, Contentious, Compliance, Due Diligence, Drafting).']
  sla_target_days varchar [note: 'Service Level Agreement (SLA) target duration in days for matter completion when using this template.']
  task_count varchar [note: 'Total number of standard tasks defined in the template task sequence.']
  template_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier code for the LPM template, used for quick reference and system integration (e.g., MA_STANDARD, LIT_COMPLEX, IP_PATENT).']
  template_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the LPM template, including its purpose, scope, typical use cases, and key deliverables.']
  template_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the LPM template describing the project structure and service type (e.g., Standard M&A Transaction Template, Complex Commercial Litigation Template).']
  template_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the LPM template (Draft, Active, Under Review, Deprecated, Archived).']
  usage_count varchar [note: 'Total number of matters that have been instantiated using this template, used for template effectiveness analysis.']
  utbms_task_codes varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of Uniform Task-Based Management System (UTBMS) task codes applicable to this template for standardized billing and reporting.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version identifier for the template following semantic versioning (e.g., 1.0, 2.1, 3.0.1), enabling version control and change tracking.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the user who created this template record.']

  Note: 'Stores Legal Project Management (LPM) plan templates associated with specific legal service offerings, defining standard phases, milestones, task sequences, resource role requirements, budget allocation by phase, and risk checkpoints. Each template provides a reusable project structure for matter teams to instantiate when opening a new matter under a given service type. Supports consistent delivery, budget control, and client reporting across matters of the same service type.'
}

Table service.line {
  line_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the service line. Primary key.']
  modified_by_user_timekeeper_id varchar [note: 'The identifier of the user who last modified this service line record. Used for audit trail and accountability tracking.']
  parent_line_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on line (parent_line_id)']
  average_matter_duration_days varchar [note: 'The typical duration in days from matter opening to closure for matters in this service line. Used for resource planning, WIP forecasting, and client expectation management.']
  average_matter_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'The average total billed value (in firm base currency) of matters in this service line. Used for pipeline forecasting, partner compensation modeling, and lateral hiring business case analysis.']
  billing_model_default varchar [note: 'The predominant billing model used by this service line. Hourly = traditional time-based billing. Fixed Fee = flat fee per matter. Contingency = success-based fee. AFA (Alternative Fee Arrangement) = value-based or outcome-based pricing. Hybrid = combination. Retainer = ongoing monthly/quarterly fee.']
  client_facing_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this service line is used in client-facing branding, marketing materials, and RFP responses (True) or is purely an internal financial reporting construct (False).']
  conflict_sensitivity_level varchar [note: 'The typical conflict-of-interest sensitivity for matters in this service line. High = frequent conflicts requiring ethical walls (e.g., M&A, litigation). Medium = occasional conflicts. Low = rare conflicts. Used to inform conflict checking protocols.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this service line record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  cross_selling_affinity_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'A calculated score (0-100) representing the likelihood of cross-selling opportunities between this service line and other service lines. Based on historical client engagement patterns. Used for business development targeting.']
  diversity_target_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'The firms diversity representation target (as a percentage) for this service lines attorney population. Used for DEI reporting, lateral hiring decisions, and client diversity scorecard requirements.']
  effective_date date [note: 'The date when this service line was officially established or became operational for financial reporting and matter assignment purposes.']
  end_date date [note: 'The date when this service line was discontinued or sunset. Null for active service lines. Used for historical P&L reporting and partner compensation reconciliation.']
  fte_count_current decimal(8,2) [note: 'The current total FTE headcount (partners, associates, paralegals, and support staff) allocated to this service line. Used for utilization analysis, capacity planning, and revenue per FTE calculations.']
  geographic_scope varchar [note: 'The geographic market scope of this service lines operations and client base. Local = single city/metro. Regional = multi-city within region. National = country-wide. International = multi-country. Global = worldwide presence.']
  insurance_coverage_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether specialized professional liability insurance coverage is required for this service line beyond the firms general malpractice policy (True) or not (False). Common for high-risk practices like securities litigation or IP prosecution.']
  knowledge_management_repository varchar [note: 'The primary knowledge management system or repository used by this service line for precedent documents, practice notes, and matter templates (e.g., Thomson Reuters Practical Law, iManage Work, internal KM system).']
  line_code varchar [note: 'Short alphanumeric code used internally for financial reporting, practice management systems, and partner compensation allocation (e.g., CORP, DISP, REG, TRANS, IP, EMP).']
  line_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the service lines scope, including the types of legal services, practice areas, and client matters it encompasses. Used for business development, RFP responses, and strategic planning.']
  line_name varchar [note: 'The official name of the service line as used in firm communications, financial reporting, and client-facing materials (e.g., Corporate Advisory, Disputes & Investigations, Regulatory & Government Affairs, Transactional, Intellectual Property, Employment & Labor).']
  line_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the service line. Active lines are fully operational with revenue targets and partner assignments. Inactive lines are no longer accepting new matters. Under review indicates strategic evaluation. Sunset indicates planned phase-out.']
  market_positioning varchar [note: 'The firms market positioning and competitive differentiation statement for this service line. Used in business development, RFP responses, and lateral partner recruitment.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this service line record was last modified. Used for audit trail, change tracking, and data quality monitoring.']
  partner_count varchar [note: 'The number of equity and non-equity partners primarily assigned to this service line. Used for RPE (Revenue Per Equity Partner) and PPP (Profit Per Partner) calculations.']
  practice_area_taxonomy varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list or structured taxonomy of practice areas that roll up into this service line (e.g., M&A, Securities, Private Equity for Corporate Advisory service line). Used for matter classification and lateral hiring decisions.']
  pro_bono_allocation_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'The target percentage of this service lines total hours to be allocated to pro bono work. Used for ABA pro bono reporting and associate development planning.']
  realization_rate_target decimal(5,2) [note: 'The target realization rate (percentage of standard rates actually collected) for this service line. Expressed as a percentage (e.g., 92.50 for 92.5%). Used for pricing strategy and partner performance evaluation.']
  regulatory_oversight_body varchar [note: 'Primary regulatory or professional body governing the legal services provided by this service line (e.g., SEC for securities practice, USPTO for patent prosecution, State Bar for general practice). Used for compliance tracking and CPD planning.']
  revenue_target_annual decimal(18,2) [note: 'The annual revenue target (in firm base currency) set for this service line during strategic planning. Used for partner compensation, performance evaluation, and resource allocation decisions.']
  strategic_priority_level varchar [note: 'The firms strategic investment priority for this service line. Tier 1 = high growth investment. Tier 2 = selective growth. Tier 3 = maintain current scale. Maintain = stable revenue focus. Divest = planned wind-down or sale.']
  technology_platform_primary varchar [note: 'The primary technology platform or specialized software used by this service line (e.g., Relativity for eDiscovery, LexisNexis for legal research, Thomson Reuters for transactional work). Used for IT budgeting and training planning.']

  Note: 'Defines the firms operational business lines that group legal service offerings for P&L reporting, partner compensation allocation, strategic planning, and client-facing branding. Each service line (e.g., Corporate Advisory, Disputes & Investigations, Regulatory & Government Affairs, Transactional) aggregates multiple practice areas and legal services into a commercially-managed unit with its own revenue targets, leadership structure, and market positioning. Distinct from practice_area (which is a professional taxonomy) — service_line is the business management layer used in firm financials, lateral hiring decisions, and strategic growth planning.'
}

Table service.delivery_configuration {
  delivery_configuration_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for this service-delivery model configuration. Primary key.']
  delivery_model_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the delivery model through which the service can be provided']
  legal_service_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the legal service offering being configured for delivery']
  practice_area_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key to service.practice_area.practice_area_id']
  configuration_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of this service-delivery configuration: active (in production use), inactive (temporarily disabled), pilot (testing phase), deprecated (being phased out).']
  cost_allocation_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of service delivery cost allocated to this specific delivery model when multiple models are used for the same service. Used in financial planning and client billing allocation.']
  created_date timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this service-delivery configuration was first created in the system.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date on which this service-delivery model combination was retired or deprecated. Null indicates currently active configuration.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date from which this service-delivery model combination became available for client engagements. Tracks when specific delivery configurations were activated.']
  last_modified_date timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this service-delivery configuration record.']
  preferred_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this delivery model is the preferred or recommended approach for delivering this specific service. Used in RFP responses and capacity planning to prioritize delivery model selection.']
  sla_override varchar [note: 'Service Level Agreement override or adjustment specific to this service-delivery model combination. Captures deviations from standard SLAs defined in either the service or delivery model when this specific pairing requires different commitments.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the operational configuration between legal services and delivery models. It captures which services can be delivered through which models, tracking effectiveness, cost allocation, and SLA overrides for each combination. Each record links one legal_service to one delivery_model with attributes that exist only in the context of this specific service-model pairing, used in capacity planning, RFP staffing proposals, and client delivery optimization.. Existence Justification: In legal services operations, firms actively manage which services can be delivered through which delivery models, creating a genuine many-to-many operational relationship. A single legal service (e.g., M&A advisory) can be delivered through multiple models (traditional partner-led, ALSP co-delivery, managed legal services) depending on client requirements, matter complexity, and cost constraints. Conversely, a single delivery model (e.g., client secondment) can be used to deliver multiple different legal services (litigation, IP counsel, regulatory compliance). The firm tracks the effectiveness, cost allocation, and SLA commitments for each specific service-model pairing.'
}

Table service.practice_area_delivery_configuration {
  practice_area_delivery_configuration_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for practice_area_delivery_configuration']
  delivery_configuration_id varchar [note: 'Primary key uniquely identifying each practice area delivery configuration record']
  delivery_model_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the delivery model through which the practice area can be delivered']
  practice_area_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the practice area being configured for delivery']
  capacity_allocation_fte decimal(8,2) [note: 'Number of full-time equivalent staff allocated to deliver this specific practice area through this specific delivery model. Used for capacity planning and resource forecasting. Explicitly identified in detection phase relationship data.']
  client_demand_rating varchar [note: 'Assessment of client demand for this practice area delivered through this delivery model, based on RFP requests, client inquiries, and market analysis.']
  configuration_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of this practice area-delivery model configuration. Active configurations are in production use, pilot configurations are being tested, inactive are temporarily suspended, and deprecated are retired.']
  cost_efficiency_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Calculated score (0-100) representing the cost efficiency of delivering this practice area through this delivery model, based on cost per matter, realization rates, and resource utilization.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this configuration record was first created in the system']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date on which this practice area-delivery model configuration was retired or deprecated. Null for currently active configurations. Explicitly identified in detection phase relationship data.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date from which this practice area-delivery model configuration became active and available for use in client engagements and capacity planning. Explicitly identified in detection phase relationship data.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this configuration record was last modified']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'Strategic importance of this practice area-delivery model combination to the firm. Strategic combinations receive priority in capacity allocation and investment.']
  quality_rating varchar [note: 'Assessment of the quality and effectiveness of delivering this practice area through this delivery model, based on client feedback, matter outcomes, and internal quality reviews. Explicitly identified in detection phase relationship data.']
  utilization_target_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target utilization rate (percentage of billable hours to total available hours) for resources delivering this practice area through this delivery model. Used for performance management and capacity optimization. Explicitly identified in detection phase relationship data.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the operational configuration between practice areas and delivery models in legal service delivery. It captures which practice areas can be delivered through which delivery models, along with capacity allocation, performance targets, and effectiveness tracking. Each record links one practice area to one delivery model with attributes that define the operational parameters, resource allocation, and quality metrics for that specific combination. This is actively managed by legal operations teams for capacity planning, RFP response preparation, and delivery optimization.. Existence Justification: In legal operations, practice areas can be delivered through multiple delivery models (e.g., M&A can be delivered via traditional partner-led teams, ALSP co-delivery, or managed legal services), and each delivery model supports multiple practice areas (e.g., ALSP co-delivery can handle IP, employment law, and regulatory compliance). Legal operations teams actively manage this matrix to allocate capacity, respond to RFPs with appropriate staffing models, track delivery effectiveness, and optimize resource deployment across the practice-delivery configuration space.'
}

Table intake.prospect {
  prospect_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the prospect record. Primary key for the prospect entity representing a pre-engagement organization or individual in the new business intake pipeline.']
  aml_kyc_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.aml_kyc_program. Business justification: Prospects undergo initial AML/KYC screening before conversion to client, governed by the firms AML/KYC program. Real process: pre-client compliance screening in business development.']
  individual_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to client.individual. Business justification: Prospects can be individuals (personal legal services, estate planning, etc.) before becoming formal clients. This FK links individual prospects to the individual master. is_new_attribute=true because']
  organisation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to client.organisation. Business justification: Prospects in legal services are often organizations before they become formal clients. This FK links corporate prospects to the organisation master. is_new_attribute=true because organisation_id is no']
  originating_attorney_attorney_profile_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the attorney credited with originating this prospect opportunity. Used for origination credit and compensation calculations.']
  primary_prospect_attorney_profile_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the attorney who owns the relationship with this prospect and is responsible for business development and intake progression.']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key reference to the client record created upon successful prospect conversion. Nullable until the prospect is converted to a client. Links the pre-engagement prospect state to the post-engagement client entity.']
  aml_screening_status varchar [note: 'Status of Anti-Money Laundering (AML) screening and sanctions list checks. Must be cleared before engagement acceptance.']
  competitor_firms varchar [note: 'Names of competing law firms known to be pitching for the same engagement. Used for competitive intelligence and win/loss analysis.']
  conflict_check_status varchar [note: 'Status of the ethical conflict of interest check performed against existing clients, matters, and adverse parties. Must be cleared before engagement.']
  conflicts_system_reference varchar [note: 'External identifier linking this prospect to the new business intake record in Intapp Conflicts system. Used for conflict check workflow integration.']
  converted_to_client_date date [note: 'Date on which the prospect was successfully converted to a formal client upon Letter of Engagement (LOE) execution and matter opening. Nullable until conversion occurs.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the prospect record was first created in the system. Represents the moment the prospect entered the intake pipeline.']
  crm_account_reference varchar [note: 'External identifier linking this prospect to the corresponding account or lead record in Microsoft Dynamics 365 CRM. Enables bi-directional synchronization.']
  disqualification_reason varchar [note: 'Reason for disqualifying or declining the prospect opportunity. Populated when pipeline stage moves to lost or declined. Used for loss analysis and process improvement.']
  engagement_scope_summary varchar [note: 'High-level description of the prospective engagement scope, legal services required, and business objectives. Used for practice group routing and conflict assessment.']
  estimated_matter_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Estimated total value or revenue potential of the prospective engagement. Used for pipeline forecasting and resource allocation planning.']
  estimated_value_currency varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the estimated matter value. Supports multi-currency pipeline reporting.']
  expected_decision_date date [note: 'Anticipated date by which the prospect is expected to make a decision on the engagement. Used for pipeline forecasting and follow-up scheduling.']
  industry_sector varchar [note: 'The primary industry or business sector of the prospect organization. Used for conflict checking, practice group assignment, and sector expertise matching.']
  jurisdiction varchar [note: 'The primary legal jurisdiction or geographic location where the prospect operates or resides. Critical for conflict checking and regulatory compliance assessment.']
  kyc_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the Know Your Client (KYC) due diligence and identity verification process. Required before engagement acceptance per AML regulations.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the prospect record. Used for change tracking and data freshness monitoring.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for capturing additional context, meeting notes, special requirements, or internal observations about the prospect and intake process.']
  pipeline_stage varchar [note: 'Current stage of the prospect in the new business intake pipeline. Tracks progression from initial lead through to engagement or disqualification. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: lead|qualified|pitch_scheduled|proposal_submitted|negotiation|won|lost|on_hold — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  pitch_date date [note: 'Date of the formal pitch or presentation to the prospect. Nullable if no pitch meeting is scheduled or has occurred.']
  practice_area varchar [note: 'The primary legal practice area or service line relevant to the prospective engagement. Used for practice group routing and expertise matching.']
  primary_contact_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the primary contact for the prospect. Used for all formal intake communications, RFP responses, and engagement correspondence.']
  primary_contact_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the primary contact person at the prospect organization or the individual prospect themselves. Key relationship owner for intake communications.']
  primary_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Primary telephone number for the prospect contact. Includes country code and extension where applicable.']
  proposal_submitted_date date [note: 'Date on which the formal engagement proposal or fee quote was submitted to the prospect.']
  proposed_fee_arrangement_type varchar [note: 'The proposed billing structure or Alternative Fee Arrangement (AFA) for the prospective engagement. Influences pricing strategy and profitability analysis. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: hourly|fixed_fee|contingency|afa_blended|afa_capped|retainer|success_fee|hybrid — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  prospect_name varchar [note: 'The legal or trading name of the prospective client organization or individual. For organizations, this is the registered business name; for individuals, the full legal name.']
  prospect_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the prospect entity type. Determines the intake workflow, KYC requirements, and conflict checking scope.']
  referral_date date [note: 'Date on which the prospect was referred to the firm. Nullable if the prospect source is not a referral. Used for referral attribution and relationship tracking.']
  referral_source_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the referring party when the prospect source is a referral. Used for referral network analytics and attribution reporting.']
  referring_party_name varchar [note: 'Name of the individual or organization that referred this prospect. Nullable if the prospect source is not a referral.']
  rfp_received_date date [note: 'Date on which a formal Request for Proposal (RFP) or engagement inquiry was received from the prospect. Nullable if no formal RFP was issued.']
  rfp_response_due_date date [note: 'Deadline for submitting the proposal or RFP response to the prospect. Used for intake workflow prioritization and resource scheduling.']
  source_type varchar [note: 'The channel or method through which the prospect entered the new business intake pipeline. Used for business development attribution and marketing effectiveness analysis. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: rfp|referral|bd_outreach|directory|conference|panel|alumni_network|existing_client|website_inquiry|cold_contact — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  win_probability_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Estimated probability of winning the engagement expressed as a percentage (0.00 to 100.00). Used for weighted pipeline forecasting and resource planning.']

  Note: 'Master record for prospective clients entering the new business intake pipeline. Captures the pre-engagement identity of an organization or individual before formal client status is established in the client domain. Tracks prospect source (RFP, referral, BD outreach, directory, conference, panel, alumni network), prospect type (corporate, government, individual), referral attribution (referral source type, referring party, relationship owner, referral date), KYC/AML pre-screening status, originating attorney, and pipeline stage. Bridges Microsoft Dynamics 365 CRM lead/account records with the intake workflow. Supports BD attribution reporting and referral network analytics. Distinct from the client domains client entity — prospect represents the pre-onboarding state prior to conflict clearance and LOE execution.'
}

Table intake.rfp_submission {
  rfp_submission_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the RFP submission record. Primary key.']
  attorney_profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the partner or senior lawyer assigned as the lead for coordinating the pitch and proposal response.']
  client_contact_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the primary client contact person who issued or is managing the RFP on behalf of the issuing organisation.']
  form_template_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to knowledge.form_template. Business justification: RFP responses routinely attach or reference firm standard templates, sample deliverables, and precedent forms to demonstrate capability and methodology. Proposal assembly process in legal services dep']
  issuing_organisation_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the prospective or existing client organisation that issued this RFP.']
  primary_prospect_id varchar [note: 'FK to intake.prospect.prospect_id — Every RFP is received from a prospect — this FK is required for pipeline attribution and BD reporting. Without it, RFPs cannot be associated with their originating prospect.']
  reputational_risk_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to risk.reputational_risk. Business justification: RFP responses to high-profile, controversial, or sanctioned clients require reputational risk assessment before submission; the reputational_risk flag must reference the specific RFP for risk committe']
  rfp_last_modified_by_user_timekeeper_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the user who last modified this RFP submission record.']
  submission_owner_timekeeper_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the user (business development staff or partner) who created this RFP submission record.']
  competitive_firms varchar [note: 'Names or identifiers of other law firms known to be competing for this RFP, if disclosed by the client or identified through market intelligence.']
  conflict_check_completed_date date [note: 'The date on which the conflict check process was completed for this RFP submission.']
  conflict_check_status varchar [note: 'The status of the conflict check process for this RFP, indicating whether conflicts of interest have been identified or cleared.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this RFP submission record was first created in the system.']
  decision_outcome varchar [note: 'The final outcome of the RFP process from the clients perspective (awarded to the firm, declined, no decision made, or withdrawn by client).']
  decision_received_date date [note: 'The date on which the firm received notification of the clients decision regarding the RFP (awarded, declined, or no decision).']
  decline_reason varchar [note: 'The reason why the firm declined to respond to the RFP or why the client declined the firms proposal. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: conflict_of_interest|capacity_constraints|fee_expectations|practice_area_mismatch|strategic_fit|competitive_pricing|other — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  decline_reason_notes varchar [note: 'Additional free-text notes providing context or details about the decline reason.']
  estimated_value_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The estimated monetary value of the engagement or matter if it proceeds, as assessed by the business development team during initial qualification.']
  estimated_value_currency varchar [note: 'The currency code for the estimated value amount, following ISO 4217 three-letter standard.']
  fee_arrangement_type varchar [note: 'The type of fee arrangement requested or proposed for this engagement (e.g., hourly billing, fixed fee, contingency, capped fee, blended rate, success fee).']
  gdpr_compliant boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the RFP and proposed engagement scope comply with GDPR requirements, relevant for EU-based clients or matters involving EU personal data.']
  internal_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text internal notes and observations recorded by the pitch team, business development, or intake staff regarding this RFP submission.']
  is_existing_client boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the issuing organisation is an existing client of the firm (True) or a prospective new client (False).']
  jurisdiction varchar [note: 'The legal jurisdiction(s) in which the services are required. May include country, state, or multi-jurisdictional scope.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this RFP submission record was last updated or modified.']
  matter_type varchar [note: 'The specific type of legal matter or engagement being requested (e.g., M&A transaction, patent prosecution, employment dispute, regulatory investigation).']
  practice_area varchar [note: 'The primary legal practice area or service line that this RFP pertains to. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: corporate|litigation|intellectual_property|employment|regulatory_compliance|mergers_and_acquisitions|real_estate|tax|banking_and_finance|dispute_resolution|antitrust|environmental|healthcare|technology|energy — promote to reference product]']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'The priority level assigned to this RFP submission by the business development or intake team, reflecting strategic importance or urgency.']
  proposal_submitted_date date [note: 'The date on which the firms proposal response was submitted to the issuing organisation.']
  received_date date [note: 'The date on which the RFP was received by the firm. This is the principal business event timestamp marking the start of the intake process.']
  requires_panel_approval boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this RFP requires approval from a clients legal panel or preferred provider list.']
  response_deadline_date date [note: 'The date by which the firm must submit its response to the RFP, as specified by the issuing organisation.']
  response_deadline_time timestamp [note: 'The precise timestamp (including time of day) by which the RFP response must be submitted, if specified by the issuing organisation.']
  rfp_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the legal services being requested, including scope, objectives, and key requirements outlined in the RFP document.']
  rfp_reference_number varchar [note: 'The external reference number or identifier assigned by the issuing organisation to this RFP. This is the business identifier used in client communications.']
  rfp_title varchar [note: 'The title or subject of the RFP as provided by the issuing organisation.']
  source_channel varchar [note: 'The channel or method through which the RFP was received by the firm (e.g., email, client portal, direct mail, referral, existing client relationship). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: email|portal|direct_mail|referral|existing_client|conference|other — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  submission_status varchar [note: 'The current lifecycle status of the RFP submission within the firms intake workflow (received, under review, declined, responded, withdrawn, awarded).']
  win_probability_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'The estimated probability (0-100%) that the firm will win this RFP, as assessed by the pitch team or business development team.']

  Note: 'Transactional record capturing each Request for Proposal received from a prospective or existing client. Tracks RFP receipt date, issuing organization, RFP reference number, practice area scope, response deadline, assigned pitch team, submission status (received, under review, declined, responded), and the resulting pitch or proposal linkage. Sourced from Microsoft Dynamics 365 opportunity pipeline. Serves as the intake trigger event that initiates the prospect qualification and conflict check workflow.'
}

Table intake.pitch {
  pitch_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the pitch record. Primary key.']
  attorney_profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the partner who led the pitch and will serve as the primary relationship owner if the engagement is won.']
  check_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the conflict check record in Intapp Conflicts system performed for this pitch.']
  letter_of_engagement_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the Letter of Engagement (LOE) executed following a successful pitch. Null if pitch was lost or engagement not yet formalized.']
  matter_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the matter opened in Elite 3E following a successful pitch and LOE execution. Null if pitch was lost or matter not yet opened.']
  pitch_modified_by_user_timekeeper_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user who last modified the pitch record.']
  pitch_owner_timekeeper_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user who created the pitch record, typically a BD professional or partner.']
  precedent_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to knowledge.precedent. Business justification: Pitch presentations showcase relevant precedent transactions, sample work product, and comparable engagements to demonstrate firm experience and capability. Business development process in legal servi']
  primary_pitch_legal_document_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the pitch deck or credentials presentation document stored in the Document Management System (iManage Work or NetDocuments).']
  primary_rfp_submission_id varchar [note: 'FK to intake.rfp_submission.rfp_submission_id — Pitches are made in response to RFPs. This is the core pipeline progression link. Without it, cannot trace RFP→pitch→opportunity flow.']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the prospective or existing client organization to whom the pitch was delivered.']
  source_rfp_submission_id varchar [note: 'FK to intake.rfp_submission.rfp_submission_id — Pitches are made in response to RFPs — this FK links the response activity to the triggering request. Critical for win/loss analysis.']
  client_attendees varchar [note: 'Names and titles of client representatives who attended the pitch, captured for relationship tracking and follow-up. Comma-separated list.']
  competing_firms varchar [note: 'Names of known competing law firms participating in the pitch process, if disclosed by the client. Comma-separated list.']
  competitive_context varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the pitch was sole-sourced or competitive, and if competitive, the approximate number of competing firms: sole_pitch (no competition), competitive_2_firms, competitive_3_firms, competitive_4plus_firms, or unknown.']
  conflict_check_status varchar [note: 'Status of the conflict check performed prior to the pitch: cleared (no conflicts identified), pending (check in progress), flagged (potential conflict identified, under review), or waived (conflict waived by client).']
  cost_estimate decimal(18,2) [note: 'Estimated total cost to the firm of pursuing the pitch, including attorney time, travel, materials, and external consultants. Used for BD ROI analysis.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the pitch record was first created in the system.']
  duration_minutes varchar [note: 'Total duration of the pitch presentation in minutes, including Q&A if applicable.']
  estimated_matter_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Estimated total fee value of the engagement if won, in the firms base currency. Used for pipeline forecasting and win/loss analysis.']
  follow_up_due_date date [note: 'Target date by which post-pitch follow-up actions must be completed, if applicable.']
  follow_up_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether post-pitch follow-up actions are required (e.g., additional information requests, clarifications, supplemental proposals). True if follow-up is needed, False otherwise.']
  location varchar [note: 'Physical location or virtual platform where the pitch was delivered (e.g., client office address, firm office, video conference platform).']
  loss_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code or category explaining why the pitch was lost, if applicable (e.g., fee too high, lack of experience, conflict of interest, relationship with competitor, client chose incumbent). Used for win/loss analysis and BD strategy refinement.']
  matter_type varchar [note: 'Specific type of legal matter or engagement being pitched (e.g., Share Purchase Agreement, Patent Prosecution, Employment Tribunal Defense, Regulatory Investigation).']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the pitch record was last modified.']
  outcome varchar [note: 'Final outcome of the pitch: won (firm selected and engagement awarded), lost (firm not selected), pending (decision not yet received), withdrawn (firm withdrew from consideration), or no_decision (client did not proceed with engagement).']
  outcome_date date [note: 'Date on which the pitch outcome was communicated to the firm by the client or became known.']
  pitch_date date [note: 'The date on which the pitch was delivered or presented to the prospective client.']
  pitch_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name or title of the pitch, typically reflecting the matter type or client engagement scope being pursued.']
  pitch_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the pitch: scheduled (pitch date set but not yet delivered), delivered (pitch completed, awaiting outcome), won (firm selected), lost (firm not selected), pending (outcome not yet known), withdrawn (firm withdrew from consideration), or no_decision (client did not proceed with engagement). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: scheduled|delivered|won|lost|pending|withdrawn|no_decision — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  pitch_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the pitch format: beauty parade (competitive multi-firm presentation), credentials deck (written credentials submission), oral presentation (in-person or virtual pitch), written proposal (detailed written submission), panel interview (Q&A with client panel), or finalist presentation (final round competitive pitch).']
  practice_area varchar [note: 'Primary practice area or legal service line covered by the pitch (e.g., M&A, Litigation, IP, Employment, Regulatory Compliance). May be a comma-separated list for multi-practice pitches.']
  preparation_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total non-billable hours invested by the firm in preparing for the pitch, including research, deck creation, rehearsal, and travel. Used for BD cost analysis.']
  proposed_fee_arrangement varchar [note: 'Type of fee arrangement proposed in the pitch: hourly (standard time-based billing), fixed_fee (flat fee for scope), capped_fee (hourly with upper limit), contingency (success-based), blended_rate (single rate for all timekeepers), afa_other (alternative fee arrangement not otherwise specified), retainer (ongoing monthly/quarterly fee), or success_fee (bonus upon outcome). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: hourly|fixed_fee|capped_fee|contingency|blended_rate|afa_other|retainer|success_fee — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  reference_number varchar [note: 'Business-facing unique reference number or code assigned to the pitch for tracking and communication purposes.']
  score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Internal qualitative or quantitative score assigned to the pitch performance by the pitch team or BD leadership, used for continuous improvement. Scale and methodology defined by firm policy.']
  team_size varchar [note: 'Total number of attorneys and professionals from the firm who participated in the pitch presentation or preparation.']
  win_loss_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text narrative capturing detailed feedback from the client or internal debrief regarding the pitch outcome, including strengths, weaknesses, and lessons learned.']
  win_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code or category explaining why the pitch was won, if applicable (e.g., expertise, relationship, fee competitiveness, innovation, diversity). Used for win/loss analysis and BD strategy refinement.']

  Note: 'Master record representing a formal pitch or credentials presentation made to a prospective or existing client in response to an RFP or BD opportunity. Tracks pitch type (beauty parade, credentials deck, oral presentation), pitch date, presenting attorneys, practice areas covered, competitive context, pitch outcome (won, lost, pending, withdrawn), and win/loss reason codes. Linked to the originating RFP submission and the resulting engagement opportunity. Sourced from Microsoft Dynamics 365 opportunity and activity records.'
}

Table intake.engagement_opportunity {
  engagement_opportunity_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the engagement opportunity record. Primary key.']
  aml_kyc_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.aml_kyc_program. Business justification: Engagement opportunities require AML/KYC screening under the firms program before conversion to matter. Real process: opportunity-stage compliance screening for high-value or high-risk prospects.']
  engagement_modified_by_user_timekeeper_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the user who last modified the opportunity record.']
  legal_document_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to document.legal_document. Business justification: Engagement opportunities track proposal documents (fee proposals, scope documents, capability statements) submitted during business development. Essential for win/loss analysis, proposal reuse, and tr']
  opportunity_owner_timekeeper_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the user who created the opportunity record.']
  originating_partner_attorney_profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the partner who originated the client relationship or opportunity, relevant for credit allocation.']
  pitch_id varchar [note: 'FK to intake.pitch.pitch_id — Engagement opportunities are often created from successful pitches. Links the BD pipeline stages.']
  practice_area_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to service.practice_area. Business justification: Opportunities tracked by practice area for pipeline reporting, resource forecasting, capacity planning, and practice-level revenue projections. Critical for business development dashboards and partner']
  primary_engagement_attorney_profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the partner leading the business development effort and expected to be the originating or responsible partner if the matter opens.']
  primary_prospect_id varchar [note: 'FK to intake.prospect.prospect_id — Every opportunity is associated with a prospect. Core CRM pipeline relationship required for conversion tracking.']
  register_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to risk.risk_register. Business justification: Opportunities undergo risk assessment during business development; high-risk prospects (sanctions exposure, reputational concerns, conflict complexity) require documented risk register entries and par']
  regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_obligation. Business justification: Opportunities in regulated sectors (financial services, healthcare, energy) must identify applicable regulatory obligations early for risk assessment and scoping. Real process: regulatory risk evaluat']
  tertiary_prospect_id varchar [note: 'FK to intake.prospect.prospect_id — Each engagement opportunity is associated with a prospect. This is the core CRM pipeline link.']
  actual_close_date date [note: 'Actual date the opportunity was closed (won or lost).']
  afa_type varchar [note: 'Type of fee arrangement under consideration or negotiated for this opportunity. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: hourly|fixed_fee|capped_fee|contingency|blended_rate|success_fee|retainer — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  competitor_name varchar [note: 'Name of the competing law firm or ALSP that won the engagement, if known.']
  conflict_check_completed_date date [note: 'Date the conflict check was completed and cleared (or waived) for this opportunity.']
  conflict_check_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the conflict check process in Intapp Conflicts for this opportunity.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the opportunity record was first created in the CRM system.']
