  register_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to risk.risk_register. Business justification: Cyber risk events should be tracked in the enterprise risk register. Links operational cyber events to strategic risk register. Valid business relationship for risk aggregation.']
  risk_control_id varchar [note: 'FK to risk.risk_control.risk_control_id — Events may indicate control failures. The description mentions linkage to ISO 27001 controls. This FK enables control effectiveness analysis.']
  actual_loss_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The realized financial loss incurred as a direct result of the cyber risk event, including recovery costs, legal fees, regulatory fines, and business interruption costs.']
  affected_client_count varchar [note: 'The number of clients whose data or services were affected by the cyber risk event.']
  affected_practice_group varchar [note: 'Name of the practice group or business unit affected by the cyber risk event, if the impact was localized to a specific area of the firm.']
  affected_record_count varchar [note: 'The total number of data records (documents, files, database rows) compromised or affected by the cyber risk event.']
  affected_system varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the primary IT system, application, or infrastructure component impacted by the cyber risk event (e.g., Elite 3E, iManage Work, email server, network infrastructure).']
  affected_user_count varchar [note: 'The number of internal users (timekeepers, staff) whose accounts or data were affected by the cyber risk event.']
  containment_action varchar [note: 'Description of the immediate containment measures taken to limit the spread or impact of the cyber risk event (e.g., system isolation, account lockdown, network segmentation).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this cyber risk event record was first created in the system.']
  data_breach_incident_id varchar [note: 'foreign_key_to']
  detection_date date [note: 'The date when the cyber risk event was first detected or identified by the firm, which may differ from the event occurrence date.']
  detection_source varchar [note: 'The mechanism or channel through which the cyber risk event was first identified (e.g., automated monitoring, user report, internal audit, external notification). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: automated_monitoring|user_report|internal_audit|external_notification|penetration_test|vulnerability_scan|siem_alert|third_party_notification|regulatory_inquiry — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  downtime_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'The total duration in hours that systems or services were unavailable as a result of the cyber risk event.']
  estimated_loss_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The estimated potential financial loss for near-miss events or events where actual loss has not yet been fully quantified.']
  event_date date [note: 'The calendar date when the cyber risk event occurred or was first realized by the firm.']
  event_realizes_risk varchar [note: 'FK to risk.risk_register.risk_register_id — Risk events represent the realization of identified risks. Linking events back to the register enables loss tracking against risk items.']
  event_reference_number varchar [note: 'Business-facing unique reference number for the cyber risk event, used for tracking and reporting purposes across the firm.']
  event_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the cyber risk event indicating the stage of incident response and resolution.']
  event_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time when the cyber risk event occurred or was first detected, supporting forensic analysis and timeline reconstruction.']
  event_type varchar [note: 'Primary classification of the cyber risk event indicating the nature of the incident (e.g., phishing attack, ransomware, DDoS, insider threat, system outage, process failure). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: phishing|ransomware|ddos|insider_threat|malware|vulnerability_exploit|unauthorized_access|data_exfiltration|system_outage|process_failure|human_error|external_fraud|third_party_failure|physical_security_breach — 14 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  external_party_involved varchar [note: 'Name of any external third party involved in the cyber risk event (e.g., vendor, service provider, attacker organization if known).']
  insurance_claim_filed boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether a professional indemnity or cyber insurance claim was filed for this cyber risk event.']
  insurance_claim_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number for the insurance claim filed in relation to this cyber risk event, if applicable.']
  is_near_miss boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the event was a near-miss incident (detected and prevented before causing actual loss or impact).']
  is_reportable_breach boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the cyber risk event constitutes a reportable data breach under GDPR Article 33/34 or other regulatory notification requirements.']
  iso_control_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific ISO 27001 Annex A control(s) that were breached or tested by this cyber risk event (e.g., A.12.6.1, A.16.1.2).']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this cyber risk event record was last updated or modified.']
  lessons_learned varchar [note: 'Documentation of key insights, process improvements, and preventive measures identified through post-incident review to reduce future risk exposure.']
  loss_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the actual or estimated loss amount (e.g., USD, GBP, EUR).']
  recovery_cost_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The total cost incurred to recover from the cyber risk event, including forensic investigation, system restoration, and external consultant fees.']
  regulatory_notification_date date [note: 'The date when the cyber risk event was formally notified to the relevant regulatory authority, if required.']
  regulatory_notification_required boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the cyber risk event requires notification to regulatory authorities (e.g., ICO, SRA, state bar associations).']
  remediation_action varchar [note: 'Description of the corrective actions taken to resolve the cyber risk event and restore normal operations (e.g., patch deployment, password reset, system rebuild).']
  resolution_date date [note: 'The date when the cyber risk event was fully resolved and normal operations were restored.']
  root_cause_analysis varchar [note: 'Detailed analysis of the underlying cause(s) of the cyber risk event, identifying systemic weaknesses or control failures that enabled the incident.']
  severity_level varchar [note: 'Impact severity classification of the event aligned to ISO 27001 impact assessment levels (critical, high, medium, low, informational).']
  to_register varchar [note: 'FK to risk.risk_register.risk_register_id — Risk events realize risks tracked in the register. This FK enables loss event attribution to registered risks for trend analysis and risk scoring updates.']

  Note: 'Unified operational and cybersecurity risk event record serving as the single source of truth for all realized risk losses, near-miss events, and security incidents across the firm. Covers the full spectrum of operational risk events including process failures, system outages, human error, external events, as well as cybersecurity-specific events such as phishing, ransomware, DDoS, insider threats, and vulnerability exploits. Each record captures event date, event type and category, severity classification (aligned to ISO 27001 impact levels), affected systems or practice groups, detection source, containment actions, root cause analysis, financial impact (actual loss or near-miss estimated loss), matter reference if applicable, lessons-learned documentation, and linkage to ISO 27001 controls. Distinct from data_breach_incident which tracks privacy-specific regulatory notification obligations under GDPR Article 33/34.'
}

Table risk.sanctions_screening {
  sanctions_screening_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for sanctions_screening']
  attorney_profile_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the compliance officer who reviewed this screening result.']
  contract_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.agreement. Business justification: Sanctions screening performed at client onboarding and contract execution. Linking screening results to specific agreements enables AML/KYC compliance, contract approval workflow, conflict checking, a']
  matter_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the matter for which this screening was performed.']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the client being screened.']
  register_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to risk.risk_register. Business justification: Sanctions screening hits (especially non-false-positives) represent enterprise risks and should be tracked in the risk register. Valid business relationship for AML/sanctions risk aggregation.']
  reputational_risk_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to risk.reputational_risk. Business justification: Sanctions screening hits can trigger reputational risk flags. Business semantics: this screening result generated this reputational risk flag. Valid causal relationship.']
  screened_receipt_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to trust.trust_receipt. Business justification: AML sanctions screening occurs at trust receipt intake before deposit. All receipts require payor screening against OFAC/sanctions lists per AML regulations; screening result determines whether receip']
  screened_trust_disbursement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to trust.trust_disbursement. Business justification: All trust disbursements require payee sanctions screening before execution per AML policy. Screening result blocks disbursement if match found; clearance decision and screening reference must be docum']
  adverse_media_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether adverse media or negative news was identified for the screened entity during the screening process (True = adverse media found, False = no adverse media).']
  aml_risk_rating varchar [note: 'Overall AML risk rating assigned to the screened entity based on the screening results and compliance review.']
  clearance_conditions varchar [note: 'Specific conditions or restrictions imposed if the clearance decision was conditional approval.']
  clearance_date date [note: 'Date when the final clearance decision was made.']
  clearance_decision varchar [note: 'Final clearance decision for the screened entity: approved (cleared to proceed), declined (engagement rejected), or conditional approval (proceed with restrictions).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this screening result record was first created in the system.']
  enhanced_due_diligence_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether enhanced due diligence procedures are required for this entity based on the screening results (True = EDD required, False = standard due diligence sufficient).']
  escalation_date date [note: 'Date when the screening result was escalated to senior compliance or risk management.']
  escalation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this screening result was escalated to senior compliance or risk management for further review (True = escalated, False = not escalated).']
  false_positive_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the match was determined to be a false positive after review (True = false positive, False = genuine match).']
  false_positive_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation of why the match was classified as a false positive, including distinguishing factors identified during review.']
  match_count varchar [note: 'Number of potential matches identified during the screening process.']
  match_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numerical confidence score (0.00 to 100.00) indicating the strength of the match between the screened entity and the sanctions list entry.']
  match_type varchar [note: 'Type of match found during screening: exact name match, fuzzy match, alias match, phonetic match, or no match.']
  matched_list_entry_name varchar [note: 'Name of the entity on the sanctions list that matched the screened entity.']
  matched_list_entry_reference varchar [note: 'Unique reference or identifier of the matched entry on the sanctions list (e.g., OFAC SDN number, UN reference number).']
  pep_category varchar [note: 'Classification of the PEP status: domestic PEP, foreign PEP, international organization PEP, family member of PEP, or close associate of PEP.']
  pep_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the screened entity is identified as a Politically Exposed Person (True = PEP, False = not PEP).']
  rescreening_due_date date [note: 'Date when the entity is due for periodic rescreening to maintain ongoing AML compliance.']
  review_date date [note: 'Date when the compliance officer completed their review of this screening result.']
  review_notes varchar [note: 'Detailed notes and rationale documented by the compliance officer during their review of the screening result.']
  review_outcome varchar [note: 'Final outcome of the compliance officer review: cleared (no risk), rejected (engagement declined), pending review, requires enhanced due diligence, or approved with conditions.']
  screened_entity_country_of_residence varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code representing the country of residence or incorporation of the screened entity.']
  screened_entity_date_of_birth date [note: 'Date of birth of the individual being screened, used to improve match accuracy and reduce false positives.']
  screened_entity_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the individual or organization being screened against sanctions lists.']
  screened_entity_national_identifier varchar [note: 'National identification number or registration number of the screened entity used for enhanced screening accuracy.']
  screened_entity_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the entity being screened (individual, organization, beneficial owner, counterparty, matter participant, or trust).']
  screening_date date [note: 'Date when the sanctions screening check was performed.']
  screening_list_checked varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of sanctions and watchlists checked during this screening (OFAC, UN, EU, HMT, PEP, etc.). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: OFAC|UN|EU|HMT|PEP|INTERPOL|DFAT|SECO — promote to reference product]']
  screening_reference_number varchar [note: 'Unique business reference number assigned to this screening check for tracking and audit purposes.']
  screening_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the screening process: completed, in progress, pending review, or failed.']
  screening_system_name varchar [note: 'Name of the automated screening system or service used to perform the sanctions check (e.g., Dow Jones, LexisNexis, Refinitiv World-Check).']
  screening_system_version varchar [note: 'Version number of the screening system or database used at the time of the check.']
  screening_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise timestamp when the sanctions screening check was initiated.']
  screening_trigger_event varchar [note: 'Business event that triggered this sanctions screening: new client intake, matter opening, periodic review, regulatory update, or risk event.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this screening result record was last modified.']

  Note: 'Record of each sanctions and AML screening check performed against a client, counterparty, beneficial owner, or matter participant. Captures screening date, screened entity name and type, screening list(s) checked (OFAC, UN, EU, HMT), match type (exact, fuzzy, alias), match score, false positive determination, escalation flag, compliance officer review outcome, and clearance or rejection decision. Supports FATF AML obligations and SRA AML regulations. Distinct from conflict check domain which handles conflict-of-interest screening.'
}

Table risk.reputational_risk {
  reputational_risk_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for reputational_risk']
  attorney_profile_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the partner who approved the decision regarding the reputational risk flag, typically a senior or managing partner.']
  category_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to risk.risk_category. Business justification: Reputational risks are categorized by risk type. reputational_risk has flag_type (STRING) which should normalize to risk_category for taxonomy consistency.']
  escalation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to risk.risk_escalation. Business justification: Reputational risks may trigger escalations to senior leadership or board. Valid business relationship for reputational risk governance.']
  monitored_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to trust.trust_account. Business justification: High-risk clients flagged for reputational concerns require enhanced trust account monitoring. Enhanced due diligence applies to trust accounts of flagged clients with increased reconciliation frequen']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the client associated with this reputational risk flag.']
  register_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to risk.risk_register. Business justification: Reputational risks should be tracked in the enterprise risk register. Valid business relationship for reputational risk aggregation.']
  board_notification_date date [note: 'Date on which the firms board of directors or management committee was formally notified about the reputational risk flag.']
  closure_date date [note: 'Date on which the reputational risk flag was formally closed, either because the risk was mitigated, the engagement was declined, or the matter concluded.']
  closure_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation for why the reputational risk flag was closed, such as risk no longer applicable, engagement declined, matter completed, or false positive determination.']
  conditions_imposed varchar [note: 'Specific conditions or controls imposed if the engagement was accepted despite the reputational risk, such as enhanced due diligence, ethical walls, or senior partner oversight.']
  counterparty_name varchar [note: 'Name of the counterparty or third party that is the subject of the reputational risk flag.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the reputational risk flag record was first created in the system.']
  disposition varchar [note: 'Final decision or action taken regarding the reputational risk flag: accepted with conditions (engagement proceeds with enhanced monitoring), declined (engagement rejected), monitored (ongoing surveillance), escalated to board for decision, or no action required (risk deemed immaterial).']
  external_counsel_engaged_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether external legal counsel was engaged to advise on the reputational risk flag and appropriate firm response.']
  flag_raised_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the individual or system that raised the reputational risk flag.']
  flag_raised_date date [note: 'Date on which the reputational risk flag was first raised and recorded in the system.']
  flag_reference_number varchar [note: 'Business-facing unique reference number for the reputational risk flag, used for tracking and communication.']
  flag_source varchar [note: 'Origin of the reputational risk flag: internal research, third-party screening service, media monitoring tool, regulatory alert, client disclosure, conflict check system output, or manual review by risk team. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: internal_research|third_party_screening|media_monitoring|regulatory_alert|client_disclosure|conflict_check_system|manual_review — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  flag_source_detail varchar [note: 'Additional detail about the source of the flag, such as the name of the screening vendor, media outlet, or regulatory body that issued the alert.']
  impact_assessment varchar [note: 'Assessment of the potential impact to the firm if the reputational risk materializes, including potential regulatory scrutiny, client loss, or media attention.']
  industry_sector varchar [note: 'Industry sector or business activity associated with the reputational risk, such as arms trade, extractive industries, gambling, or tobacco.']
  insurance_notification_date date [note: 'Date on which the firm notified its professional indemnity insurance carrier about the reputational risk flag.']
  insurance_notification_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the firms professional indemnity insurance carrier was notified about the reputational risk flag due to potential exposure.']
  jurisdiction varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code of the jurisdiction associated with the reputational risk flag, particularly relevant for high-risk jurisdiction flags.']
  last_modified_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the user who last modified the reputational risk flag record.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the reputational risk flag record was last updated or modified.']
  media_exposure_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the reputational risk flag has resulted in or has potential for adverse media coverage of the firm.']
  mitigation_actions varchar [note: 'Specific actions taken or planned to mitigate the reputational risk, such as enhanced client due diligence, engagement letter modifications, or public relations strategy.']
  monitoring_frequency varchar [note: 'Frequency at which the reputational risk flag is monitored if the disposition is to monitor: continuous, monthly, quarterly, semi-annual, annual, or as needed based on trigger events.']
  next_review_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next periodic review of the reputational risk flag, applicable when ongoing monitoring is required.']
  regulatory_body varchar [note: 'Name of the regulatory body notified about the reputational risk flag, such as Solicitors Regulation Authority (SRA), Law Society, or Financial Conduct Authority (FCA).']
  regulatory_notification_date date [note: 'Date on which the firm notified the relevant regulatory body about the reputational risk flag, if notification was required.']
  regulatory_notification_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the reputational risk flag triggers a mandatory notification to a regulatory body such as the Solicitors Regulation Authority (SRA) or Law Society.']
  regulatory_reference_number varchar [note: 'Reference number assigned by the regulatory body for the notification or investigation related to this reputational risk flag.']
  review_date date [note: 'Date on which the reputational risk flag was formally reviewed by the risk committee or designated partner.']
  review_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the reputational risk flag review process: pending review, under review by risk committee, reviewed and decided, escalated to senior management, or closed.']
  reviewed_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the individual or committee that conducted the formal review of the reputational risk flag.']
  risk_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the reputational risk concern, including specific allegations, media reports, or regulatory findings.']
  severity_level varchar [note: 'Assessed severity of the reputational risk: low, medium, high, or critical, based on potential impact to the firms reputation and regulatory standing.']

  Note: 'Reputational risk flag record identifying clients, matters, counterparties, or industries that present elevated reputational risk to the firm. Captures flag type (adverse media, politically exposed person, high-risk jurisdiction, controversial industry, ESG concern), flag source, severity level, flag raised date, review status, approving partner identity, and disposition (accepted with conditions, declined, monitored). Feeds new business intake and matter acceptance decisions. Supports the firms ethical obligations under SRA Principles and Code of Conduct regarding client selection.'
}

Table risk.matter_risk_profile {
  matter_risk_profile_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the matter risk profile record. Primary key for this entity. Role: MASTER_RESOURCE (risk profile as a managed resource).']
  appetite_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to risk.risk_appetite. Business justification: Matter risk profiles are evaluated against firm risk appetite thresholds. Valid business relationship for matter acceptance and risk governance.']
  matter_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the legal matter to which this risk profile applies. Links this risk profile to the specific case or engagement being assessed.']
  practice_group_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the practice group responsible for this matter, enabling practice-level risk aggregation and benchmarking.']
  primary_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to trust.trust_account. Business justification: High-risk matters require segregated trust accounting with enhanced controls. Matter risk tier determines trust account monitoring frequency, reconciliation requirements, and authorization thresholds ']
  primary_attorney_profile_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the partner with primary responsibility for matter oversight and risk management decisions.']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the client associated with this matter. Enables client-level risk aggregation and portfolio analysis.']
  aml_risk_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the matter presents elevated anti-money laundering risk requiring enhanced due diligence, source of funds verification, and suspicious activity monitoring.']
  client_credit_risk_rating varchar [note: 'Credit risk assessment of the client using standard rating scale, indicating likelihood of payment default and informing billing arrangements and retainer requirements. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: AAA|AA|A|BBB|BB|B|CCC|CC|C|D — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  client_payment_history_flag varchar [note: 'Historical payment performance indicator for this client based on past matters, informing credit risk and billing strategy.']
  conflict_risk_level varchar [note: 'Assessment of potential conflicts of interest based on client relationships, adverse parties, and matter subject matter, informing ethical wall requirements and engagement decisions.']
  cross_border_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the matter involves multiple jurisdictions, triggering additional conflict-of-laws analysis and regulatory compliance requirements.']
  cybersecurity_risk_level varchar [note: 'Assessment of cybersecurity and data breach risk for this matter based on data sensitivity, technology platforms used, third-party access, and threat landscape, informing ISO 27001 controls.']
  ediscovery_volume_exposure varchar [note: 'Assessment of the volume and complexity of Electronically Stored Information (ESI) that may be subject to discovery, impacting matter cost, timeline, and technology-assisted review (TAR) requirements.']
  escalation_date date [note: 'Date when the matter was escalated to higher authority for review and approval, if escalation was required.']
  escalation_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this matter risk profile requires escalation to senior management, risk committee, or board level due to risk tier or specific risk factors.']
  esi_volume_gb decimal(18,2) [note: 'Estimated volume of ESI in gigabytes that may be subject to collection, review, and production in this matter, driving eDiscovery cost and resource planning.']
  estimated_matter_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Estimated total value at stake in the matter (transaction value, claim amount, or potential liability), used to assess proportionality of risk and insurance coverage adequacy. Expressed in firm base currency.']
  insurance_notification_date date [note: 'Date when professional indemnity insurer was notified of this matter, if notification was required.']
  insurance_notification_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether professional indemnity insurer notification is required for this matter based on risk profile and policy terms.']
  jurisdictional_risk_level varchar [note: 'Risk rating based on the legal jurisdictions involved, considering political stability, judicial predictability, enforcement mechanisms, and regulatory environment.']
  last_review_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent risk profile review, typically conducted at matter milestones (mid-matter review, pre-trial, significant developments).']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this risk profile record, supporting change tracking and audit requirements.']
  lpp_sensitivity_level varchar [note: 'Assessment of the sensitivity of privileged communications and work product in this matter, driving information security controls and ethical wall requirements.']
  matter_complexity_rating varchar [note: 'Assessment of the legal and factual complexity of the matter considering number of parties, jurisdictions, legal issues, document volume, and expert requirements.']
  matter_profile_references_risks varchar [note: 'FK to risk.risk_register.risk_register_id — Matter risk profiles aggregate risk dimensions that may reference specific risk register items for that matter.']
  matter_value_currency varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the estimated matter value (e.g., USD, GBP, EUR).']
  media_exposure_likelihood varchar [note: 'Probability that the matter will attract media attention or public scrutiny, informing communications strategy and reputational risk management.']
  next_review_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next mandatory risk profile review, driven by overall risk tier and matter lifecycle stage.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional risk assessment notes, context, and qualitative factors not captured in structured fields.']
  overall_risk_tier varchar [note: 'Consolidated risk rating for the matter using traffic-light classification: green (low risk), amber (medium risk), red (high risk). Drives partner review frequency and approval thresholds.']
  pii_data_volume_category varchar [note: 'Classification of the volume of PII involved in the matter, informing GDPR compliance requirements, data breach risk exposure, and data protection impact assessment needs.']
  primary_jurisdiction varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code representing the primary legal jurisdiction governing this matter (e.g., USA, GBR, DEU).']
  professional_indemnity_exposure varchar [note: 'Assessment of potential professional liability and malpractice risk exposure for this matter, informing insurance notification requirements and risk mitigation protocols.']
  profile_created_date date [note: 'Date when this risk profile was initially created, typically at matter intake or new business approval stage.']
  profile_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise timestamp when this risk profile record was first created in the system, supporting audit trail and lifecycle tracking.']
  profile_reference_number varchar [note: 'Human-readable unique reference code for this matter risk profile, typically formatted as MRP-YYYYNNNN for external communication and audit trails.']
  profile_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the risk profile indicating whether it is in draft, actively monitored, under review by risk committee, escalated to senior management, closed upon matter completion, or archived.']
  regulatory_scrutiny_level varchar [note: 'Assessment of the level of regulatory oversight and reporting requirements applicable to this matter, considering industry sector, transaction type, and jurisdictional requirements.']
  reputational_risk_rating varchar [note: 'Assessment of potential reputational harm to the firm from association with this matter, considering client profile, matter type, public visibility, and ethical considerations.']
  risk_mitigation_plan varchar [note: 'Summary of key risk mitigation strategies and controls implemented for this matter, including enhanced supervision, ethical walls, insurance notifications, and client communications.']
  sanctions_screening_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether parties, jurisdictions, or transaction elements require screening against international sanctions lists (OFAC, UN, EU) to ensure compliance with economic sanctions regimes.']
  special_category_data_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the matter involves GDPR Article 9 special category data (racial/ethnic origin, political opinions, religious beliefs, health data, biometric data, genetic data, sex life/orientation), requiring heightened data protection controls.']
  third_party_risk_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the matter involves third-party service providers (expert witnesses, eDiscovery vendors, co-counsel, ALSPs) requiring vendor due diligence and data sharing agreements.']
  to_register varchar [note: 'FK to risk.risk_register.risk_register_id — Matter risk profiles aggregate risk dimensions that may correspond to registered risks. This enables drill-down from matter risk tier to specific risk items.']
  to_risk_register varchar [note: 'FK to risk.risk_register.risk_register_id — Matter risk profiles aggregate risk items from the register that pertain to a specific matter. This link enables the roll-up view.']

  Note: 'Matter-level risk profile aggregating all risk dimensions for a specific legal matter including complexity rating, jurisdictional risk, client credit risk, legal professional privilege (LPP) sensitivity, eDiscovery/ESI volume exposure, regulatory scrutiny level, and overall matter risk tier (green/amber/red). Created at matter intake and updated at key lifecycle milestones (mid-matter review, pre-trial, closure). Provides the consolidated risk view consumed by matter management, billing, and partner review processes.'
}

Table risk.appetite {
  appetite_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for appetite']
  appetite_last_updated_by_timekeeper_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key reference to the user or system that last updated this risk appetite threshold record.']
  appetite_owner_timekeeper_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key reference to the user or system that created this risk appetite threshold record.']
  category_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to risk.risk_category. Business justification: Risk appetite thresholds are defined per risk category. risk_appetite has risk_category (STRING) which should normalize to risk_category reference table.']
  practice_group_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key reference to the practice group if this threshold applies at practice group level (nullable for firm-wide thresholds).']
  primary_appetite_attorney_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key reference to the individual (partner or executive) who formally approved this risk appetite threshold.']
  superseded_by_appetite_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key reference to the risk appetite threshold record that supersedes this one (nullable for current active thresholds).']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date on which this risk appetite threshold was formally approved by the governance body.']
  approving_governance_body varchar [note: 'The governance body or role responsible for approving and maintaining this risk appetite threshold (e.g., Risk Committee, Managing Partner, Board, Executive Committee).']
  automatic_escalation_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether risks exceeding the escalation trigger score are automatically escalated to the governance body (true) or require manual review (false).']
  client_notification_required_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether client notification is required when a risk exceeds this appetite threshold (relevant for data breach, conflict, or professional indemnity risks).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this risk appetite threshold record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the financial exposure limit (e.g., USD, GBP, EUR).']
  effective_from_date date [note: 'Date from which this risk appetite threshold becomes active and applicable to risk assessments.']
  effective_to_date date [note: 'Date on which this risk appetite threshold expires or is superseded. Nullable for currently active thresholds.']
  escalation_trigger_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Risk score threshold at which escalation to senior management or governance body is automatically triggered. Typically lower than maximum tolerable score.']
  financial_exposure_limit decimal(15,2) [note: 'Maximum financial exposure (in firm base currency) the firm is willing to accept for risks in this category before mandatory action is required.']
  insurance_coverage_required_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether professional indemnity or other insurance coverage is required for risks in this category exceeding the threshold.']
  last_review_date date [note: 'Date on which this risk appetite threshold was last reviewed by the governance body.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this risk appetite threshold record was last modified.']
  mandatory_mitigation_required_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether risks exceeding this threshold require mandatory mitigation actions (true) or are subject to discretionary treatment (false).']
  maximum_tolerable_risk_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'The highest risk score the firm is willing to accept before mandatory mitigation or escalation is required. Represents the upper boundary of risk tolerance.']
  minimum_acceptable_risk_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'The lower boundary of the risk tolerance band. Risks scoring below this threshold are considered within normal operational tolerance.']
  next_review_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next review of this risk appetite threshold by the governance body.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional notes, comments, or context regarding this risk appetite threshold, including special conditions or exceptions.']
  organizational_level varchar [note: 'Level of the organization to which this risk appetite threshold applies (firm-wide, practice group, office, or matter-specific).']
  regulator_notification_required_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether regulatory notification (e.g., to ICO, SRA, State Bar) is required when a risk exceeds this appetite threshold.']
  regulatory_framework varchar [note: 'Regulatory or compliance framework that influences this risk appetite threshold (e.g., GDPR, ISO 27001, AML, SOX, SRA Code of Conduct).']
  review_frequency varchar [note: 'Frequency at which this risk appetite threshold must be reviewed and reaffirmed (monthly, quarterly, semi-annually, annually, or ad-hoc).']
  risk_score_scale varchar [note: 'The scoring scale used for risk assessment (e.g., 1-5, 1-10, 1-25, 1-100). Provides context for interpreting threshold scores.']
  risk_subcategory varchar [note: 'Detailed subcategory or classification within the primary risk category (e.g., GDPR breach, ISO 27001 incident, AML screening failure).']
  risk_tolerance_band varchar [note: 'Categorical classification of the risk tolerance level (low, medium, high, critical) corresponding to the threshold score range.']
  threshold_rationale varchar [note: 'Business justification and rationale for setting this specific risk appetite threshold, including strategic considerations and risk-return trade-offs.']
  threshold_reference_code varchar [note: 'Business-readable unique reference code for the risk appetite threshold (e.g., RAT-FIRM-001, RAT-LITG-002).']
  threshold_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the risk appetite threshold (draft, active, under review, superseded, expired).']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version number of this risk appetite threshold record, incremented with each revision or update.']

  Note: 'Reference record defining the firms risk appetite thresholds by risk category and organizational level (firm-wide, practice group, office). Captures maximum tolerable risk score, escalation trigger score, risk tolerance band (low/medium/high/critical), review frequency, approving governance body (Risk Committee, Managing Partner, Board), and effective date range. Used to evaluate whether assessed risks fall within or outside the firms stated risk appetite.'
}

Table risk.mitigation_action {
  mitigation_action_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the risk mitigation action record. Primary key.']
  category_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to risk.risk_category. Business justification: Mitigation actions target specific risk categories. Assuming action_category (STRING) aligns with risk taxonomy and should normalize to risk_category table.']
  practice_group_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the practice group responsible for or affected by this mitigation action (e.g., Corporate, Litigation, IP).']
  primary_attorney_profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the timekeeper or workforce member responsible for executing and completing the mitigation action.']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the client associated with this mitigation action, if the risk is client-specific. Null for enterprise-level risks.']
  register_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent risk in the risk register that this mitigation action addresses.']
  reviewer_attorney_profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the timekeeper or workforce member responsible for reviewing and verifying completion of the mitigation action.']
  risk_control_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to risk.risk_control. Business justification: Mitigation actions implement, test, or remediate specific controls. Business semantics: this action addresses this control. Creates valid chain: risk_mitigation_action → risk_control → risk_register']
  action_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the mitigation or remediation action to be taken, including specific steps, controls, or interventions required.']
  action_owner_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the individual assigned as the action owner, denormalized for reporting convenience.']
  action_priority varchar [note: 'Priority level assigned to the mitigation action based on risk severity and urgency: critical (immediate), high (urgent), medium (standard), or low (routine).']
  action_reference_number varchar [note: 'Business-facing unique reference code for the mitigation action, used in reporting and tracking (e.g., RMA-00012345).']
  action_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the mitigation action: assigned (not started), in_progress (underway), pending_review (awaiting approval), completed (finished), overdue (past due date), or cancelled (no longer required).']
  action_title varchar [note: 'Short descriptive title of the mitigation action for quick identification and reporting.']
  action_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the mitigation action by control type: preventive (avoid risk), detective (identify occurrence), corrective (fix issue), compensating (alternative control), directive (policy/procedure), or recovery (restore after event).']
  actual_cost decimal(15,2) [note: 'Actual financial cost incurred to implement the mitigation action, captured for budget tracking and ROI analysis.']
  actual_effort_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'Actual number of hours spent completing the mitigation action, captured for performance tracking and future estimation.']
  assigned_date date [note: 'Date when the mitigation action was formally assigned to the action owner.']
  completion_date date [note: 'Actual date when the mitigation action was completed and closed. Null if action is not yet completed.']
  completion_evidence varchar [note: 'Description or reference to evidence demonstrating completion of the mitigation action (e.g., document reference, system log, attestation, audit report).']
  completion_status varchar [note: 'Detailed completion state: not_started (no work done), partial (in progress), complete (finished but not verified), verified (reviewed and approved), or rejected (completion not accepted).']
  control_effectiveness_rating varchar [note: 'Post-implementation assessment of how effective the mitigation action was in reducing the risk: effective (fully mitigated), partially_effective (some reduction), ineffective (no impact), or not_tested (not yet evaluated).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this mitigation action record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for estimated and actual cost amounts (e.g., USD, GBP, EUR).']
  due_date date [note: 'Target completion date for the mitigation action, used for tracking and escalation.']
  escalation_date date [note: 'Date when the mitigation action was escalated to senior management or the risk committee. Null if no escalation occurred.']
  escalation_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation of why the mitigation action was escalated (e.g., overdue, insufficient resources, control ineffective, regulatory deadline).']
  escalation_required boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this mitigation action requires escalation to senior management or the risk committee due to delays, resource constraints, or effectiveness concerns.']
  estimated_cost decimal(15,2) [note: 'Estimated financial cost to implement the mitigation action, including internal labor, external counsel, technology, or other resources.']
  estimated_effort_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'Estimated number of hours required to complete the mitigation action, used for resource planning and capacity management.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this mitigation action record was most recently modified, used for audit trail and change tracking.']
  lpp_applies boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether Legal Professional Privilege applies to this mitigation action and its associated documentation, restricting disclosure.']
  pii_involved boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this mitigation action involves processing or handling of Personally Identifiable Information (PII), triggering additional data protection controls.']
  regulatory_body varchar [note: 'Name of the regulatory body or governing authority to which this mitigation action must be reported (e.g., ICO, SRA, State Bar, FCA).']
  regulatory_framework varchar [note: 'Applicable regulatory or compliance framework that this mitigation action addresses (e.g., GDPR, ISO 27001, AML, SOX, SRA Code of Conduct). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: GDPR|ISO_27001|AML|SOX|SRA_Code|CCPA|PCI_DSS|FATF|DPA — promote to reference product]']
  regulatory_reporting_required boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether completion or failure of this mitigation action must be reported to a regulatory body or governing authority.']
  residual_risk_reassessment_date date [note: 'Date when the residual risk was reassessed following completion of this mitigation action. Null if reassessment not yet performed.']
  residual_risk_reassessment_required boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether completion of this action triggers a formal reassessment of the residual risk rating in the risk register.']
  review_comments varchar [note: 'Comments or notes provided by the reviewer regarding the quality, completeness, or effectiveness of the mitigation action.']
  review_date date [note: 'Date when the completed mitigation action was formally reviewed and verified by the reviewer.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of each risk mitigation or remediation action assigned to address an identified risk or control deficiency. Captures action description, action owner, assigned date, due date, priority, completion status, completion date, evidence of completion, and residual risk re-assessment trigger. Provides the action tracking layer that drives risk reduction workflows and management reporting.'
}

Table risk.third_party_risk {
  third_party_risk_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the third-party risk assessment record. Primary key for the third_party_risk product.']
  category_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to risk.risk_category. Business justification: Third-party risks are classified by risk category. Business semantics support this relationship for vendor risk categorization.']
  escalation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to risk.risk_escalation. Business justification: High-risk third parties may trigger escalations for vendor approval or termination decisions. Valid business relationship for vendor risk governance.']
  primary_attorney_profile_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for the individual responsible for managing and monitoring the vendor risk, typically a partner, general counsel, or risk manager.']
  primary_contract_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for the master service agreement or contract governing the vendor relationship.']
  register_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to risk.risk_register. Business justification: Third-party risks should be tracked in the enterprise risk register. Standard risk aggregation pattern for vendor/ALSP risk management.']
  trust_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to trust.trust_account. Business justification: Third-party payment processors and banks handling trust funds require vendor risk assessment. Trust banking relationships link to third-party risk records for due diligence, SOC2 verification, and DPA']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for the external vendor or service provider being assessed. Links to the vendor master record.']
  assessment_frequency varchar [note: 'Frequency at which the vendor risk assessment is conducted, based on the risk tier and criticality of services provided.']
  client_data_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the vendor has access to client data, including matter files, client communications, or client-specific information.']
  contractual_security_obligations varchar [note: 'Summary of security and data protection obligations imposed on the vendor through contractual terms, including encryption, access controls, incident notification, and audit rights.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the third-party risk record was first created in the system.']
  cybersecurity_incident_count varchar [note: 'Number of cybersecurity incidents or data breaches reported by or involving the vendor during the current assessment period.']
  data_access_level varchar [note: 'Level of access the vendor has to firm or client data. Indicates whether vendor handles Personally Identifiable Information (PII), Legal Professional Privilege (LPP) protected data, or confidential information.']
  dpa_execution_date date [note: 'Date when the Data Processing Agreement (DPA) was signed and became effective.']
  dpa_signed_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a Data Processing Agreement (DPA) has been executed with the vendor as required under GDPR Article 28 for data processors.']
  due_diligence_completion_date date [note: 'Date when the initial due diligence assessment was completed for the vendor.']
  due_diligence_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the vendor due diligence process, including Know Your Client (KYC) and Anti-Money Laundering (AML) checks required for third-party onboarding.']
  gdpr_compliant_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the vendor has demonstrated compliance with General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR) requirements for data processing and protection.']
  inherent_risk_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Quantitative risk score representing the inherent risk posed by the vendor before considering mitigating controls. Calculated based on data access, service criticality, and vendor profile.']
  iso_27001_certificate_number varchar [note: 'Certificate number for the vendors ISO 27001 certification, if applicable.']
  iso_27001_certified_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the vendor holds ISO 27001 certification for Information Security Management Systems (ISMS), demonstrating compliance with international security standards.']
  iso_27001_expiry_date date [note: 'Expiration date of the vendors ISO 27001 certification, requiring renewal or re-assessment.']
  last_assessment_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent risk assessment conducted for this vendor.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the third-party risk record was most recently updated or modified.']
  lpp_access_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the vendor has access to data protected by Legal Professional Privilege (LPP), requiring heightened confidentiality and security controls.']
  monitoring_status varchar [note: 'Status of ongoing monitoring activities for the vendor, including continuous security assessments, performance reviews, and compliance checks.']
  next_assessment_due_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next periodic risk assessment or review of the vendor relationship.']
  onboarding_date date [note: 'Date when the vendor was formally onboarded and approved to provide services to the firm.']
  pii_access_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the vendor has access to Personally Identifiable Information (PII) as defined under GDPR and data protection regulations.']
  reputational_risk_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the vendor poses a reputational risk to the firm based on adverse media, regulatory actions, or ethical concerns.']
  reputational_risk_notes varchar [note: 'Detailed notes describing any reputational risk factors identified during vendor assessment, including adverse media findings or regulatory enforcement actions.']
  residual_risk_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Quantitative risk score representing the residual risk after considering contractual controls, certifications, and monitoring activities.']
  risk_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the vendor risk profile, indicating whether the risk is within acceptable thresholds or requires remediation actions.']
  risk_tier varchar [note: 'Overall risk classification assigned to the vendor based on data access level, service criticality, and security posture. Critical tier vendors require enhanced monitoring and controls.']
  sanctions_screening_date date [note: 'Date when the most recent sanctions screening was performed for the vendor.']
  sanctions_screening_status varchar [note: 'Result of sanctions screening checks against international sanctions lists (OFAC, UN, EU) to ensure the vendor is not subject to trade restrictions or financial sanctions.']
  service_category varchar [note: 'Category of service provided by the third party. Includes Alternative Legal Service Provider (ALSP), technology vendors, document management systems, eDiscovery platforms, and other outsourced functions. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: ALSP|technology_vendor|document_management|ediscovery|legal_research|cloud_services|managed_services|consulting|outsourced_legal_services — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  service_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the services provided by the third party, including scope of work and deliverables.']
  soc2_certified_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the vendor has completed a Service Organization Control (SOC) 2 audit, demonstrating controls for security, availability, processing integrity, confidentiality, and privacy.']
  termination_date date [note: 'Date when the vendor relationship was terminated or the contract expired.']
  to_register varchar [note: 'FK to risk.risk_register.risk_register_id — Third-party risks should be registered in the enterprise risk register for consolidated reporting and governance oversight.']
  to_risk_register varchar [note: 'FK to risk.risk_register.risk_register_id — Third-party risks should be registered in the enterprise risk register. This ensures vendor risks are visible in the consolidated risk view.']
  vendor_reference_code varchar [note: 'Internal reference code or identifier assigned to the vendor for tracking and reporting purposes.']
  vendor_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the vendor relationship, indicating whether services are actively being provided or the relationship has been suspended or terminated.']

  Note: 'Third-party and vendor risk record assessing the risk profile of external service providers, ALSPs, technology vendors, and outsourced functions that handle firm or client data. Captures vendor name, service category, data access level (PII, LPP, confidential), due diligence status, ISO 27001 certification status, contractual security obligations, last assessment date, risk tier (critical/high/medium/low), and ongoing monitoring status. Supports SRA outsourcing requirements and GDPR processor obligations.'
}

Table risk.escalation {
  escalation_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the risk escalation record. Primary key.']
  attorney_profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the senior individual who made or approved the final decision.']
  category_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to risk.risk_category. Business justification: Escalations are categorized by risk type. risk_escalation has risk_category (STRING) which should normalize to risk_category reference table for taxonomy consistency.']
  escalation_last_updated_by_timekeeper_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the user who last updated the escalation record.']
  escalation_owner_timekeeper_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the individual or role who initiated the escalation.']
  matter_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the matter associated with the escalated risk, if applicable.']
  prior_escalation_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on escalation (prior_escalation_id)']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the client associated with the escalated risk, if applicable.']
  register_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the underlying risk register entry that triggered this escalation.']
  board_notification_date date [note: 'Date when the Board of Partners was formally notified of the escalation.']
  board_notification_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the escalation requires formal notification to the Board of Partners.']
  closure_date date [note: 'Date when the escalation was formally closed after decision implementation or risk resolution.']
  conditions_imposed varchar [note: 'Any specific conditions, restrictions, or requirements imposed by the governance body as part of the decision.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the escalation record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the estimated financial impact.']
  decision_date date [note: 'Date when the governance body made the formal decision on the escalated risk.']
  decision_maker_name varchar [note: 'Name of the senior individual who made or approved the final decision.']
  decision_outcome varchar [note: 'The risk treatment decision made by the governance body following review.']
  decision_rationale varchar [note: 'Detailed explanation of the reasoning behind the governance decision, including factors considered and risk-benefit analysis.']
  escalating_party_name varchar [note: 'Name of the individual or role who escalated the risk.']
  escalating_party_role varchar [note: 'Job title or functional role of the person who escalated the risk.']
  escalation_date date [note: 'Date when the risk was formally escalated to the governance body.']
  escalation_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the escalation event.']
  escalation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise timestamp when the escalation was initiated.']
  estimated_financial_impact decimal(18,2) [note: 'Estimated financial exposure or potential loss associated with the escalated risk.']
  follow_up_action_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number or identifier linking to follow-up mitigation actions or monitoring activities.']
  follow_up_action_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether follow-up actions or monitoring are required as a result of the decision.']
  insurance_notification_date date [note: 'Date when the professional indemnity insurer was notified of the escalated risk.']
  insurance_notification_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the escalated risk requires notification to the firms professional indemnity insurer.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the escalation record was last modified.']
  lpp_applies_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether Legal Professional Privilege applies to the escalation documentation and decision records.']
  pii_involved_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the escalated risk involves Personally Identifiable Information.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Priority level assigned to the escalation based on urgency and potential impact.']
  receiving_governance_body varchar [note: 'The senior governance body or authority to which the risk was escalated. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: risk_committee|board_of_partners|managing_partner|executive_committee|compliance_committee|audit_committee|practice_group_head|other — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  reference_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique reference number for this escalation event, used for tracking and audit purposes.']
  regulatory_body varchar [note: 'Name of the regulatory authority to which the risk must be reported, if applicable.']
  regulatory_framework varchar [note: 'Applicable regulatory framework or compliance standard relevant to the escalated risk.']
  regulatory_notification_date date [note: 'Date when the regulatory body was notified of the escalated risk.']
  regulatory_reporting_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the escalated risk requires notification to external regulatory bodies.']
  review_due_date date [note: 'Date by which the escalated risk must be reviewed again or follow-up actions completed.']
  risk_severity_level varchar [note: 'Assessed severity level of the risk at the time of escalation.']
  trigger varchar [note: 'The event or condition that triggered the escalation to senior governance. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: threshold_breach|novel_risk_type|regulatory_inquiry|client_complaint|material_loss|reputational_threat|policy_exception|control_failure|other — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  trigger_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative explaining the specific circumstances that triggered the escalation.']

  Note: 'Governance escalation and decision record capturing formal risk escalation events from operational teams to Risk Committee, Managing Partner, or Board. Each record documents the escalated risk or event reference, escalation trigger (threshold breach, novel risk type, regulatory inquiry), escalation date, escalating party, receiving governance body, decision outcome (accept, mitigate, transfer, avoid, defer), conditions imposed, decision rationale, decision date, and follow-up action references. Provides the governance audit trail required by SRA, ISO 31000, and internal risk governance frameworks to demonstrate that material risks received appropriate senior oversight.'
}

Table court.service_of_process {
  service_of_process_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the service of process record. Primary key.']
  form_template_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to knowledge.form_template. Business justification: Service of process requires specific forms (summons, proof of service affidavits, declaration of service). Linking service events to the forms used tracks compliance with service requirements and enab']
  indemnity_claim_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.indemnity_claim. Business justification: Service of process in indemnity claims triggers insurer notification and defense coordination; critical for claims management and regulatory compliance.']
  matter_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the legal matter or case to which this service of process belongs.']
  primary_proof_of_service_legal_document_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the document management system record for the filed proof of service (affidavit, certificate, or return of service).']
  privacy_breach_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.privacy_breach. Business justification: Service of process in privacy litigation triggers DPO notification and supervisory authority reporting; essential for GDPR compliance and breach incident tracking.']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to client.client_profile. Business justification: Service of process events involve clients as parties. Linking enables client notification workflows, litigation timeline tracking, response deadline calculation, and proof-of-service documentation for']
  register_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to risk.risk_register. Business justification: Service failures create jurisdictional defects and statute of limitations issues, leading to malpractice exposure. Risk teams track service compliance for quality control and insurer reporting.']
  attempted_service_count varchar [note: 'Number of attempts made to effect service on the party before successful service or abandonment.']
  central_authority_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number or identifier assigned by the Central Authority when service is effected under the Hague Service Convention.']
  compliance_deadline_date date [note: 'Date by which the served party must comply with the served document (e.g., response deadline for complaint, production deadline for subpoena duces tecum, appearance date for subpoena ad testificandum).']
  created_by_user_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or system account that created this service of process record.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this service of process record was first created in the system.']
  document_type varchar [note: 'Type of legal document being served. Distinguishes between complaints, summonses, subpoenas (ad testificandum for testimony, duces tecum for documents), arbitration notices, and other compulsory instruments. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: complaint|summons|subpoena_ad_testificandum|subpoena_duces_tecum|arbitration_notice|motion|order|notice|other — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  ecf_filing_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number or transaction ID from the Electronic Case Filing (ECF) system if proof of service was filed electronically.']
  esi_ediscovery_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this service of process involves electronically stored information (ESI) or triggers eDiscovery obligations (True/False), particularly relevant for subpoenas duces tecum requesting electronic documents.']
  hague_convention_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether service was effected under the Hague Service Convention for international service of process (True/False).']
  jurisdiction_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the legal jurisdiction governing this service of process (federal, state, county, or international jurisdiction).']
  modified_by_user_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or system account that last modified this service of process record.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this service of process record was last modified or updated.']
  objection_date date [note: 'Date on which an objection or motion to quash service was filed by the served party.']
  objection_filed_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the party served has filed an objection or motion to quash service (True/False).']
  objection_reason varchar [note: 'Textual description of the grounds for objection to service (e.g., insufficient process, insufficient service of process, lack of personal jurisdiction).']
  pacer_case_number varchar [note: 'PACER case number for federal court cases, linking this service of process to the federal court docket.']
  party_served_name varchar [note: 'Full legal name of the individual or entity upon whom service was effected.']
  party_served_role varchar [note: 'Role of the party being served in the legal proceeding (defendant, plaintiff, witness, third-party, agent, registered agent). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: defendant|plaintiff|witness|third_party|agent|registered_agent|other — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  process_server_license_number varchar [note: 'License or registration number of the process server, if applicable under jurisdictional requirements.']
  process_server_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the individual or entity who effected service (process server, sheriff, marshal, attorney, or other authorized person).']
  proof_of_service_filed_date date [note: 'Date on which the proof of service (affidavit or certificate of service) was filed with the court or tribunal.']
  response_due_date date [note: 'Date by which the served party must file a responsive pleading or answer, calculated from the service date per applicable rules.']
  service_address_line1 varchar [note: 'Primary street address line where service was effected.']
  service_address_line2 varchar [note: 'Secondary address line (suite, apartment, floor) where service was effected.']
  service_city varchar [note: 'City where service was effected.']
  service_cost_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total cost incurred for effecting service of process, including process server fees, mailing costs, publication costs, or international service fees.']
  service_cost_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO currency code for the service cost amount (e.g., USD, GBP, EUR).']
  service_country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code where service was effected (e.g., USA, GBR, CAN).']
  service_date date [note: 'Date on which service of process was completed and the document was delivered to or left with the party served.']
  service_docket varchar [note: 'FK to court.docket.docket_id — Service of process is performed in connection with a docket. Required for proof of service filing and compliance tracking.']
  service_method varchar [note: 'Method by which service was effected: personal service, substituted service, service by publication, electronic service, Hague Convention service for international parties, certified or registered mail, or other jurisdictionally-approved method. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: personal|substituted|publication|electronic|hague_convention|certified_mail|registered_mail|other — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  service_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes documenting circumstances of service, challenges encountered, special instructions followed, or other relevant details provided by the process server or attorney.']
  service_postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code where service was effected.']
  service_reference_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier or tracking number assigned to this service of process event for external reference and tracking.']
  service_state_province varchar [note: 'State or province where service was effected.']
  service_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the service of process attempt: pending (not yet attempted), completed (successfully served), failed (attempt unsuccessful), returned unserved (unable to locate or serve party), or quashed (invalidated by court).']
  service_time timestamp [note: 'Precise timestamp when service was completed, including time of day, for jurisdictions requiring exact time documentation.']
  service_to_docket varchar [note: 'FK to court.docket.docket_id — Service of process occurs in connection with a litigated matter/docket. Required to track which case the service relates to.']
  service_validity_status varchar [note: 'Current validity status of the service of process: valid (accepted by court), invalid (rejected or defective), pending validation, quashed (set aside by court order), or objected (party has filed objection).']

  Note: 'Tracks formal service of legal process on parties including complaints, summonses, subpoenas (ad testificandum and duces tecum), arbitration notices, and other compulsory instruments. Records service method (personal, substituted, publication, electronic, Hague Convention), service date, process server identity, party served, document type and reference, proof of service filing date, service validity status, objection status, compliance deadline, and ESI/eDiscovery implications flag. Supports litigation workflow, ensures compliance with jurisdictional service requirements, and links to document management for collection when subpoenas duces tecum are involved.'
}

Table court.adr_proceeding {
  adr_proceeding_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the ADR proceeding. Primary key for the ADR proceeding record.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to trust.trust_account. Business justification: ADR proceedings frequently involve trust or escrow arrangements for settlement funds, arbitration deposits, or mediation settlement amounts. Attorneys must track which trust account holds ADR-related ']
  indemnity_claim_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.indemnity_claim. Business justification: Mediation/arbitration of indemnity claims is standard practice for settlement; essential for claims management, insurer coordination, and regulatory compliance.']
  matter_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent matter under which this ADR proceeding is conducted. Links the ADR proceeding to the broader legal matter for tracking and billing purposes.']
  practice_note_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to knowledge.practice_note. Business justification: ADR forums have specific practice notes (arbitration procedures, mediation protocols, institutional rules, discovery in arbitration). Linking proceeding to practice notes ensures procedural compliance']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to client.client_profile. Business justification: ADR proceedings are client matters. Direct link supports alternative dispute resolution tracking, client preference analysis for settlement vs. litigation, cost-benefit reporting, and ADR outcome metr']
  regulatory_breach_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_breach. Business justification: ADR/arbitration of regulatory disputes is common in commercial regulatory matters; essential for breach resolution tracking and regulatory reporting.']
  administering_institution varchar [note: 'The arbitration or ADR institution administering the proceeding (e.g., ICC, LCIA, AAA, JAMS, SIAC, HKIAC). For ad hoc proceedings, this field may be null or indicate Ad Hoc.']
  adr_administering_body varchar [note: 'FK to court.tribunal.tribunal_id — ADR proceedings are administered by an institution (ICC, LCIA, AAA) which is stored as a tribunal record. Required for institutional rule application.']
  adr_to_administering_body varchar [note: 'FK to court.tribunal.tribunal_id — ADR proceedings are administered by a specific body (ICC, LCIA, AAA). The tribunal product covers arbitral bodies. Links ADR to its forum.']
  adr_to_seat_jurisdiction varchar [note: 'FK to court.court_jurisdiction.court_jurisdiction_id — ADR proceedings have a seat of arbitration which determines the governing jurisdiction for procedural matters and enforcement.']
  adr_type varchar [note: 'The type of ADR mechanism being used to resolve the dispute. Arbitration is binding; mediation is facilitative; expert determination involves a neutral expert; adjudication is common in construction disputes; conciliation is similar to mediation; dispute boards provide ongoing dispute resolution during long-term projects.']
  award_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The monetary amount awarded by the tribunal in favor of the prevailing party. May be positive (in favor of claimant) or negative (in favor of respondent if counterclaim succeeds). Excludes costs and interest unless specified. Confidential financial information.']
  award_currency varchar [note: 'The currency in which the award amount is denominated, using ISO 4217 three-letter currency code. Null if no award has been issued.']
  award_date date [note: 'The date on which the final award was issued by the arbitral tribunal. For mediation, the date of the settlement agreement. Null if no award or settlement has been reached.']
  case_reference_number varchar [note: 'External case or proceeding reference number assigned by the administering institution or by the parties. This is the business identifier used in correspondence and filings.']
  claim_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The monetary value of the claim as stated by the claimant in the notice of arbitration or statement of claim. Excludes interest, costs, and fees unless explicitly included in the claim. Confidential financial information.']
  claim_currency varchar [note: 'The currency in which the claim amount is denominated, using ISO 4217 three-letter currency code (e.g., USD, GBP, EUR, CHF).']
  claimant_party_name varchar [note: 'The name of the claimant party (or parties) initiating the ADR proceeding. May be a single entity or multiple co-claimants. Confidential business information.']
  commencement_date date [note: 'The date on which the ADR proceeding was formally commenced, typically the date the notice of arbitration or request for mediation was filed with the administering institution or served on the respondent.']
  confidentiality_level varchar [note: 'The level of confidentiality applicable to the ADR proceeding. Public: no confidentiality restrictions. Confidential: standard confidentiality per institutional rules. Highly confidential: enhanced confidentiality by agreement. Sealed: court-ordered sealing or equivalent. Most arbitrations are confidential by default.']
  costs_awarded_to_claimant decimal(18,2) [note: 'The amount of legal costs and expenses awarded to the claimant by the tribunal. Null if no costs award has been made or if costs were awarded to respondent. Confidential financial information.']
  costs_awarded_to_respondent decimal(18,2) [note: 'The amount of legal costs and expenses awarded to the respondent by the tribunal. Null if no costs award has been made or if costs were awarded to claimant. Confidential financial information.']
  counterclaim_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The monetary value of any counterclaim filed by the respondent. Null if no counterclaim has been filed. Confidential financial information.']
  counterclaim_currency varchar [note: 'The currency in which the counterclaim amount is denominated, using ISO 4217 three-letter currency code. Null if no counterclaim exists.']
  dispute_subject_matter varchar [note: 'A brief description of the subject matter of the dispute (e.g., breach of contract, intellectual property infringement, construction delay, shareholder dispute, M&A dispute). Confidential business information.']
  emergency_arbitrator_appointed_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether an emergency arbitrator was appointed under the institutional rules to grant urgent interim relief before the tribunal is constituted. True if emergency arbitrator appointed; False otherwise.']
  enforcement_jurisdiction varchar [note: 'The country or jurisdiction in which enforcement of the award is being sought or has been obtained. May be multiple jurisdictions if enforcement is pursued in multiple countries. Null if no enforcement action has been initiated.']
  enforcement_status varchar [note: 'The status of enforcement of the arbitral award. Not applicable: no award issued or settlement reached voluntarily. Pending: award issued, enforcement not yet initiated. In progress: enforcement proceedings underway. Enforced: award successfully enforced. Challenged: enforcement challenged by losing party.
